
### Decoded Body

```text
To translate the contents file

1	In Help Workshop, click the File menu, and then make sure Translation is checked.

2	Open the contents (.cnt) file.

3	Select a heading or topic you want to translate, and then click Edit.

Note

·	When the translation command is checked, you can modify only the visible text that appears on the Contents tab. You cannot modify topic IDs, Help filenames, or window names.

{button ,AL(`BAS_CNT_OVERVIEW;BAS_TRANSLATING')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_CNT_OVERVIEW;BAS_TRANSLATING')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf

\par 2\tab Open the contents (.cnt) file.
\par 3\tab Select a heading or topic you want to translate, and then click Edit.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Note
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \plain\f2\fs16 \'b7\tab \f1 When the translation command is checked, you can modify only the visible text that appears on the Contents tab. You cannot modify topic IDs, Help filenames, or window names.
```

### Decoded Body

```text
To compile a Help file

1	If the project file is already open in Help Workshop, save the file.

2	Click the Compile button.22T9EAQ

3	In the Project File box, type the name and location of the project (.hpj) file you want to compile, and then click Compile.

Tips

·	You do not have to open a project file to compile it.

·	If a project file is open, you can click the Save And Compile button to compile the file.

·	For information about the other settings in this dialog box, click {bmc bm0.bmp} at the top of the dialog box, and then click the setting.

{button ,AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES;EXEC_HCW')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES;EXEC_HCW')
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 2\tab Click the \ul Compile button.\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 22T9EAQ}
\par 3\tab In the Project File box, type the name and location of the project (.hpj) file you want to compile, and then click Compile.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Tips
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \plain\f2\fs16 \'b7\f1 \tab You do not have to open a project file to compile it.

\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab For information about the other settings in this dialog box, click \{bmc bm0.bmp\} at the top of the dialog box, and then click the setting.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```


### Decoded Body

```text
To compile more than one Help file at a time

1	Using a text editor, create an ASCII text file that lists the full path of each of your project files on a separate line. Specify the project files in the order in which you want Help Workshop to compile them.

2	Save the file using the filename extension .hmk.

3	Open Help Workshop, and then click Compile button.22T9EAQ

4	In the Project File box, type the name of your .hmk file, and then click Compile.

{button ,AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES;EXEC_HCW')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES;EXEC_HCW')
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 2\tab Save the file using the filename extension .hmk.
\par 3\tab Open Help Workshop, and then click \ul Compile button.\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 22T9EAQ}
\par 4\tab In the Project File box, type the name of your .hmk file, and then click Compile.
```

### Decoded Body

```text
To test a contents file

1	In Help Workshop, click the Test menu, and then click Contents File.

2	Type the name of the contents (.cnt) file you want to test.

	Help Workshop checks your contents file to make sure that the syntax is correct. It then jumps to every topic specified in the contents file and displays a message if one of the jumps does not work.

Note

·	If your contents file includes other contents files, make sure they are available to be tested.

{button ,AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES;PRO_HCW_CLOSE_ALL_HELP')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES;PRO_HCW_CLOSE_ALL_HELP')
```

```rtf
\par \tab Help Workshop checks your contents file to make sure that the syntax is correct. It then jumps to every topic specified in the contents file and displays a message if one of the jumps does not work.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Note
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \plain\f2\fs16 \'b7\f1 \tab If your contents file includes other contents files, make sure they are available to be tested.
```

### Decoded Body

```text
To generate a report on a Help file

1	In Help Workshop, click the File menu, and then click Report.

2	In the Help Filename box, type the path and name of the Help (.hlp) file.

3	In the Output Filename box, type the path and name of the file in which you want to save the report.

4	In the Display area, select the type of report you want, and then click Report.

Note

·	For help on an item, click {bmc bm0.bmp} at the top of the dialog box, and then click the item.

{button ,AL(`BAS_TESTING_HELP_FILES')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_TESTING_HELP_FILES')
```

```rtf
\par 3\tab In the Output Filename box, type the path and name of the file in which you want to save the report.
```

## 131. Button macros

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE0027:005e`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE0027:005e
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Button macros`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Button macros
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `MACRO_REF_BUTTON`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    MACRO_REF_BUTTON
    ```
- Marker `>`
  - Decoded: `main`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    main
    ```
- Marker `A`
  - Decoded: `MACRO_REF_BUTTON`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    MACRO_REF_BUTTON
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `button macros;buttons,;buttons, macros for;macros,;macros, button`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    button macros;buttons,;buttons, macros for;macros,;macros, button
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Button macros

You can use the following macros to access standard Help buttons, create new buttons, or modify button functionality. Macros that are new in WinHelp version 4.0 are noted with an asterisk.

BackBack>main	Displays the topic that was previously displayed in the current window.

BrowseButtonsBrowseButtons>main	Adds browse buttons to the Help button bar.

ChangeButtonBindingChangeButtonBinding>main	Changes the macro assigned to a button on the button bar.

ChangeEnable*ChangeEnable>main	Assigns a macro to a button on the button bar and enables that button.

ContentsContents>main	Displays the Contents tab or default topic of the current Help file.

CreateButtonCreateButton>main	Creates a new button and adds it to the button bar.

DestroyButtonDestroyButton>main	Removes a button from the button bar.

DisableButtonDisableButton>main	Disables a button on the button bar.

EnableButtonEnableButton>main	Enables a button on the button bar.

Find*Find>main	Displays the Find tab of the Help Topics dialog box.

Finder*Finder>main	Displays the Help Topics dialog boxKMK38I in its last state.

Menu*Menu>main	Displays the context menu,JNVVI2 which is typically accessed by using the right mouse button.

NextNext>main	Displays the next topic in a browse sequence.

PrevPrev>main	Displays the previous topic in a browse sequence.

SearchSearch>main	Displays the Index tab of the Help Topics dialog box.

{button ,AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')} Related Topics%!AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Button macros
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 You can use the following macros to access standard Help buttons, create new buttons, or modify button functionality. Macros that are new in WinHelp version 4.0 are noted with an asterisk.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2065\ri125\fi-1935\tx2065 \uldb Back\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Back>main}\tab Displays the topic that was previously displayed in the current window.
\par \uldb BrowseButtons\plain\f1\fs16 {\v BrowseButtons>main}\tab Adds browse buttons to the Help button bar.
\par \uldb ChangeButtonBinding\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ChangeButtonBinding>main}\tab Changes the macro assigned to a button on the button bar.
\par \uldb ChangeEnable*\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ChangeEnable>main}\tab Assigns a macro to a button on the button bar and enables that button.
\par \uldb Contents\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Contents>main}\tab Displays the Contents tab or default topic of the current Help file.
\par \uldb CreateButton\plain\f1\fs16 {\v CreateButton>main}\tab Creates a new button and adds it to the button bar.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2065\ri125\fi-1935\tx2065 \uldb DestroyButton\plain\f1\fs16 {\v DestroyButton>main}\tab Removes a button from the button bar.
\par \uldb DisableButton\plain\f1\fs16 {\v DisableButton>main}\tab Disables a button on the button bar.
\par \uldb EnableButton\plain\f1\fs16 {\v EnableButton>main}\tab Enables a button on the button bar.
\par \uldb Find*\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Find>main}\tab Displays the Find tab of the Help Topics dialog box.
\par \uldb Finder*\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Finder>main}\tab Displays the \ul Help Topics dialog box\plain\f1\fs16 {\v KMK38I} in its last state.
\par \uldb Menu*\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Menu>main}\tab Displays the \ul context menu,\plain\f1\fs16 {\v JNVVI2} which is typically accessed by using the right mouse button.
\par \uldb Next\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Next>main}\tab Displays the next topic in a browse sequence.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2065\ri125\fi-1935\tx2065 \uldb Prev\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Prev>main}\tab Displays the previous topic in a browse sequence.
\par \uldb Search\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Search>main}\tab Displays the Index tab of the Help Topics dialog box.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')}
\par
```

- Marker `K`

  - Raw:
    ```rtf

    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Menu macros

You can use the following macros to create or modify menus and menu items.

AppendItemAppendItem>main	Appends a menu item to the end of a menu.

ChangeItemBindingChangeItemBinding>main	Changes the macro assigned to a menu item.

CheckItemCheckItem>main	Displays a check mark next to a menu item.

DeleteItemDeleteItem>main	Removes a menu item from a menu.

DisableItemDisableItem>main	Disables a menu item.

EnableItemEnableItem>main	Enables a disabled menu item.

ExtAbleItemExtAbleItem>main	Enables or disables a menu item.

ExtInsertItemExtInsertItem>main	Inserts a menu item at a given position on a menu, in a given state.

ExtInsertMenuExtInsertMenu>main	Inserts a submenu in a previously defined menu.

InsertItemInsertItem>main	Inserts a menu item at a given position on a menu.

InsertMenuInsertMenu>main	Adds a new menu to the Help menu bar.

ResetMenuResetMenu>main	Resets the Help menu bar and menus to their default states.

UncheckItemUncheckItem>main	Removes a check mark from a menu item.

{button ,AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')} Related Topics%!AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Menu macros
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 You can use the following macros to create or modify menus and menu items.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1985\ri125\fi-1855\tx1975 \uldb AppendItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v AppendItem>main}\tab Appends a menu item to the end of a menu.
\par \uldb ChangeItemBinding\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ChangeItemBinding>main}\tab Changes the macro assigned to a menu item.
\par \uldb CheckItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v CheckItem>main}\tab Displays a check mark next to a menu item. 
\par \uldb DeleteItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v DeleteItem>main}\tab Removes a menu item from a menu.
\par \uldb DisableItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v DisableItem>main}\tab Disables a menu item.
\par \uldb EnableItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v EnableItem>main}\tab Enables a disabled menu item.
\par \uldb ExtAbleItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ExtAbleItem>main}\tab Enables or disables a menu item.
\par \uldb ExtInsertItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ExtInsertItem>main}\tab Inserts a menu item at a given position on a menu, in a given state.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1985\ri125\fi-1855\tx1975 \uldb ExtInsertMenu\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ExtInsertMenu>main}\tab Inserts a submenu in a previously defined menu.
\par \uldb InsertItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v InsertItem>main}\tab Inserts a menu item at a given position on a menu.
\par \uldb InsertMenu\plain\f1\fs16 {\v InsertMenu>main}\tab Adds a new menu to the Help menu bar.
\par \uldb ResetMenu\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ResetMenu>main}\tab Resets the Help menu bar and menus to their default states.
\par \uldb UncheckItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v UncheckItem>main}\tab Removes a check mark from a menu item.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```

- Marker `K`


### Decoded Body

```text
Linking macros

You can use the following macros to create hypertext links to specific Help topics. Macros that are new in WinHelp version 4.0 are noted with an asterisk.

ALink*ALink>main	Jumps to the topics that contain the specified A-keyword.

JumpContentsJumpContents>main	Jumps to the contents topic of the specified Help file.

JumpContextJumpContext>main	Jumps to the topic specified by a context number.2O07FH3

JumpHashJumpHash>main	Jumps to the topic specified by a topic hash number.OYZM9

JumpHelpOnJumpHelpOn>main	Jumps to the contents topic of the How To Use Help file.

JumpIdJumpId>main	Jumps to the topic specified by a topic ID.

JumpKeywordJumpKeyword>main	Jumps to the topic containing a specified K-keyword.

KLink*KLink>main	Jumps to the topics that contain the specified K-keywords.

PopupContextPopupContext>main	Displays in a pop-up window the topic specified by a context number.

PopupHashPopupHash>main	Displays in a pop-up window the topic specified by a topic hash number.

PopupIdPopupId>main	Displays in a pop-up window the topic specified by a topic ID.

UpdateWindow*UpdateWindow>main	Jumps to the specified topic in the specified window, and then returns the focus to the window that called the macro.

{button ,AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')} Related Topics%!AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Linking macros
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 You can use the following macros to create hypertext links to specific Help topics. Macros that are new in WinHelp version 4.0 are noted with an asterisk.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1985\ri125\fi-1855\tx1975 \uldb ALink*\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ALink>main}\tab Jumps to the topics that contain the specified A-keyword.
\par \uldb JumpContents\plain\f1\fs16 {\v JumpContents>main}\tab Jumps to the contents topic of the specified Help file.
\par \uldb JumpContext\plain\f1\fs16 {\v JumpContext>main}\tab Jumps to the topic specified by a \ul context number.\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2O07FH3}
\par \uldb JumpHash\plain\f1\fs16 {\v JumpHash>main}\tab Jumps to the topic specified by a \ul topic hash number.\plain\f1\fs16 {\v OYZM9}
\par \uldb JumpHelpOn\plain\f1\fs16 {\v JumpHelpOn>main}\tab Jumps to the contents topic of the How To Use Help file.
\par \uldb JumpId\plain\f1\fs16 {\v JumpId>main}\tab Jumps to the topic specified by a topic ID.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1985\ri125\fi-1855\tx1975 \uldb JumpKeyword\plain\f1\fs16 {\v JumpKeyword>main}\tab Jumps to the topic containing a specified K-keyword.
\par \uldb KLink*\plain\f1\fs16 {\v KLink>main}\tab Jumps to the topics that contain the specified K-keywords.
\par \uldb PopupContext\plain\f1\fs16 {\v PopupContext>main}\tab Displays in a pop-up window the topic specified by a context number.
\par \uldb PopupHash\plain\f1\fs16 {\v PopupHash>main}\tab Displays in a pop-up window the topic specified by a topic hash number.
\par \uldb PopupId\plain\f1\fs16 {\v PopupId>main}\tab Displays in a pop-up window the topic specified by a topic ID.
\par \uldb UpdateWindow*\plain\f1\fs16 {\v UpdateWindow>main}\tab Jumps to the specified topic in the specified window, and then returns the focus to the window that called the macro.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295

\par
```


### Decoded Body

```text
Window macros

You can use the following macros to control or modify the behavior of various Help windows. Macros that are new in WinHelp version 4.0 are noted with an asterisk.

CloseSecondarys*CloseSecondarys>main	Closes all Help windows except the current secondary window.

CloseWindowCloseWindow>main	Closes the main or secondary Help window.

FocusWindowFocusWindow>main	Changes the focus to a specific Help window.

HelpOnTopHelpOnTop>main	Places all Help windows on top of other windows.

PositionWindowPositionWindow>main	Sets the size and position of a Help window.

SetPopupColor*SetPopupColor>main	Sets the background color of pop-up windows.

{button ,AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')} Related Topics%!AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Window macros
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 You can use the following macros to control or modify the behavior of various Help windows. Macros that are new in WinHelp version 4.0 are noted with an asterisk.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1985\ri125\fi-1855\tx1975 \uldb CloseSecondarys*\plain\f1\fs16 {\v CloseSecondarys>main}\tab Closes all Help windows except the current secondary window.
\par \uldb CloseWindow\plain\f1\fs16 {\v CloseWindow>main}\tab Closes the main or secondary Help window.
\par \uldb FocusWindow\plain\f1\fs16 {\v FocusWindow>main}\tab Changes the focus to a specific Help window.
\par \uldb HelpOnTop\plain\f1\fs16 {\v HelpOnTop>main}\tab Places all Help windows on top of other windows.
\par \uldb PositionWindow\plain\f1\fs16 {\v PositionWindow>main}\tab Sets the size and position of a Help window.
\par \uldb SetPopupColor*\plain\f1\fs16 {\v SetPopupColor>main}\tab Sets the background color of pop-up windows.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295

\par
```

```text
Keyboard macros

You can use the following macros to add keyboard access to a Help macro.

AddAcceleratorAddAccelerator>main	Assigns an accelerator keyABYXEJ to a Help macro.

RemoveAcceleratorRemoveAccelerator>main	Removes an accelerator key from a Help macro.

{button ,AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')} Related Topics%!AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')
```

### Decoded Body

```text
Program macros

You can use the following macros to access programs. These macros are new in WinHelp version 4.0.

ControlPanelControlPanel>main	Opens a specific tab in a Control Panel dialog box.

ExecFileExecFile>main	Runs a program or opens a file and runs the program associated with that file.

FileExistFileExist>main	Checks to see whether the specified file exists on a user's computer.

ShellExecuteShellExecute>main	Opens, prints or runs a file or program.

ShortCutShortCut>main	Runs or activates a program and sends it a WM_COMMAND message.

{button ,AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR;ALink;KLink')} Related Topics%!AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR;ALink;KLink')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Program macros
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 You can use the following macros to access programs. These macros are new in WinHelp version 4.0.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1985\ri125\fi-1855\tx1975 \uldb ControlPanel\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ControlPanel>main}\tab Opens a specific tab in a Control Panel dialog box.
\par \uldb ExecFile\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ExecFile>main}\tab Runs a program or opens a file and runs the program associated with that file.
\par \uldb FileExist\plain\f1\fs16 {\v FileExist>main}\tab Checks to see whether the specified file exists on a user's computer.\uldb 
\par ShellExecute\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ShellExecute>main}\tab Opens, prints or runs a file or program.\uldb 
\par ShortCut\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ShortCut>main}\tab Runs or activates a program and sends it a WM_COMMAND message.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```

    ```rtf
    macros,;macros, text-marker;text markers, macros for
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Text-marker macros

You can use the following macros to create and manipulate text markers. Macros that are new in WinHelp version 4.0 are noted with an asterisk.

DeleteMarkDeleteMark>main	Removes a marker added by the SaveMark macro.

GotoMarkGoToMark>main	Jumps to a marker set by the SaveMark macro.

IfThenIfThen>main	Runs a Help macro if a given marker exists.

IfThenElseIfThenElse>main	Runs one of two macros if a given marker exists.

IsMarkIsMark>main	Tests whether a marker set by the SaveMark macro exists.

IsNotMark*IsNotMark>main	Tests whether a marker set by the SaveMark macro does not exist.

NotNot>main	Reverses the result returned by the IsMark macro.

SaveMarkSaveMark>main	Saves a marker for the current topic, window and Help file.

{button ,AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')} Related Topics%!AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Text-marker macros
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 You can use the following macros to create and manipulate text markers. Macros that are new in WinHelp version 4.0 are noted with an asterisk.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1985\ri125\fi-1855\tx1975 \uldb DeleteMark\plain\f1\fs16 {\v DeleteMark>main}\tab Removes a marker added by the SaveMark macro.
\par \uldb GotoMark\plain\f1\fs16 {\v GoToMark>main}\tab Jumps to a marker set by the SaveMark macro.
\par \uldb IfThen\plain\f1\fs16 {\v IfThen>main}\tab Runs a Help macro if a given marker exists.
\par \uldb IfThenElse\plain\f1\fs16 {\v IfThenElse>main}\tab Runs one of two macros if a given marker exists.
\par \uldb IsMark\plain\f1\fs16 {\v IsMark>main}\tab Tests whether a marker set by the SaveMark macro exists.
\par \uldb IsNotMark*\plain\f1\fs16 {\v IsNotMark>main}\tab Tests whether a marker set by the SaveMark macro does not exist.
\par \uldb Not\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Not>main}\tab Reverses the result returned by the IsMark macro.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1985\ri125\fi-1855\tx1975 \uldb SaveMark\plain\f1\fs16 {\v SaveMark>main}\tab Saves a marker for the current topic, window and Help file.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`MACRO_REF_OVR')}
\par
```

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`

    ```rtf
    AddAccelerator
    ```
- Marker `>`
  - Decoded: `main`
  - Raw:

    ```rtf
    main
    ```
- Marker `A`
  - Decoded: `accelerators;AddAccelerator`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    accelerators;AddAccelerator
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `AA macro;accelerator keys;AddAccelerator macro;macros,;macros, AddAccelerator`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    AA macro;accelerator keys;AddAccelerator macro;macros,;macros, AddAccelerator
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
AddAccelerator macro

AddAccelerator(key, shift-state, macro)

Assigns a Help macro to an accelerator keyABYXEJ (or key combination). WinHelp runs the macro whenever a user presses the accelerator key.

Parameter	Description

key	Specifies the Windows virtual-key value. This parameter can be a numeric digit, a quoted character (as in `A'), or one of the following strings:

VK_LBUTTONVK_NUMPAD2
VK_RBUTTONVK_NUMPAD3
VK_CANCELVK_NUMPAD4
VK_MBUTTONVK_NUMPAD5
VK_BACKVK_NUMPAD6
VK_TABVK_NUMPAD7
VK_CLEARVK_NUMPAD8
VK_RETURNVK_NUMPAD9
VK_SHIFTVK_MULTIPLY
VK_CONTROLVK_ADD
VK_MENUVK_SEPARATOR
VK_PAUSEVK_SUBTRACT
VK_CAPITALVK_DECIMAL
VK_KANAVK_DIVIDE
VK_KANJIVK_F1
VK_HANGEULVK_F3
VK_JUNJAVK_F5
VK_HANJAVK_F6
VK_ESCAPEVK_F7
VK_SPACEVK_F8
VK_PRIORVK_F9
VK_NEXTVK_F10
VK_ENDVK_F11
VK_HOMEVK_F12
VK_LEFTVK_F13
VK_UPVK_F14
VK_RIGHTVK_F15
VK_DOWNVK_F16
VK_SELECTVK_F17
VK_PRINTVK_F18
VK_EXECUTEVK_F19
VK_SNAPSHOTVK_F20
VK_INSERTVK_F21
VK_DELETEVK_F22
VK_HELPVK_F23
VK_SCROLLVK_F24
VK_NUMPAD0VK_NUMLOCK
VK_NUMPAD1
shift-state	Specifies the combination of ALT, SHIFT, and CTRL keys to be used with the accelerator. This parameter can be one of the following values:

NONE (0)

shift (1)

ctrl (2)

shift+ctrl (3)

alt (4)

alt+shift (5)

alt+ctrl (6)

shift+alt+ctrl (7)

macro	Specifies the Help macro or macro string to run when the user presses the accelerator key(s). Multiple macros in a string must be separated by colons or semicolons.

Comment

Help Workshop automatically converts the AddAccelerator macro to AA.

Example

The following macro runs the Notepad program when a user presses shift+ctrl+f9:

	AddAccelerator(VK_F9, SHIFT+CTRL, ExecFile(notepad))

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_KEYBOARD;removeaccelerator;macro_all")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_KEYBOARD;removeaccelerator;macro_all")
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Assigns a Help macro to an \ul accelerator key\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ABYXEJ} (or key combination). WinHelp runs the macro whenever a user presses the accelerator key.
```

## 140. ALink macro

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`

- Marker `K`

### Decoded Body

```text
ALink macro

ALink(keyword[; keyword] [, type[, topic-ID [, window-name]]])

Searches for keywords specified by A-footnotes.

Parameter	Description

keyword	Specifies one or more keywords to search for. Multiple keywords must be separated by a semicolon. If any keyword contains a comma, the entire keyword string must be enclosed in quotation marks.

type	Specifies the action to perform if one or more keywords are found. If this parameter is not specified or is zero, the default action is always to display the Topics Found dialog box containing the topic title. This parameter may specify one or more of the following values, separated by spaces.

Value	Meaning

JUMP (1)	Specifies that if only one topic matches any of the keywords, WinHelp should jump directly to that topic.

TITLE (2)	Specifies that if a keyword is found in more than one Help file, WinHelp should display the title of the Help file (as specified in the contents [.cnt] file) beside the topic title in the Topics Found dialog box.

TEST (4)	Specifies that the macro should return a value indicating whether or not there is at least one match. The TestALink macro is converted by Help Workshop into an ALink macro with this parameter.

topic-ID	Specifies the topic to display in a pop-up window if no matches are found. If this parameter is not specified, WinHelp displays a message box with the text “No additional information is available”. To specify a topic in a different Help file, the topic ID should end with an ‘@’ character and the name of the Help file.

window-name	Specifies the window type in which to display the topic. If this parameter is not specified, the window type that is specified for a topic (if one is defined) is used, or the default or current window is used. If this macro results in an interfile jump, the window type must be defined in the project file for the Help file that is being jumped to.

Comments

The ALink macro searches for A-keyword matches in the current Help file. If the Help file is associated with a contents file, WinHelp searches all Help files specified in the contents file (by the :Index and :Link commands) for matching A-keywords.

The ALink macro is identical to the KLink macro except that it searches for A-keywords instead of K-keywords.

Help Workshop automatically converts the ALink macro to AL, which is the only form of the macro that WinHelp can use.

The ALink macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

Examples

The following example generates a list of topics that contain the “network printing” or “local printing” keywords in A-footnotes:

	ALink(network printing;local printing)

The following example generates a list of topics that contain the keyword “how_to” in A-footnotes and displays those topics in the Steps window:

	ALink(how_to, , , Steps)

The following example returns a value of “1” if at least one topic with the keyword “saving” is found:

	ALink(saving, TEST)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;KLink;macro_all")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;KLink;macro_all")
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Searches for keywords specified by A-footnotes.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1445\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16

\par type\tab Specifies the action to perform if one or more keywords are found. If this parameter is not specified or is zero, the default action is always to display the Topics Found dialog box containing the topic title. This parameter may specify one or more of the following values, separated by spaces.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li2875\ri125\fi-1295\brdrb\brdrs\tx2875\tx3145 \b Value\tab Meaning
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2875\ri125\fi-1295\tx2875\tx3145 \plain\f1\fs16 JUMP (1)\b \tab \plain\f1\fs16 Specifies that if only one topic matches any of the keywords, WinHelp should jump directly to that topic.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2875\ri125\fi-1295\tx1575\tx2875 TITLE (2)\tab Specifies that if a keyword is found in more than one Help file, WinHelp should display the title of the Help file (as specified in the contents [.cnt] file) beside the topic title in the Topics Found dialog box.

If this parameter is not specified, WinHelp displays a message box with the text \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 No additional information is available\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1 . To specify a topic in a different Help file, the topic ID should end with an \plain\f0\fs16 \'91\f1 @\plain\f0\fs16 \'92\f1  character and the name of the Help file.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1445\tx1575\tx2965 window-name\tab Specifies the window type in which to display the topic. If this parameter is not specified, the window type that is specified for a topic (if one is defined) is used, or the default or current window is used. If this macro results in an interfile jump, the window type must be defined in the project file for the Help file that is being jumped to.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575\tx2965 \b Comments

If the Help file is associated with a contents file, WinHelp searches all Help files specified in the contents file (by the :Index and :Link commands) for matching A-keywords.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575\tx2965 The ALink macro is identical to the KLink macro except that it searches for A-keywords instead of K-keywords.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575\tx2965 Help Workshop automatically converts the ALink macro to AL, which is the only form of the macro that WinHelp can use.
```

- Marker `K`

### Decoded Body

```text
AppendItem macro

AppendItem(menu-ID, item-ID, item-name, macro)

Appends a menu item to the end of an existing menu.

Parameter	Description

menu-ID	Specifies the name used in the InsertMenu macro that creates the menu, or the name of a standard WinHelp menu. The new item is appended to this menu.

item-ID	Specifies the name that WinHelp uses internally to identify the menu item. This name is used by the DisableItem, DeleteItem, EnableItem, and ExtAbleItem macros.

item-name	Specifies the name that WinHelp displays on the menu for the item. Place an ampersand (&) before the character used for the macro's accelerator key.ABYXEJ

macro	Specifies one or more macros that WinHelp should run when a user chooses the menu item. Separate multiple macros with colons or semicolons (use only colons if multiple macros are specified in a project file).

Comments

WinHelp ignores this macro if it is run in a secondary window.

You can append as many as 20 items to WinHelp's context menu (mnu_floating).

If the keyboard accelerator conflicts with other menu access keys, WinHelp ignores the macro.

Help Workshop automatically converts the AppendItem macro to AI.

Example

The following macro appends a menu item labeled "Tools." Choosing the menu item causes a jump to a topic with the topic ID "tpc1" in the Tls.hlp file:

	AppendItem(IDM_BKS, IDM_TLS, &Tools, JumpId(tls.hlp, tpc1))

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;ResetMenu;ChangeItemBinding;DeleteItem;DisableItem;ExtAbleItem;ExtInsertItem;InsertItem;InsertMenu;macro_all;REF_STD_MENU;FloatingMenu")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;ResetMenu;ChangeItemBinding;DeleteItem;DisableItem;ExtAbleItem;ExtInsertItem;InsertItem;InsertMenu;macro_all;REF_STD_MENU;FloatingMenu")
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Appends a menu item to the end of an existing menu.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 menu-ID\tab Specifies the name used in the InsertMenu macro that creates the menu, or the name of a standard WinHelp menu. The new item is appended to this menu.
\par item-ID\tab Specifies the name that WinHelp uses internally to identify the menu item. This name is used by the DisableItem, DeleteItem, EnableItem, and ExtAbleItem macros.
\par item-name\tab Specifies the name that WinHelp displays on the menu for the item. Place an ampersand (&) before the character used for the macro's \ul accelerator key.\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ABYXEJ}
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 macro\tab Specifies one or more macros that WinHelp should run when a user chooses the menu item. Separate multiple macros with colons or semicolons (use only colons if multiple macros are specified in a project file).
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp ignores this macro if it is run in a secondary window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 You can append as many as 20 items to WinHelp's context menu (mnu_floating).
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 If the keyboard accelerator conflicts with other menu access keys, WinHelp ignores the macro.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 Help Workshop automatically converts the AppendItem macro to AI.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro appends a menu item labeled "Tools." Choosing the menu item causes a jump to a topic with the topic ID "tpc1" in the Tls.hlp file:

\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```

### Decoded Body

```text
Back macro

Back()

Displays the previous topic in the history list of the current window. The history list is a list of the last 30 topics (not including pop-up topics) that the user displayed in the current window. This list is kept separately from the list displayed in the History window.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

Comments

The Back macro works only for topics that were displayed in the current window type; it does not display topics that were displayed in other window types.

If a user runs the Back macro when the Back list is empty, WinHelp takes no action.

A user can use Back to back up to a topic only once. After Back is used to return to a topic, that topic is removed from the list of previously displayed topics. For example, after jumps to topics A, B, and C, the user can use Back to return to B. After a jump back to C from B, however, Back goes to A, not B.

{button ,AL("macro_all;History;BACKTRACK;BackFlush")} Related Topics%!AL("macro_all;History;BACKTRACK;BackFlush")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Back macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 Back()\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Displays the previous topic in the history list of the current window. The history list is a list of the last 30 topics (not including pop-up topics) that the user displayed in the current window. This list is kept separately from the list displayed in the History window.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Parameters
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 This macro does not take any parameters.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 The Back macro works only for topics that were displayed in the current window type; it does not display topics that were displayed in other window types.
\par If a user runs the Back macro when the Back list is empty, WinHelp takes no action.
\par A user can use Back to back up to a topic only once. After Back is used to return to a topic, that topic is removed from the list of previously displayed topics. For example, after jumps to topics A, B, and C, the user can use Back to return to B. After a jump back to C from B, however, Back goes to A, not B.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \{button ,AL("macro_all;History;BACKTRACK;BackFlush")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("macro_all;History;BACKTRACK;BackFlush")}
\par
```

### Decoded Body

```text
BookmarkDefine macro

BookmarkDefine()

Displays the Define dialog box, which is available from the Bookmark menu in a main Help window.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

Comments

Bookmarks do not keep track of the window type the topic was originally displayed in. When you use the Bookmark menu or call the BookmarkMore macro, the bookmark topic is displayed in the current window type.

If the BookmarkDefine macro is run from a pop-up window, the bookmark is attached to the topic that invoked the pop-up window.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;BookmarkMore")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;BookmarkMore")

    ```
- Marker `K`

  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BrowseButtons macro;macros,;macros, BrowseButtons
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
BrowseButtons macro

BrowseButtons()

Adds browse buttons to the button bar.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

Comments

If the BrowseButtons macro is used with one or more CreateButton macros in the [CONFIG] section of the project file, the order of the browse buttons on the WinHelp button bar is determined by the order of the BrowseButtons macro in relation to the other macros listed in the [CONFIG] section.

Browse buttons cannot be removed from the button bar after they have been added with the BrowseButtons macro.

For secondary windows, you can add browse buttons as part of the window definition in the project file.

Example

The following macros in the project file cause the Notepad button to appear immediately before the two browse buttons on the button bar:

	[CONFIG]

CreateButton(btn_clock,&Notepad, ExecFile(notepad))

BrowseButtons()

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;CreateButton;Next;Prev;CONFIG")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;CreateButton;Next;Prev;CONFIG")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 BrowseButtons macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 BrowseButtons()\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Adds browse buttons to the button bar.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Parameters\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 This macro does not take any parameters.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 If the BrowseButtons macro is used with one or more CreateButton macros in the [CONFIG] section of the project file, the order of the browse buttons on the WinHelp button bar is determined by the order of the BrowseButtons macro in relation to the other macros listed in the [CONFIG] section.
\par Browse buttons cannot be removed from the button bar after they have been added with the BrowseButtons macro.
\par For secondary windows, you can add browse buttons as part of the window definition in the project file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macros in the project file cause the Notepad button to appear immediately before the two browse buttons on the button bar:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab [CONFIG]\line
CreateButton(btn_clock,&Notepad, ExecFile(notepad))\line
BrowseButtons()
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;CreateButton;Next;Prev;CONFIG")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;CreateButton;Next;Prev;CONFIG")}
\par
```

### Decoded Body

```text
ChangeButtonBinding macro

ChangeButtonBinding(button-ID, button-macro)

Assigns a macro to a button bar button.

Parameter	Description

button-ID	Specifies the identifier assigned to the button by the CreateButton macro, or for a standard Help button.

button-macro	Specifies the macro assigned to the button. Separate multiple macros using colons or semicolons. If multiple macros are specified in a project file, use colons.

Comment

Help Workshop automatically converts the ChangeButtonBinding macro to CBB.

Example

In the following macro, an ALink macro is assigned to a button whose ID is SeeAlso:

	[CONFIG]

ChangeButtonBinding(SeeAlso, ALink(another topic))

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;CreateButton;ChangeEnable;DestroyButton;DisableButton;EnableButton;REF_STD_BTN;CONFIG")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;CreateButton;ChangeEnable;DestroyButton;DisableButton;EnableButton;REF_STD_BTN;CONFIG")
```

### Decoded Body

```text
ChangeEnable macro

ChangeEnable(button-ID, button-macro)

Assigns a macro to a button bar button and enables that button.

Parameter	Description

button-ID	Specifies the identifier assigned to the button by the CreateButton macro, or for a standard Help button.

button-macro	Specifies the macro assigned to the button.

Comments

This macro is equivalent to calling both ChangeButtonBinding and EnableButton.

Help Workshop automatically converts the ChangeEnable macro to CE, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

The ChangeEnable macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

Example

In the following macro, an ALink macro is assigned to a button whose ID is SeeAlso:

	ChangeEnable(SeeAlso, ALink(another topic))

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;CreateButton;REF_STD_BTN")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;CreateButton;REF_STD_BTN")
```

### Decoded Body

```text
ChangeItemBinding macro

ChangeItemBinding(item-ID, item-macro)

Assigns a new macro to a WinHelp menu item.

Parameter	Description

item-ID	Identifies the menu item. The ID must have been created with the AppendItem or InsertItem macros, or must be the ID for a standard WinHelp menu item.

item-macro	Specifies the Help macro or macro string to run when a user chooses the menu item. Separate multiple macros in a string using colons or semicolons.

Comments

This macro is ignored if a user runs it in a secondary window.

Help Workshop automatically converts the ChangeItemBinding macro to CIB.

Example

The following macro changes the menu item identified by "notepad_item" to open the Notepad program:

	ChangeItemBinding(notepad_item, ExecFile(notepad))

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;AppendItem;InsertItem;DeleteItem;EnableItem;ExtAbleItem;ExtInsertMenu;UncheckItem;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;AppendItem;InsertItem;DeleteItem;EnableItem;ExtAbleItem;ExtInsertMenu;UncheckItem;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU")
```

```rtf
\par item-macro\tab Specifies the Help macro or macro string to run when a user chooses the menu item. Separate multiple macros in a string using colons or semicolons.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comments
```

```text
CheckItem macro

CheckItem(item-ID)

Places a check mark beside a menu item.

Parameter	Description

item-ID	Identifies the menu item. The ID must have been created using the AppendItem or InsertItem macros, or must be a standard ID for a WinHelp menu item.

Comments

This macro is ignored if a user runs it in a secondary window.

Use the UncheckItem macro to clear a check mark.

Help Workshop automatically converts the CheckItem macro to CI.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;UncheckItem")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;UncheckItem")
```

### Decoded Body

```text
CloseWindow macro

CloseWindow([window-name])

Closes either a secondary window or the main Help window.

Parameter	Description

window-name	Specifies the name of the window to close. The name "main" is reserved for the main Help window. For secondary windows, the window name is defined in the [WINDOWS] section of the project file.

Comments

If no name is specified, the main window is closed.

If the main window is closed, and no other windows are open, WinHelp will quit.

If the specified window-name does not exist, WinHelp ignores this macro.

Help Workshop automatically converts the CloseWindow macro to CW.

Example

The following macro closes the secondary window named "keys":

	CloseWindow(keys)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all;CloseSecondarys;Exit;WINDOWS")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all;CloseSecondarys;Exit;WINDOWS")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 CloseWindow macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 CloseWindow(\plain\f1\fs16 [window-name]\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Closes either a secondary window or the main Help window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1695\ri125\fi-1575\brdrb\brdrs\tx1695 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1695\ri125\fi-1575\tx1695 window-name\tab Specifies the name of the window to close. The name "main" is reserved for the main Help window. For secondary windows, the window name is defined in the [WINDOWS] section of the project file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1695 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1695 \plain\f1\fs16 If no name is specified, the main window is closed.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1695 If the main window is closed, and no other windows are open, WinHelp will quit.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1695 If the specified window-name does not exist, WinHelp ignores this macro.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1695 Help Workshop automatically converts the CloseWindow macro to CW.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1695 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1695 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro closes the secondary window named "keys":
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab CloseWindow(keys)
```


### Decoded Body

```text
Compare macro

Compare(HLP-filename)

Displays a Help file in a second instance of WinHelp. The current Help file and the second Help file are displayed side-by-side. Most actions performed in one Help file (for example, clicking jumps or the Back and browse buttons) will be automatically reflected in the other file.

Parameter	Description

HLP-filename	Specifies the name of the Help file to display.

Comments

This macro is useful for comparing original and translated versions of the same Help file. You can run this macro from the Jump dialog box by typing the following in the Enter Topic Identifier text box:

!compare("filename.hlp")

For filename.hlp, substitute the name of the file that you want to compare to the one that is already open.

The Compare macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

{button ,AL("macro_all;new_help_author;BAS_TRANSLATING;Test")} Related Topics%!AL("macro_all;new_help_author;BAS_TRANSLATING;Test")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Compare macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 Compare(\plain\f1\fs16 HLP-filename\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Displays a Help file in a second instance of WinHelp. The current Help file and the second Help file are displayed side-by-side. Most actions performed in one Help file (for example, clicking jumps or the Back and browse buttons) will be automatically reflected in the other file.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li2215\ri125\fi-2095\brdrb\brdrs\tx2215 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2215\ri125\fi-2095\tx2215 HLP-filename\tab Specifies the name of the Help file to display.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2215 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2215 \plain\f1\fs16 This macro is useful for comparing original and translated versions of the same Help file. You can run this macro from the Jump dialog box by typing the following in the Enter Topic Identifier text box:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2215 \b !compare("filename.hlp")
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2215 \plain\f1\fs16 For filename.hlp, substitute the name of the file that you want to compare to the one that is already open.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2215 The Compare macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb25\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \{button ,AL("macro_all;new_help_author;BAS_TRANSLATING;Test")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("macro_all;new_help_author;BAS_TRANSLATING;Test")}
```

  - Raw:

### Decoded Body

```text
Contents macro

Contents()

Displays the Contents tab in the Help Topics dialog box if the Help file is associated with a contents file that specifies Contents tab information. If the Help file is not associated with a contents file, the Contents macro displays the default (contents) topic in the current Help file.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

Comments

The contents file is specified by the CNT option in the [OPTIONS] section of the project file. If the project file does not have a CNT option, WinHelp searches for a .cnt file with the same base name as the current Help file.

The default topic32JI7C9 is defined by the CONTENTS option in the [OPTIONS] section of the project file. If the project file does not have a CONTENTS option, the first topic in the first topic file specified in the [FILES] section in the project file is used as the default topic.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;JumpContents;SetContents;CNT;CONTENTS_hpj")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;JumpContents;SetContents;CNT;CONTENTS_hpj")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Contents macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 Contents()\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Displays the Contents tab in the Help Topics dialog box if the Help file is associated with a contents file that specifies Contents tab information. If the Help file is not associated with a contents file, the Contents macro displays the default (contents) topic in the current Help file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Parameters\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 This macro does not take any parameters.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 The contents file is specified by the CNT option in the [OPTIONS] section of the project file. If the project file does not have a CNT option, WinHelp searches for a .cnt file with the same base name as the current Help file.
\par The \ul default topic\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 32JI7C9} is defined by the CONTENTS option in the [OPTIONS] section of the project file. If the project file does not have a CONTENTS option, the first topic in the first topic file specified in the [FILES] section in the project file is used as the default topic.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb25\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;JumpContents;SetContents;CNT;CONTENTS_hpj")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;JumpContents;SetContents;CNT;CONTENTS_hpj")}
\par
```

### Decoded Body

```text
ControlPanel macro

ControlPanel(CPL_name[, panel_name, tabnum])

Opens a control panel applet with a specific tab on top.

Parameter	Description

CPL_name	Specifies the name of the program that contains the control panel applet.

panel_name	Specifies the name of the control panel applet. This must be identical to the text that appears under the control panel applet's icon.

tabnum	Specifies the number of the tab to display on top. The first tab is number 0, the second tab is number 1, and so on.

Comments

Help Workshop converts the ControlPanel macro into the ExecFile macro, which is in turn converted to EF. EF is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

Not all control panel applets recognize the panel_name and tabnum parameters.

The ControlPanel macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

{button ,KL("macros, auxiliary;macros, syntax;ShortCut macro;ExecFile macro ")} Related Topics%!KL("macros, auxiliary;macros, syntax;ShortCut macro;ExecFile macro ")
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Opens a control panel applet with a specific tab on top.

\par panel_name\tab Specifies the name of the control panel applet. This must be identical to the text that appears under the control panel applet's icon.
\par tabnum\tab Specifies the number of the tab to display on top. The first tab is number 0, the second tab is number 1, and so on.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop converts the ControlPanel macro into the ExecFile macro, which is in turn converted to EF. EF is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 Not all control panel applets recognize the panel_name and tabnum parameters.
```

```text
CopyDialog()

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.
```

### Decoded Body

```text
CopyTopic macro

CopyTopic()

Copies all the text in the currently displayed topic to the Clipboard.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

Comments

The CopyTopic macro copies only text, not bitmaps or other graphics.

If the CopyTopic macro is run from a pop-up window, only the text of the topic that invoked the pop-up window is placed on the Clipboard.

Help Workshop automatically converts the CheckTopic macro to CT.

{button ,AL(`MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all')} Related Topics%!AL(`MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 CopyTopic macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 CopyTopic()\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Copies all the text in the currently displayed topic to the Clipboard.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Parameters\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 This macro does not take any parameters.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 The CopyTopic macro copies only text, not bitmaps or other graphics.
\par If the CopyTopic macro is run from a pop-up window, only the text of the topic that invoked the pop-up window is placed on the Clipboard.
\par Help Workshop automatically converts the CheckTopic macro to CT.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \{button ,AL(`MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all')}
\par
```

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `buttons,;buttons, adding to button bar;CB macro;CreateButton macro;macros,;macros, CreateButton`

    ```rtf
    buttons,;buttons, adding to button bar;CB macro;CreateButton macro;macros,;macros, CreateButton
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
CreateButton macro

CreateButton(button-ID, name, macro)

Appends a new button to the button bar.

Parameter	Description

button-ID	Specifies the name that WinHelp uses internally to identify the button. Use this name in the EnableButton, DisableButton or DestroyButton macro to enable, disable, or destroy the button. Use this name in the ChangeButtonBinding macro to change the macro that the button runs in certain topics.

name	Specifies the text that appears on the button. To specify which letter in this text should be used as the accelerator key for the button, place an ampersand (&) before that letter (for more information, see the Comments section). You can use as many as 96 characters on a button label.

macro	Specifies the macro or macros WinHelp should run when a user clicks the button. Separate multiple macros using colons or semicolons.

Comments

If no accelerator is specified, WinHelp automatically chooses the first letter that can be used as an accelerator without conflicting with any previous buttons on the button bar. If an accelerator is specified and it conflicts with a previous button (including standard WinHelp buttons), WinHelp attempts to move the accelerator to the first letter that does not conflict. If no such letter can be found, the accelerator will not be changed. If users attempt to use the accelerator in this case, WinHelp displays a dialog box asking them to choose which button they want.

WinHelp allows a maximum of 22 buttons on a button bar.

If the BrowseButtons macro is used with one or more CreateButton macros in the project file, the buttons appear in the same order on the button bar as the macros appear in the project file.

Help Workshop automatically converts the CreateButton macro to CB, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

Example

The following macro creates a new button labeled "Ideas" that jumps to a topic located in the Ideas.hlp file whose topic ID is "dir":

	CreateButton(btn_ideas, Ideas, JumpId(ideas.hlp, dir))

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;BrowseButtons;EnableButton;DisableButton;DestroyButton;ChangeButtonBinding;button;CONFIG")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;BrowseButtons;EnableButton;DisableButton;DestroyButton;ChangeButtonBinding;button;CONFIG")
```

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 CreateButton macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 CreateButton(\plain\f1\fs16 button-ID\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0name\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0macro\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Appends a new button to the button bar.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 button-ID\tab Specifies the name that WinHelp uses internally to identify the button. Use this name in the EnableButton, DisableButton or DestroyButton macro to enable, disable, or destroy the button. Use this name in the ChangeButtonBinding macro to change the macro that the button runs in certain topics.
\par name\tab Specifies the text that appears on the button. To specify which letter in this text should be used as the accelerator key for the button, place an ampersand (&) before that letter (for more information, see the Comments section). You can use as many as 96 characters on a button label.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 macro\tab Specifies the macro or macros WinHelp should run when a user clicks the button. Separate multiple macros using colons or semicolons.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 If no accelerator is specified, WinHelp automatically chooses the first letter that can be used as an accelerator without conflicting with any previous buttons on the button bar. If an accelerator is specified and it conflicts with a previous button (including standard WinHelp buttons), WinHelp attempts to move the accelerator to the first letter that does not conflict. If no such letter can be found, the accelerator will not be changed. If users attempt to use the accelerator in this case, WinHelp displays a dialog box asking them to choose which button they want.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 WinHelp allows a maximum of 22 buttons on a button bar.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 If the BrowseButtons macro is used with one or more CreateButton macros in the project file, the buttons appear in the same order on the button bar as the macros appear in the project file.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 Help Workshop automatically converts the CreateButton macro to CB, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro creates a new button labeled "Ideas" that jumps to a topic located in the Ideas.hlp file whose topic ID is "dir":
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab CreateButton(btn_ideas, Ideas, JumpId(ideas.hlp, dir))
```

### Decoded Body

```text
DeleteMark macro

DeleteMark(marker-text)

Removes a text marker added by using the SaveMark macro.

Parameter	Description

marker-text	Specifies the text marker previously added by the SaveMark macro.

Comment

If the marker does not exist when the DeleteMark macro is run, WinHelp displays an error message.

Example

The following macro removes the marker “Managing Memory” from a Help file if the marker actually exists:

	IfThen("Managing memory", DeleteMark(Managing Memory))

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;ifthen;SaveMark;GotoMark;IsMark;IsNotMark")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;ifthen;SaveMark;GotoMark;IsMark;IsNotMark")
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Removes a text marker added by using the SaveMark macro.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 If the marker does not exist when the DeleteMark macro is run, WinHelp displays an error message.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Example
```

### Decoded Body

```text
DisableButton macro

DisableButton(button-ID)

Disables (grays out) a button on the WinHelp button bar.

Parameter	Description

button-ID	Specifies the standard button identifier or the identifier assigned to the button by the CreateButton macro.

Comments

A button that is disabled by the DisableButton macro cannot be used in the topic until it is re-enabled by the EnableButton macro or re-activated by a user. For example, a disabled Contents, Index, or Help Topics button is re-enabled if a user clicks an interfile jump.

Help Workshop automatically converts the DisableButton macro to DB.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;EnableButton;DestroyButton;REF_STD_BTN")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;EnableButton;DestroyButton;REF_STD_BTN")
```

```text
Comments
```

```rtf
\b\fs24 EnableButton macro
```

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE0027:001f`

- Marker `>`
  - Decoded: `main`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    main
    ```
- Marker `A`
  - Decoded: `ExecFile;new_macro`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    ExecFile;new_macro
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `EF macro;ExecFile macro;macros,;macros, ExecFile`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    EF macro;ExecFile macro;macros,;macros, ExecFile
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
ExecFile macro

ExecFile(program[, arguments[, display-state[, topic-ID]]])

Runs a program or the program associated with a file.

Parameter	Description

program	Specifies the name of the program to be run or the name of a file. If a file is specified, the program associated with that file type is started.

arguments	Specifies the command-line arguments to send to the program.

display-state	Specifies a value indicating how the program's window is to be shown. If this parameter is not specified, SW_SHOW is used (activates the window and shows it in its default size and position). This optional parameter can be one of the following values.

Value	Meaning

SW_HIDE	Hides the window.

SW_MINIMIZE	Minimizes the window.

SW_RESTORE	Restores the window to its original size and position if the window is minimized or maximized.

SW_SHOW	Activates the window and displays it in its current size and position.

SW_SHOWMAXIMIZED	Activates the window and maximizes it.

SW_SHOWMINIMIZED	Activates the window and displays it minimized.

SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE	Displays the window minimized, but WinHelp keeps the focus.

SW_SHOWNA	Displays a window in its current state, but WinHelp keeps the focus. (If the window was minimized before this call, it will stay minimized.)

SW_SHOWNOACTIVATE	Displays a window in its most recent size and position, but WinHelp keeps the focus.

SW_SHOWNORMAL	Activates and displays the window. If the window is minimized or maximized, Windows restores it to its original size and position (same as SW_RESTORE).

topic-ID	Specifies the ID of the topic to display if the specified file or program cannot be started.

Comments

If a path is specified, WinHelp first searches for the file in the specified path. If the file is not found or no path was specified, WinHelp searches the same locations as it does when it searches for Help files:

·	The folder of the current Help file.

·	The current folder.

·	The System subfolder in the Windows folder.

·	The Windows folder.

·	The folders listed in the PATH environment.

·	The location specified in the Winhelp.ini file.

·	The Help portion of the registry.

Help Workshop automatically converts the ExecFile macro to EF, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

The ExecFile macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

Example

The following example opens the WIN.INI file in the program associated with .ini files:

	ExecFile(win.ini)

{button ,KL("macros, auxiliary;macros, syntax;ControlPanel macro;ExecFile macro;ShellExecute")} Related Topics%!KL("macros, auxiliary;macros, syntax;ControlPanel macro;ExecFile macro;ShellExecute")
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Runs a program or the program associated with a file.

\par display-state\tab Specifies a value indicating how the program's window is to be shown. If this parameter is not specified, SW_SHOW is used (activates the window and shows it in its default size and position). This optional parameter can be one of the following values.

\par SW_SHOWMINIMIZED\tab Activates the window and displays it minimized.

\par SW_SHOWNA\tab Displays a window in its current state, but WinHelp keeps the focus. (If the window was minimized before this call, it will stay minimized.)
```

### Decoded Body

```text
ExtAbleItem macro

ExtAbleItem(item-ID, display-state)

Enables or disables a menu item.

Parameter	Description

item-ID	Specifies the identifier assigned to the menu item by the AppendItem, ExtInsertItem, or InsertItem macro.

display-state	Specifies the state of the menu item. This parameter can be one of the following values:

Value	Meaning

GRAYED	Disables (grays out) the menu item.

CHECKED	Places a check mark next to the menu item.

Comments

You cannot use this macro to change a standard WinHelp menu item.

WinHelp ignores this macro if it is run in a secondary window.

The ExtAbleItem macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;AppendItem;ChangeItemBinding;DeleteItem;DisableItem;EnableItem;ExtInsertItem;ExtInsertMenu;InsertItem;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;AppendItem;ChangeItemBinding;DeleteItem;DisableItem;EnableItem;ExtInsertItem;ExtInsertMenu;InsertItem;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU")
```

## 172. ExtInsertItem macro

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE0027:0023`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE0027:0023
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `ExtInsertItem macro`

    ```rtf
    ExtInsertItem macro
    ```
- Marker `#`


  - Raw:

- Marker `A`
  - Decoded: `ExtInsertItem;MACROS_MENU`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    ExtInsertItem;MACROS_MENU
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `ExtInsertItem macro;macros,;macros, ExtInsertItem;menu items;menu items, inserting at given position`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    ExtInsertItem macro;macros,;macros, ExtInsertItem;menu items;menu items, inserting at given position
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
ExtInsertItem macro

ExtInsertItem(menu-ID, item-ID, item-name, macro, position[, display-state])

Inserts a menu item at a given position on an existing menu.

Parameter	Description

menu-ID	Identifies either a standard WinHelp menu or a menu previously created by the ExtInsertMenu or InsertMenu macro. The new item is inserted in this menu.

item-ID	Specifies the name that WinHelp uses internally to identify the menu item.

item-name	Specifies the name WinHelp displays on the menu for the item. This name is case-sensitive. An ampersand (&) before a character in the name identifies it as the item's accelerator key.ABYXEJ

macro	Specifies the macro or macro string to run when a user clicks the menu item. Separate multiple macros in a string using colons or semicolons. Colons are required if this macro is specified in a project file.

position	Specifies the numeric position of the menu item in the menu. Position 0 is the first or topmost position in the menu.

display-state	Specifies the state of the menu item. This parameter may be one of the following values:

Value	Meaning

GRAYED	Disables (grays-out) the menu item.

CHECKED	Places a check mark next to the menu item.

SEPARATOR	Adds a separator bar to the menu.

Comments

The item-ID parameter can be used in a subsequent DisableItem, EnableItem, ExtAbleItem, or DeleteItem macro to remove or disable the item or to change the operations that the item performs in certain topics.

WinHelp ignores this macro if it is run in a secondary window.

The specified accelerator keys must be unique. If a key conflicts with other accelerator keys, WinHelp displays the error message "Unable to add item" and ignores the macro.

The ExtInsertItem macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

Example

The following macro inserts a menu item labeled “WordPad” on WinHelp’s File menu:

	ExtInsertItem(mnu_file, itm_wpad, &WordPad, ExecFile(wordpad.exe), 3)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;AppendItem;ChangeItemBinding;DeleteItem;DisableItem;EnableItem;ExtAbleItem;InsertItem;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU;CONFIG")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;AppendItem;ChangeItemBinding;DeleteItem;DisableItem;EnableItem;ExtAbleItem;InsertItem;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU;CONFIG")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 ExtInsertItem macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 ExtInsertItem(\plain\f1\fs16 menu-ID\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0item-ID\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0item-name\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0macro\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0position[\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0display-state]\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Inserts a menu item at a given position on an existing menu.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\brdrb\brdrs\tx1435 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\tx1435 menu-ID\tab Identifies either a standard WinHelp menu or a menu previously created by the ExtInsertMenu or InsertMenu macro. The new item is inserted in this menu.
\par item-ID\tab Specifies the name that WinHelp uses internally to identify the menu item.
\par item-name\tab Specifies the name WinHelp displays on the menu for the item. This name is case-sensitive. An ampersand (&) before a character in the name identifies it as the item's \ul accelerator key.\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ABYXEJ}
\par macro\tab Specifies the macro or macro string to run when a user clicks the menu item. Separate multiple macros in a string using colons or semicolons. Colons are required if this macro is specified in a project file.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\tx1435 position\tab Specifies the numeric position of the menu item in the menu. Position 0 is the first or topmost position in the menu.
\par display-state\tab Specifies the state of the menu item. This parameter may be one of the following values:
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2875\ri125\fi-1435\brdrb\brdrs\tx2875 \b Value\tab Meaning
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2875\ri125\fi-1435\tx2875 \plain\f1\fs16 GRAYED\tab Disables (grays-out) the menu item.
\par CHECKED\tab Places a check mark next to the menu item.
\par SEPARATOR\tab Adds a separator bar to the menu.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2875 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2875 \plain\f1\fs16 The item-ID parameter can be used in a subsequent DisableItem, EnableItem, ExtAbleItem, or DeleteItem macro to remove or disable the item or to change the operations that the item performs in certain topics.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2875 WinHelp ignores this macro if it is run in a secondary window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2875 The specified accelerator keys must be unique. If a key conflicts with other accelerator keys, WinHelp displays the error message "Unable to add item" and ignores the macro.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2875 The ExtInsertItem macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2875 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2875 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro inserts a menu item labeled \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 WordPad\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  on WinHelp\plain\f0\fs16 \'92\f1 s File menu:
\par \pard\keep\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab ExtInsertItem(mnu_file, itm_wpad, &WordPad, ExecFile(wordpad.exe), 3)
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;AppendItem;ChangeItemBinding;DeleteItem;DisableItem;EnableItem;ExtAbleItem;InsertItem;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU;CONFIG")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;AppendItem;ChangeItemBinding;DeleteItem;DisableItem;EnableItem;ExtAbleItem;InsertItem;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU;CONFIG")}
\par
```

## 173. ExtInsertMenu macro

### WinHelp Footnotes

### Decoded Body

```text
ExtInsertMenu macro

ExtInsertMenu(parent-ID, menu-ID, menu-name, menu-position[, display-state])

Inserts a submenu in a previously defined menu.

Parameter	Description

parent-ID	Specifies the name that WinHelp uses to identify the parent menu to which this submenu is added. For a custom menu, this parameter is the name that was used when the menu was created by the ExtInsertMenu or the InsertMenu macro.

menu-ID	Specifies the name that WinHelp uses internally to identify the menu. This identifier can be used by the AppendItem, ExtInsertItem or ExtInsertMenu macros to access to the menu.

menu-name	Specifies the name that WinHelp displays for this menu. An ampersand (&) before a character in the name identifies it as the menu's accelerator.

menu-position	Specifies the integer position on the menu bar of the new menu name. Position 0 is the first position.

display-state	Specifies the state of the menu item. This parameter may be one of the following values:

Value	Meaning

CHECKED	Places a check mark next to the menu item.

GRAYED	Disables (grays out) the menu item.

Comment

WinHelp ignores this macro if it is run in a secondary window.

Example

The following macro adds a Wizard submenu to the standard Help menu:

	ExtInsertMenu(mnu_help, mnu_wizard, &Wizard, 2)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;ExtInsertItem;ExtAbleItem;AppendItem;InsertMenu;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU;CONFIG")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;ExtInsertItem;ExtAbleItem;AppendItem;InsertMenu;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU;CONFIG")
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Inserts a submenu in a previously defined menu.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1725\ri125\fi-1605\brdrb\brdrs\tx1725 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1725\ri125\fi-1595\tx1725 parent-ID\tab Specifies the name that WinHelp uses to identify the parent menu to which this submenu is added. For a custom menu, this parameter is the name that was used when the menu was created by the ExtInsertMenu or the InsertMenu macro.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1725\ri125\fi-1605\tx1725 menu-ID\tab Specifies the name that WinHelp uses internally to identify the menu. This identifier can be used by the AppendItem, ExtInsertItem or ExtInsertMenu macros to access to the menu.

\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1725\ri125\fi-1605\tx1705 display-state\tab Specifies the state of the menu item. This parameter may be one of the following values:
```

### Decoded Body

```text
FileExist macro

FileExist(filename)

Checks to see whether the specified file or program exists.

Parameter	Description

filename	Specifies the name of the file.

Comments

This macro can be used in conjunction with macros such as IfThenElse, which use the result of a Boolean macro to determine what action to take.

If a path is specified, WinHelp first searches for the file in the specified path. If the file is not found, or no path was specified, WinHelp searches the same locations it does to find a Help file:

·	The folder of the current Help file.

·	The current folder.

·	The System subfolder in the Windows folder.

·	The Windows folder.

·	The folders listed in the PATH environment.

·	The location specified in the Winhelp.ini file.

·	The Help portion of the registry.

Help Workshop automatically converts the FileExist macro to FE, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

The FileExist macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

Example

The following macro checks to see if “Myapp.exe” has been installed. If the file is present, WinHelp runs it. If the file is not present, WinHelp displays a topic:

	IfThenElse(FileExist(myapp.exe), ExecFile(myapp), JumpId(install_my_app))

{button ,AL(`macro_all;execfile;ifthenelse;ifthen')} Related Topics%!AL(`execfile;ifthenelse;ifthen;macro_all')
```
