\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Checks to see whether the specified file or program exists.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1725\ri125\fi-1605\brdrb\brdrs\tx1725 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1725\ri125\fi-1595\tx1725 filename\tab Specifies the name of the file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1725 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1725 \plain\f1\fs16 This macro can be used in conjunction with macros such as IfThenElse, which use the result of a Boolean macro to determine what action to take.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1725 If a path is specified, WinHelp first searches for the file in the specified path. If the file is not found, or no path was specified, WinHelp searches the same locations it does to find a Help file:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab The folder of the current Help file.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab The current folder.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab The System subfolder in the Windows folder.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab The Windows folder.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab The folders listed in the PATH environment.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab The location specified in the Winhelp.ini file.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab The Help portion of the registry.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 Help Workshop automatically converts the FileExist macro to FE, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 The FileExist macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro checks to see if \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 Myapp.exe\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  has been installed. If the file is present, WinHelp runs it. If the file is not present, WinHelp displays a topic:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab IfThenElse(FileExist(myapp.exe), ExecFile(myapp), JumpId(install_my_app))

### Decoded Body

```text
FloatingMenu macro

FloatingMenu()

Displays the context (floating) menu at the current mouse cursor position. This menu also appears when users click a topic using their right mouse button.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

Comments

You can add as many as 20 menu items to the context menu.

The FloatingMenu macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;ResetMenu;AppendItem;InsertItem;ExtInsertItem;menu")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;ResetMenu;AppendItem;InsertItem;ExtInsertItem;menu")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 FloatingMenu macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 FloatingMenu()\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Displays the context (floating) menu at the current mouse cursor position. This menu also appears when users click a topic using their right mouse button.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Parameters\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 This macro does not take any parameters.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 You can add as many as 20 menu items to the context menu.

### Decoded Body

```text
FocusWindow macro

FocusWindow(window-name)

Changes the focus to the specified window.

Parameter	Description

window-name	Specifies the name of the window that should receive the focus. The name "main" is reserved for the main Help window. For secondary windows, the window name is defined in the [WINDOWS] section of the project file.

Comment

This macro is ignored if the specified window does not exist.

Example

The following macro changes the focus to the secondary window “keys”:

	FocusWindow(keys)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all;CloseSecondarys;CloseWindow;PositionWindow;UpdateWindow;WINDOWS")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all;CloseSecondarys;CloseWindow;PositionWindow;UpdateWindow;WINDOWS")
```

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Changes the focus to the specified window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1695\ri125\fi-1575\brdrb\brdrs\tx1695 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1695\ri125\fi-1575\tx1695 window-name\tab Specifies the name of the window that should receive the focus. The name "main" is reserved for the main Help window. For secondary windows, the window name is defined in the [WINDOWS] section of the project file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1695 \b Comment
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1695 \plain\f1\fs16 This macro is ignored if the specified window does not exist.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1695 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1695 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro changes the focus to the secondary window \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 keys\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1 :

lParam	Specifies the second argument of the message.

### Decoded Body

```text
GotoMark macro

GotoMark(marker-text)

Jumps to a marker set by the SaveMark macro.

Parameter	Description

marker-text	Specifies a text marker previously defined by the SaveMark macro.

Comment

WinHelp displays an error message if the specified text marker does not exist.

Example

The following macro jumps to the marker “Managing Memory” if it has been previously set.

	IfThen("Managing Memory", GotoMark(Managing Memory))

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;DeleteMark;IsMark;IsNotMark;SaveMark")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;DeleteMark;IsMark;IsNotMark;SaveMark")
```

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16

\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comment
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp displays an error message if the specified text marker does not exist.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro jumps to the marker \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 Managing Memory\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  if it has been previously set.

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HelpOnTop macro;macros,;macros, HelpOnTop;on-top state;windows,;windows, on-top state`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HelpOnTop macro;macros,;macros, HelpOnTop;on-top state;windows,;windows, on-top state
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HelpOnTop macro

HelpOnTop()

Toggles the authored on-top state of a Help window.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

Comments

A Help window can have three states:

Default	Authored as part of the window definition in the project file

On Top	Forced on

Not On Top	Forced off

The latter two states, set by the user, affect all Help windows. If the user sets either the On Top or Not On Top options (from the Options menu, the Menu button, or a context menu), the HelpOnTop macro has no effect.

You can use the Generate macro to send a message to WinHelp as if the user had selected a menu command. To specify the Default option, use a value of 1470 as the message parameter in the Generate macro. To set the On Top or Not On Top options, use 1471 or 1472, respectively.

In WinHelp version 4.0, the HelpOnTop macro affects only the current window.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all;WINDOWS")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all;WINDOWS")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 HelpOnTop macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 HelpOnTop()\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Toggles the authored on-top state of a Help window.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Parameters\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 This macro does not take any parameters.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 A Help window can have three states:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1255\ri125\fi-1145\tx295\tx1255 Default\tab Authored as part of the window definition in the project file
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li1255\ri125\fi-1145\tx295\tx1255 On Top\tab Forced on
\par Not On Top\tab Forced off
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295\tx1255 The latter two states, set by the user, affect all Help windows. If the user sets either the On Top or Not On Top options (from the Options menu, the Menu button, or a context menu), the HelpOnTop macro has no effect.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295\tx1255 You can use the Generate macro to send a message to WinHelp as if the user had selected a menu command. To specify the Default option, use a value of 1470 as the message parameter in the Generate macro. To set the On Top or Not On Top options, use 1471 or 1472, respectively.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295\tx1255 In WinHelp version 4.0, the HelpOnTop macro affects only the current window.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb25\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all;WINDOWS")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all;WINDOWS")}
\par
```

History macro

History()

Displays the historyNJZ4W5 list, which shows the last 31 topics a user viewed in the main Help window since opening the file in WinHelp.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

Comments

The number of topics kept in the history list can be changed by using the BACKTRACK switch in the Win.ini file.

WinHelp ignores this macro if it is run in a secondary window.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;Back;BACKTRACK")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;Back;BACKTRACK")

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 This macro does not take any parameters.

    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
IfThen macro

IfThen("marker"/macro, macro)

Runs a macro if a given marker exists or a macro returns a non-zero value.

Parameter	Description

marker/macro	Specifies either a text marker previously created by using the SaveMark macro, or a macro that returns a Boolean value. If a marker is used, it must be enclosed in quotation marks. If a macro is used, it must not be enclosed in quotation marks (so Help Workshop can distinguish between a marker and a macro).

macro	Specifies one or more macros to run if the marker exists. Separate multiple macros using colons or semicolons.

Comment

Help Workshop automatically converts the IfThen macro to IF.

Example

The following macro jumps to the topic whose topic ID is “man_mem” if a marker named "Managing Memory" has been set by the SaveMark macro:

	IfThen("Managing Memory", JumpId(man_mem))

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;DeleteMark;GotoMark;IfThenElse;IsMark;IsNotMark;Not;SaveMark")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;DeleteMark;GotoMark;IfThenElse;IsMark;IsNotMark;Not;SaveMark")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 IfThen macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 IfThen("\plain\f1\fs16 marker\b "\plain\f1\fs16 /macro\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0macro\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Runs a macro if a given marker exists or a macro returns a non-zero value.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 marker/macro\tab Specifies either a text marker previously created by using the SaveMark macro, or a macro that returns a Boolean value. If a marker is used, it must be enclosed in quotation marks. If a macro is used, it must \b not\plain\f1\fs16  be enclosed in quotation marks (so Help Workshop can distinguish between a marker and a macro).
\par macro\tab Specifies one or more macros to run if the marker exists. Separate multiple macros using colons or semicolons.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comment
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop automatically converts the IfThen\b  \plain\f1\fs16 macro to IF.\b 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro jumps to the topic whose topic ID is \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 man_mem\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  if a marker named "Managing Memory" has been set by the SaveMark macro:

### Decoded Body

```text
IfThenElse macro

IfThenElse("marker"/macro, macro1, macro2)

Runs one of two macros depending on whether or not a marker exists.

Parameter	Description

marker/macro	Specifies either a text marker previously created by the SaveMark macro or a macro that returns a Boolean value. If a marker is used, it must be enclosed in quotation marks. If a macro is used, it must not be enclosed in quotation marks (so Help Workshop can distinguish between a marker and a macro).

macro1	Specifies one or more macros to run if the marker exists. Separate multiple macros using colons or semicolons.

macro2	Specifies one or more macros to run if the marker does not exist. Separate multiple macros using colons or semicolons.

Comment

Help Workshop automatically converts the IfThenElse macro to IE.

Example

The following macro jumps to the topic whose ID is “mem” if a marker named “Memory” has been set by the SaveMark macro. If the marker does not exist, the macro jumps to the next topic in the browse sequence.1SRKR1R

	IfThenElse("Memory", JumpId(mem), Next())

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;DeleteMark;GotoMark;IfThen;IsMark;IsNotMark;Not;SaveMark")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;DeleteMark;GotoMark;IfThen;IsMark;IsNotMark;Not;SaveMark")
```

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Runs one of two macros depending on whether or not a marker exists.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 marker/macro\tab Specifies either a text marker previously created by the SaveMark macro or a macro that returns a Boolean value. If a marker is used, it must be enclosed in quotation marks. If a macro is used, it must \b not\plain\f1\fs16  be enclosed in quotation marks (so Help Workshop can distinguish between a marker and a macro).
\par macro1\tab Specifies one or more macros to run if the marker exists. Separate multiple macros using colons or semicolons.
\par macro2\tab Specifies one or more macros to run if the marker does not exist. Separate multiple macros using colons or semicolons.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comment
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop automatically converts the IfThenElse macro to IE.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro jumps to the topic whose ID is \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 mem\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  if a marker named \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 Memory\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  has been set by the SaveMark macro. If the marker does not exist, the macro jumps to the next topic in the \ul browse sequence.\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1SRKR1R}
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab IfThenElse("Memory", JumpId(mem), Next())

### Decoded Body

```text
InitMPrint macro

InitMPrint()

Initializes WinHelp in preparation for printing multiple topics.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

Comments

This macro displays the Printer Setup dialog box. If a user clicks OK, the InitMPrint macro returns TRUE. Otherwise, it returns FALSE. This macro should be used as the first parameter in an IfThen macro to ensure that printing multiple topics occurs only if a user clicks OK.

The InitMPrint macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

Example

The following example prints the two topics whose IDs are “topic_a” and “topic_b”:

	IfThen(InitMPrint(), MPrintId(topic_a): MPrintId(topic_b): EndMPrint())

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;EndMPrint;MPrintID;MPrintHash;Print")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;EndMPrint;MPrintID;MPrintHash;Print")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 


\b\fs24 InitMPrint macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 InitMPrint()\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Initializes WinHelp in preparation for printing multiple topics.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Parameters\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 This macro does not take any parameters.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 This macro displays the Printer Setup dialog box. If a user clicks OK, the InitMPrint macro returns TRUE. Otherwise, it returns FALSE. This macro should be used as the first parameter in an IfThen macro to ensure that printing multiple topics occurs only if a user clicks OK.
\par The InitMPrint macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example prints the two topics whose IDs are \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 topic_a\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  and \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 topic_b\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1 :
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab IfThen(InitMPrint(), MPrintId(topic_a): MPrintId(topic_b): EndMPrint())
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;EndMPrint;MPrintID;MPrintHash;Print")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;EndMPrint;MPrintID;MPrintHash;Print")}
\par

- Marker `K`

### Decoded Body

```text
InsertItem macro

InsertItem(menu-ID, item-ID, item-name, macro, position)

Inserts a menu item at a given position on an existing menu. The menu can either be one you create by using the InsertMenu macro or a standard WinHelp menu.

Parameter	Description

menu-ID	Identifies either a standard WinHelp menu or a menu previously created by the ExtInsertMenu or InsertMenu macro.

item-ID	Specifies the name that WinHelp uses internally to identify the menu item.

item-name	Specifies the name that WinHelp displays on the menu for the item. This name is case-sensitive. An ampersand (&) before a character in the name identifies it as the item's accelerator.

macro	Specifies a macro or macro string to run when a user clicks the menu item. The macro must be enclosed in quotation marks. If multiple macros are specified in the project file, separate the macros by using semicolons or colons.

position	Specifies the integer position of the menu item in the menu. Position 0 is the first or topmost position on the menu.

Comments

The item ID parameter can be used in a subsequent EnableItem, ExtAbleItem, DisableItem or DeleteItem macro to remove or disable the item or to change the operations that the item performs in certain topics.

WinHelp ignores this macro if it is run in a secondary window.

The specified accelerator keys must be unique. If a key conflicts with other accelerator keys, WinHelp displays the error message "Unable to add item" and ignores the macro.

Example

The following macro inserts a menu item labeled “Tools” as the third item on a menu whose identifier is “MNU_BKS”. Selecting the menu item causes a jump to a topic located in the Tls.hlp file whose topic ID is "tls1":

	InsertItem(mnu_bks, m_tls, &Tools, JI(`tls.hlp', `tls1'), 3)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;AppendItem;ChangeItemBinding;DeleteItem;DisableItem;EnableItem;ExtAbleItem;ExtInsertItem;InsertMenu;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU;CONFIG")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;InsertMenu;AppendItem;ChangeItemBinding;DeleteItem;DisableItem;EnableItem;ExtAbleItem;ExtInsertItem;ResetMenu;REF_STD_MENU;CONFIG")
```

\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 InsertItem(\plain\f1\fs16 menu-ID\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0item-ID\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0item-name\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0macro\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0position\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Inserts a menu item at a given position on an existing menu. The menu can either be one you create by using the InsertMenu macro or a standard WinHelp menu.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 menu-ID\tab Identifies either a standard WinHelp menu or a menu previously created by the ExtInsertMenu or InsertMenu macro.
\par item-ID\tab Specifies the name that WinHelp uses internally to identify the menu item.
\par item-name\tab Specifies the name that WinHelp displays on the menu for the item. This name is case-sensitive. An ampersand (&) before a character in the name identifies it as the item's accelerator.
\par macro\tab Specifies a macro or macro string to run when a user clicks the menu item. The macro must be enclosed in quotation marks. If multiple macros are specified in the project file, separate the macros by using semicolons or colons.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 position\tab Specifies the integer position of the menu item in the menu. Position 0 is the first or topmost position on the menu.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The item ID parameter can be used in a subsequent EnableItem, ExtAbleItem, DisableItem or DeleteItem macro to remove or disable the item or to change the operations that the item performs in certain topics.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 WinHelp ignores this macro if it is run in a secondary window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 The specified accelerator keys must be unique. If a key conflicts with other accelerator keys, WinHelp displays the error message "Unable to add item" and ignores the macro.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro inserts a menu item labeled \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 Tools\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  as the third item on a menu whose identifier is \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 MNU_BKS\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1 . Selecting the menu item causes a jump to a topic located in the Tls.hlp file whose topic ID is "tls1":
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab InsertItem(mnu_bks, m_tls, &Tools, JI(`tls.hlp', `tls1'), 3)

### Decoded Body

```text
InsertMenu macro

InsertMenu(menu-ID, menu-name, menu-position)

Inserts a new menu in the WinHelp menu bar.

Parameter	Description

menu-ID	Specifies the name that WinHelp uses internally to identify the menu. This identifier can be used by the AppendItem, ExtInsertItem, and ExtInsertMenu macros to add macros to the menu.

menu-name	Specifies the name that WinHelp displays on the menu bar. An ampersand (&) before a character in the name identifies it as the menu's accelerator.

menu-position	Specifies the position on the menu bar of the new menu name. This parameter must be an integer. Positions are numbered from left to right, with position 0 being the left-most menu.

Comments

The specified accelerator keys must be unique. If a key conflicts with other accelerator keys, WinHelp displays the error message "Unable to add menu" and ignores the macro.

WinHelp ignores this macro if it is run in a secondary window.

Example

The following macro adds a menu named “Utilities” to the WinHelp program. The label “Utilities” appears as the fourth item on the WinHelp menu bar. The user can press ALT+U to open the menu.

	InsertMenu(IDM_UTIL, &Utilities, 3)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;AppendItem;ExtInsertItem;ExtInsertMenu;ResetMenu")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all;AppendItem;ExtInsertItem;ExtInsertMenu;ResetMenu")
```

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Inserts a new menu in the WinHelp menu bar.

\par menu-position\tab Specifies the position on the menu bar of the new menu name. This parameter must be an integer. Positions are numbered from left to right, with position 0 being the left-most menu.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1725 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1725 \plain\f1\fs16 The specified accelerator keys must be unique. If a key conflicts with other accelerator keys, WinHelp displays the error message "Unable to add menu" and ignores the macro.

The label \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 Utilities\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  appears as the fourth item on the WinHelp menu bar. The user can press ALT+U to open the menu.

### Decoded Body

```text
IsBook macro

IsBook()

Determines whether WinHelp is running as a standalone system (a double-clicked book icon), or if it is being run from a program. This macro can be used as the first parameter of a IfThen or IfThenElse macro to take specific action depending on whether the current Help file is being run as a double-clicked book icon.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

Comments

The IsBook macro returns a value of TRUE if WinHelp is being run as a book; otherwise, the macro returns FALSE.

The IsBook macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

{button ,AL("macro_all;IfThen;IfThenElse ")} Related Topics%!AL("macro_all;IfThen;IfThenElse ")
```

This macro can be used as the first parameter of a IfThen or IfThenElse macro to take specific action depending on whether the current Help file is being run as a double-clicked book icon.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Parameters\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 This macro does not take any parameters.

### Decoded Body

```text
IsMark macro

IsMark(marker-text)

Tests whether or not a marker that was set by the SaveMark macro exists.

Parameter	Description

marker-text	Specifies a text marker that was previously created by the SaveMark macro.

Comments

The IsMark macro is used as a parameter in the conditional macros IfThen and IfThenElse. The IsMark macro returns TRUE if the mark exists or FALSE if it does not.

To reverse the result of the IsMark macro, use the IsNotMark macro.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IfThen;IfThenElse;IsNotMark;SaveMark")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IfThen;IfThenElse;IsNotMark;SaveMark")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 IsMark macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 IsMark(\plain\f1\fs16 marker-text\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Tests whether or not a marker that was set by the SaveMark macro exists.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 marker-text\tab Specifies a text marker that was previously created by the SaveMark macro.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The IsMark\b  \plain\f1\fs16 macro is used as a parameter in the conditional macros IfThen and IfThenElse. The IsMark macro returns TRUE if the mark exists or FALSE if it does not.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 To reverse the result of the IsMark macro, use the IsNotMark macro.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295

### Decoded Body

```text
IsNotMark macro

IsNotMark("marker-text")

Tests whether or not a marker that was set by the SaveMark macro exists.

Parameter	Description

marker-text	Specifies a text marker that was previously created by the SaveMark macro.

Comments

The IsNotMark macro is used as a parameter in the conditional macros IfThen or IfThenElse. The IsNotMark macro returns FALSE if the mark exists or TRUE if it does not. This result is the exact opposite of the implicit IsMark macro that Help Workshop usually uses for the IfThen and IfThenElse macros.

Help Workshop automatically converts the IsNotMacro macro to NM, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

The IsNotMark macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IfThen;IfThenElse;IsMark;SaveMark")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IfThen;IfThenElse;IsMark;SaveMark")
```

\b\fs24 IsNotMark macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 IsNotMark("\plain\f1\fs16 marker-text\b ")\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Tests whether or not a marker that was set by the SaveMark macro exists.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 marker-text\tab Specifies a text marker that was previously created by the SaveMark macro.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The IsNotMark macro is used as a parameter in the conditional macros IfThen or IfThenElse. The IsNotMark macro returns FALSE if the mark exists or TRUE if it does not. This result is the exact opposite of the implicit IsMark macro that Help Workshop usually uses for the IfThen and IfThenElse macros.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 Help Workshop automatically converts the IsNotMacro macro to NM, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 The IsNotMark macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295

```text
JumpContents macro

JumpContents(filename)

Jumps to the default (contents) topic of the specified Help file.

Parameter	Description

filename	Specifies the name of the destination file for the jump. If WinHelp cannot find this file, it displays an error message and does not perform the jump.

Example

The following macro jumps to the contents topic of the Progman.hlp file:

	JumpContents(progman.hlp)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;Contents;SetContents;HelpOn;CONTENTS_hpj")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;Contents;SetContents;HelpOn;CONTENTS_hpj")

### Decoded Body

```text
JumpContext macro

JumpContext([filename>window-name,] context-number)

Jumps to a topic identified by a context number.

Parameter	Description

filename	Specifies the name of the destination file for the jump. This parameter is optional.

window-name	Specifies the window type in which to display the topic.

context-number	Specifies the context number2O07FH3 of the topic in the destination file. The context number must be defined in the [MAP] section of the project file.

Comments

The context number is identified by an entry in the [MAP] section of the project file.

Help Workshop automatically converts the JumpContext macro to JC, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

Example

The following macro jumps to the topic mapped to the context number 801 in the Progman.hlp file:

	JumpContext(progman.hlp, 801)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;JumpHash;JumpId;JumpKeyword;PopupContext;PopupHash;PopupId;MAP")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;JumpHash;JumpId;JumpKeyword;PopupContext;PopupHash;PopupId;MAP")

### Decoded Body

```text
JumpHash macro

JumpHash([filename>window-name,] hash-code)

Jumps to a topic identified by a hash numberOYZM9.

Parameter	Description

filename	Specifies the name of the Help file containing the hash number. This optional parameter is used when jumping to a topic that is not in the current Help file.

window-name	Specifies the window type in which to display the topic.

hash-code	Specifies the hash number of the topic in the destination file.

Comments

You can use the Report command in Help Workshop to list the hash numbers of all the topics in a Help file. This enables you to display information about a Help file for which you have no topic, project, or contents files.

Help Workshop automatically converts the JumpHash macro to JH.

The JumpHash macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

Example

The following macro displays this topic:

	JumpHash(hcw, 4104506004)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;JumpId;PopupHash;PopupId;REPORT")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;JumpId;PopupHash;REPORT")
```

### Decoded Body

```text
JumpId macro

JumpId([filename>window-name,] topic-ID)

Jumps to the topic whose topic ID is specified.

Parameter	Description

filename	Specifies the name of the Help file that contains the topic ID. This optional parameter is used when jumping to a topic that is not in the current Help file.

window-name	Specifies the window type in which to display the topic.

topic-ID	Specifies the ID of the topic you want to jump to.

Comments

When the window-name parameter is used, it must be preceded by a greater-than sign (>).

Help Workshop automatically converts the JumpId macro to JI.

Example

The following macro jumps to a topic with “another_topic” as its topic ID in the current Help file:

	JumpId(another_topic)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;JumpHash;JumpKeyword;JumpContext;PopupContext;PopupHash;PopupId")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;JumpHash;JumpKeyword;JumpContext;PopupContext;PopupHash;PopupId")

    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
JumpKeyword macro

JumpKeyword([filename,] keyword)

Searches for keywords specified by K-footnotes. If WinHelp finds no matches, it displays the Help Topics dialog box with the Index tab on top. If WinHelp finds one match, it displays the topic. If WinHelp finds more than one match, it displays the Topics Found dialog box and lists the topic titles.

Parameter	Description

filename	Specifies the name of the Help file that contains the keyword. Use this optional parameter when you jump to a topic that is not in the current Help file.

keyword	Specifies one or more keywords to search for. Separate multiple keywords using a semicolon. If any keyword contains a comma, enclose the entire keyword string in quotation marks.

Comments

This macro is functionally equivalent to the KLink macro.

Help Workshop automatically converts the JumpKeyword macro to JK.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;KLink")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;Klink")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 JumpKeyword macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 JumpKeyword(\plain\f1\fs16 [filename\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 ]\'a0keyword\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Searches for keywords specified by K-footnotes. If WinHelp finds no matches, it displays the Help Topics dialog box with the Index tab on top. If WinHelp finds one match, it displays the topic. If WinHelp finds more than one match, it displays the Topics Found dialog box and lists the topic titles.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1635\ri125\fi-1515\brdrb\brdrs\tx1635 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1635\ri125\fi-1515\tx1635 filename\tab Specifies the name of the Help file that contains the keyword. Use this optional parameter when you jump to a topic that is not in the current Help file.
\par keyword\tab Specifies one or more keywords to search for. Separate multiple keywords using a semicolon. If any keyword contains a comma, enclose the entire keyword string in quotation marks.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1635 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1635 \plain\f1\fs16 This macro is functionally equivalent to the KLink macro.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1635 Help Workshop automatically converts the JumpKeyword macro to JK.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb25\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;KLink")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;Klink")}
\par
```

## 200. KLink macro

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE0027:003f`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE0027:003f
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `KLink macro`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    KLink macro
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `KLink`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    KLink
    ```
- Marker `>`
  - Decoded: `main`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    main
    ```
- Marker `A`
  - Decoded: `KLink;new_macro`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    KLink;new_macro
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `KL macro;KLink macro;macros,;macros, KLink`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    KL macro;KLink macro;macros,;macros, KLink
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
KLink macro

KLink("keyword[; keyword]"[, type[, "topic-ID"[, window-name]]])

Searches for keywords specified by K-footnotes.

Parameter	Description

keyword	Specifies one or more keywords to search for. Separate multiple keywords using a semicolon. If any keyword contains a comma, enclose the entire keyword string in quotation marks.

type	Specifies the action to perform if one or more keywords is found. If this parameter is not specified or is zero, the default action is to display the Topics Found dialog box containing the topic title. This parameter can be one or more of the following values, separated by spaces.

Value	Meaning

JUMP (1)	Specifies that if only one topic matches any of the keywords, WinHelp should jump directly to that topic.

TITLE (2)	Specifies that if a keyword is found in more than one Help file, WinHelp should display the title of the Help file (as specified in the contents [.cnt] file) beside the topic title in the Topics Found dialog box.

TEST (4)	Specifies that the macro should return a value indicating whether or not there is at least one match. The TestKLink macro is converted by Help Workshop into an KLink macro with this parameter.

topic-ID	Specifies the topic to display in a pop-up window if no matches are found. If this parameter is not specified, WinHelp displays a message box with the text “No additional information is available”. To specify a topic in a different Help file, the topic ID should end with an ‘@’ character and the name of the Help file.

window-name	Specifies the window type in which to display the topic. If this parameter is not specified, the window type that is specified for a topic (if one is defined) is used, or the default or current window is used. If this macro results in an interfile jump, the window type must be defined in the project file for the Help file that is being jumped to.

Comments

The KLink macro searches for K-keyword matches in the current Help file. If the Help file is associated with a contents file, WinHelp searches all Help files specified in the contents file (by the :Index and :Link commands) for matching K-keywords.

The KLink macro is identical to the ALink macro except that it searches for K-keywords instead of A-keywords.

Help Workshop automatically converts the KLink macro to KL, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

The KLink macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

Examples

The following example generates a list of topics that contain the “network printing” or “local printing” keywords in K-footnotes:

	KLink(network printing; local printing)

The following example generates a list of topics that contain the keyword “how_to” in K-footnotes and displays those topics in the Steps window:

	KLink(how_to, , , Steps)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;ALink;WINDOWS")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;ALink;WINDOWS")
```

\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 KLink(\plain\f1\fs16 "keyword[\b ;\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0keyword]"[\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0type[\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0"topic-ID"[\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0window-name]]]\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Searches for keywords specified by K-footnotes.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1445\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1445\tx1575 keyword\tab Specifies one or more keywords to search for. Separate multiple keywords using a semicolon. If any keyword contains a comma, enclose the entire keyword string in quotation marks.
\par type\tab Specifies the action to perform if one or more keywords is found. If this parameter is not specified or is zero, the default action is to display the Topics Found dialog box containing the topic title. This parameter can be one or more of the following values, separated by spaces.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li2875\ri125\fi-1295\brdrb\brdrs\tx2875\tx3145 \b Value\tab Meaning
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2875\ri125\fi-1295\tx2875\tx3145 \plain\f1\fs16 JUMP (1)\b \tab \plain\f1\fs16 Specifies that if only one topic matches any of the keywords, WinHelp should jump directly to that topic.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2875\ri125\fi-1295\tx1575\tx2875 TITLE (2)\tab Specifies that if a keyword is found in more than one Help file, WinHelp should display the title of the Help file (as specified in the contents [.cnt] file) beside the topic title in the Topics Found dialog box.
\par TEST (4)\tab Specifies that the macro should return a value indicating whether or not there is at least one match. The TestKLink macro is converted by Help Workshop into an KLink macro with this parameter.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1445\tx1575\tx5035 topic-ID\tab Specifies the topic to display in a pop-up window if no matches are found. If this parameter is not specified, WinHelp displays a message box with the text \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 No additional information is available\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1 . To specify a topic in a different Help file, the topic ID should end with an \plain\f0\fs16 \'91\f1 @\plain\f0\fs16 \'92\f1  character and the name of the Help file.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1445\tx1575\tx2965 window-name\tab Specifies the window type in which to display the topic. If this parameter is not specified, the window type that is specified for a topic (if one is defined) is used, or the default or current window is used. If this macro results in an interfile jump, the window type must be defined in the project file for the Help file that is being jumped to.

If the Help file is associated with a contents file, WinHelp searches all Help files specified in the contents file (by the :Index and :Link commands) for matching K-keywords.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575\tx2965 The KLink macro is identical to the ALink macro except that it searches for K-keywords instead of A-keywords.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575\tx2965 Help Workshop automatically converts the KLink macro to KL, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

  - Raw:

### Decoded Body

```text
Menu macro

Menu()

Displays the context menuJNVVI2 for the current Help window.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

Comments

Unlike the FloatingMenu macro, the Menu macro is carried out the moment it is encountered. If the Menu macro is used in a macro string, it may be carried out before any macros that precede it.

If the current window has a Menu button, the menu will be displayed relative to that button. Otherwise, it will be displayed relative to the mouse position.

Help Workshop automatically converts the Menu macro to MU, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

The Menu macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Menu macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 Menu()\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Displays the \ul context menu\plain\f1\fs16 {\v JNVVI2} for the current Help window.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Parameters\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 This macro does not take any parameters.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 Unlike the FloatingMenu macro, the Menu macro is carried out the moment it is encountered. If the Menu macro is used in a macro string, it may be carried out before any macros that precede it.
\par If the current window has a Menu button, the menu will be displayed relative to that button. Otherwise, it will be displayed relative to the mouse position.
\par Help Workshop automatically converts the Menu macro to MU, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.
\par The Menu macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb25\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all")}
\par
```

MPrintHash macro

MPrintHash(hash-code)

Prints a topic identified by a hash number.OYZM9 This macro must be used in conjunction with the InitMPrint and EndMPrint macros.

Parameter	Description

hash-code	Specifies the hash number of the topic to be printed.

Comments

The MPrintHash macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

You can use the Report command in Help Workshop to list the hash numbers of all the topics in a Help file. This enables you to display information about a Help file for which you have no topic, project, or contents files.

{button ,AL("macro_all;MPrintID;Print;EndMPrint;InitMPrint")} Related Topics%!AL("macro_all;MPrintID;Print;EndMPrint;InitMPrint")
```

### Decoded Body

```text
Not macro

Not("marker"/macro)

Returns zero (FALSE) if the marker text specified by the SaveMark macro exists or non-zero (TRUE) if the marker text does not exist. When used with a macro, the Not macro reverses the results of the macro.

Parameter	Description

marker/macro	Specifies either a text marker that was previously created by the SaveMark macro or a macro that returns a Boolean value. If a marker is used, it must be enclosed in quotation marks. If a macro is used, it must not be enclosed in quotation marks (so Help Workshop can distinguish between a marker and a macro).

Comment

The Not macro is used with the macros IfThen and IfThenElse.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IsNotMark;ifthen;ifthenelse ")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IsNotMark;ifthen;ifthenelse")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Not macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 Not("\plain\f1\fs16 marker\b "\plain\f1\fs16 /macro\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Returns zero (FALSE) if the marker text specified by the SaveMark macro exists or non-zero (TRUE) if the marker text does not exist. When used with a macro, the Not macro reverses the results of the macro.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 marker/macro\tab Specifies either a text marker that was previously created by the SaveMark macro or a macro that returns a Boolean value. If a marker is used, it must be enclosed in quotation marks. If a macro is used, it must \b not\plain\f1\fs16  be enclosed in quotation marks (so Help Workshop can distinguish between a marker and a macro).
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comment
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The Not macro is used with the macros IfThen and IfThenElse.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb25\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IsNotMark;ifthen;ifthenelse ")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IsNotMark;ifthen;ifthenelse")}
\par
```

### Decoded Body

```text
PopupContext macro

PopupContext([filename,] context-number)

Displays in a pop-up window the topic identified by a context number.2O07FH3

Parameter	Description

filename	Specifies the name of the destination Help file for the jump. Use this optional parameter if the pop-up is not in the current Help file.

context-number	Specifies the context number of the topic to be displayed. The context number must be specified in the [MAP] section of the project file.

Comment

Help Workshop automatically converts the PopupContext macro to PC.

Example

The following macro displays in a pop-up window the topic mapped to the context number 801 in the Progman.hlp file:

	PopupContext(801)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;JumpContext;JumpHash;JumpId;JumpKeyword;PopupHash;PopupId;MAP;CONTENTS_hpj")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;JumpContext;JumpHash;JumpId;JumpKeyword;PopupHash;PopupId;MAP;CONTENTS_hpj")

### Decoded Body

```text
PopupHash macro

PopupHash([filename,] hash-code)

Displays in a pop-up window the topic identified by a hash number.OYZM9

Parameter	Description

filename	Specifies the name of the destination Help file for the jump. Use this optional parameter if the pop-up is not in the current Help file.

hash-code	Specifies the hash number of the topic to be displayed.

Comments

You can use the Report command in Help Workshop to list the hash numbers of all the topics in a Help file. This enables you to display information about a Help file for which you have no topic, project, or contents files.

The PopupHash macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

Example

The following macro displays this topic in a pop-up window:

	PopupHash(hcw, 803830446)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;JumpHash;PopupContext;PopupId;REPORT")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_LINK;macro_all;JumpHash;PopupContext;PopupId;REPORT")
```

PopupId macro

PopupId([filename,] topic-ID)

Parameter	Description

filename	Specifies the name of the Help file containing the topic ID. Use this optional parameter if the topic is not in the current Help file.

topic-ID	Specifies the ID of the topic to display.

Comment

Help Workshop automatically converts the PopupId macro to PI.

Example

The following macro displays a topic whose ID is "another_topic" in a pop-up window:

	PopupId(another_topic)

### Decoded Body

```text
PositionWindow macro

PositionWindow(x, y, width, height, display-state, window-name)

Sets the size and position of a Help window.

Parameter	Description

x	Specifies the x-coordinate, in Help units, of the upper left corner of the window. WinHelp assumes the screen is 1024 Help units wide (regardless of resolution). For example, if the x-coordinate is 512, the left edge of the Help window is in the middle of the screen.

y	Specifies the y-coordinate, in Help units, of the upper left corner of the window. WinHelp assumes the screen is 1024 Help units high (regardless of resolution). For example, if the y-coordinate is 512, the top edge of the Help window is in the middle of the screen.

width	Specifies the default width, in Help units, of the window.

height	Specifies the default height, in Help units, of the window.

display-state	Specifies how the window is sized. This parameter can be one of the following values:

Value	Meaning

SW_HIDE	Hides the window.

SW_MINIMIZE	Minimizes the window.

SW_RESTORE	Restores the window to its original size and position if the window is minimized or maximized.

SW_SHOW	Activates the window and displays it in its current size and position.

SW_SHOWMAXIMIZED	Activates the window and maximizes it.

SW_SHOWMINIMIZED	Activates the window and displays it minimized.

SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE	Displays the window minimized, but WinHelp keeps the focus.

SW_SHOWNA	Displays a window in its current state, but WinHelp keeps the focus. (If the window was minimized before this call, it will stay minimized.)

SW_SHOWNOACTIVATE	Displays a window in its most recent size and position, but WinHelp keeps the focus.

SW_SHOWNORMAL	Activates and displays the window. If the window is minimized or maximized, Windows restores it to its original size and position (same as SW_RESTORE).

window-name	Specifies the name of the window to position. The name "main" is reserved for the main Help window. For secondary windows the window name must be defined in the [WINDOWS] section of the project file.

Comments

If the window to be positioned does not exist, WinHelp ignores the macro.

Help Workshop automatically converts the PositionWindow macro to PW.

Example

The following macro positions the secondary window "Samples" in the upper left corner (100, 100) with a width and height of 500 (in Help units):

	PositionWindow(100, 100, 500, 500, NORMAL, "Samples")

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all;WINDOWS",0,`main')} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all;WINDOWS",0,`main')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 PositionWindow macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 PositionWindow(\plain\f1\fs16 x\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0y\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0width\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0height\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0display-state\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0window-name\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Sets the size and position of a Help window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 x\tab Specifies the x-coordinate, in Help units, of the upper left corner of the window. WinHelp assumes the screen is 1024 Help units wide (regardless of resolution). For example, if the x-coordinate is 512, the left edge of the Help window is in the middle of the screen.
\par y\tab Specifies the y-coordinate, in Help units, of the upper left corner of the window. WinHelp assumes the screen is 1024 Help units high (regardless of resolution). For example, if the y-coordinate is 512, the top edge of the Help window is in the middle of the screen.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 width\tab Specifies the default width, in Help units, of the window.
\par height\tab Specifies the default height, in Help units, of the window.
\par display-state\tab Specifies how the window is sized. This parameter can be one of the following values:
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li3775\ri125\fi-2155\brdrb\brdrs\tx3775 \b Value\tab Meaning
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li3775\ri125\fi-2155\tx3775 \plain\f1\fs16 SW_HIDE\tab Hides the window.
\par SW_MINIMIZE\tab Minimizes the window.
\par SW_RESTORE\tab Restores the window to its original size and position if the window is minimized or maximized.
\par SW_SHOW\tab Activates the window and displays it in its current size and position.
\par SW_SHOWMAXIMIZED\tab Activates the window and maximizes it.
\par SW_SHOWMINIMIZED\tab Activates the window and displays it minimized.
\par SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE\tab Displays the window minimized, but WinHelp keeps the focus.
\par SW_SHOWNA\tab Displays a window in its current state, but WinHelp keeps the focus. (If the window was minimized before this call, it will stay minimized.)
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li3775\ri125\fi-2155\tx3775 SW_SHOWNOACTIVATE\tab Displays a window in its most recent size and position, but WinHelp keeps the focus.
\par SW_SHOWNORMAL\tab Activates and displays the window. If the window is minimized or maximized, Windows restores it to its original size and position (same as SW_RESTORE).
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 window-name\tab Specifies the name of the window to position. The name "main" is reserved for the main Help window. For secondary windows the window name must be defined in the [WINDOWS] section of the project file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 If the window to be positioned does not exist, WinHelp ignores the macro.

\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab PositionWindow(100, 100, 500, 500, NORMAL, "Samples")

    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `DLLs;DLLs, registering functions within;functions, registering within DLL;macros,;macros, RegisterRoutine;RegisterRoutine macro;RR macro`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    DLLs;DLLs, registering functions within;functions, registering within DLL;macros,;macros, RegisterRoutine;RegisterRoutine macro;RR macro
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
RegisterRoutine macro

RegisterRoutine(DLL-name, function-name, format-spec)

Registers a function within a dynamic-link library. Once registered, the function can be used the same as any WinHelp macro.

Parameter	Description

DLL-name	Specifies the filename of the DLL. If WinHelp cannot find the library, it displays an error message.

function-name	Specifies the name of the function to run in the designated DLL.

format-spec	Specifies a string indicating the formats of parameters passed to the function. The format string must be enclosed in quotation marks. Characters in the string represent C parameter types:

Character	Description

U or u	unsigned number

I or i	signed number

S or s	string

v	type unknown

=	The character previous to this one is the return type of the parameter.

Comments

It is recommended that you include the .dll filename extension when specifying the name of the DLL. Without the extension, WinHelp first looks through all its various folders trying to find the file exactly as specified. Only after this fails does it add the .dll extension and look through all the search paths again.

Functions in 32-bit DLLs must use the _stdcall calling convention. Functions in 16-bit DLLs (which are not available when running Windows NT) must use the _pascal calling convention.

Help Workshop automatically converts the RegisterRoutine macro to RR.

Example

The following macro registers the Windows MessageBox function:

	[CONFIG]

RegisterRoutine("user32.dll", "MessageBox", "USSU")

Having registered this function, you could display a message box with a call similar to the following:

	MessageBox(hwndContext, “This is my message”, “Help Message”, 0)

{button ,KL("macros, syntax;MessageBox")} Related Topics%!KL("macros, syntax;MessageBox")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 RegisterRoutine macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 RegisterRoutine(\plain\f1\fs16 DLL-name\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0function-name\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0format-spec\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Registers a function within a dynamic-link library. Once registered, the function can be used the same as any WinHelp macro.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1715\ri125\fi-1595\brdrb\brdrs\tx1715 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1715\ri125\fi-1595\tx1715 DLL-name\tab Specifies the filename of the DLL. If WinHelp cannot find the library, it displays an error message.
\par function-name\tab Specifies the name of the function to run in the designated DLL.
\par format-spec\tab Specifies a string indicating the formats of parameters passed to the function. The format string must be enclosed in quotation marks. Characters in the string represent C parameter types:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li3075\ri125\fi-1365\brdrb\brdrs\tx3085 \b Character\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li3075\ri125\fi-1365\tx3085 U or u\tab unsigned number
\par I or i\tab signed number
\par S or s\tab string
\par v\tab type unknown\b 
\par \plain\f1\fs16 =\b \tab \plain\f1\fs16 The character previous to this one is the return type of the parameter.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx3085 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx3085 \plain\f1\fs16 It is recommended that you include the .dll filename extension when specifying the name of the DLL. Without the extension, WinHelp first looks through all its various folders trying to find the file exactly as specified. Only after this fails does it add the .dll extension and look through all the search paths again.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx3085 Functions in 32-bit DLLs must use the _stdcall calling convention. Functions in 16-bit DLLs (which are not available when running Windows\'a0NT) must use the _pascal calling convention.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx3085 Help Workshop automatically converts the RegisterRoutine macro to RR.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx3085 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx3085 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro registers the Windows MessageBox function:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab [CONFIG]\line
RegisterRoutine("user32.dll", "MessageBox", "USSU")
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 Having registered this function, you could display a message box with a call similar to the following:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab MessageBox(hwndContext, \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 This is my message\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1 , \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 Help Message\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1 , 0)
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295

### Decoded Body

```text
RemoveAccelerator macro

RemoveAccelerator(key, shift-state)

Removes the assignment of a macro to an accelerator keyABYXEJ or key combination.

Parameter	Description

key	Specifies the Windows virtual-key value. This can be a numeric digit, a quoted character (as in `A'), or one of the strings listed in the AddAccelerator macro.

shift-state	Specifies the combination of ALT, SHIFT, and CTRL keys to be used with the accelerator. This parameter may be one of the following values:

NONE (0)

shift (1)

ctrl (2)

shift+ctrl (3)

alt (4)

alt+shift (5)

alt+ctrl (6)

shift+alt+ctrl (7)

Comments

An error does not occur if this macro is used with an accelerator for which no macro was defined.

Help Workshop automatically converts the RemoveAccelerator macro to RA.

Example

The following macro disassociates a macro from the alt+shift+ctrl+f9 key combination:

	RemoveAccelerator(VK_F9, SHIFT+ALT+CTRL)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_KEYBOARD;macro_all;AddAccelerator")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_KEYBOARD;macro_all;AddAccelerator")
```

\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 RemoveAccelerator(\plain\f1\fs16 key\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0shift-state\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Removes the assignment of a macro to an \ul accelerator key\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ABYXEJ} or key combination.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 key\tab Specifies the Windows virtual-key value. This can be a numeric digit, a quoted character (as in `A'), or one of the strings listed in the AddAccelerator macro.
\par shift-state\tab Specifies the combination of ALT, SHIFT, and CTRL keys to be used with the accelerator. This parameter may be one of the following values:
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2515\fi-935\tx2515\tx2555 NONE (0)
\par \scaps shift (1)
\par ctrl (2)
\par shift+ctrl (3)
\par alt (4)
\par alt+shift (5)
\par alt+ctrl (6)
\par shift+alt+ctrl (7)
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2515\tx2555 \plain\f1\b\fs16 Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2515\tx2555 \plain\f1\fs16 An error does not occur if this macro is used with an accelerator for which no macro was defined.

ResetMenu macro

ResetMenu()

Deletes all added menus and menu items, restores and enables all standard menu items, and restores the item bindings of all standard menu items to their defaults.

Parameters

This macro does not take any parameters.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all ")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_MENU;macro_all ")

### Decoded Body

```text
SaveMark macro

SaveMark(marker-text)

Saves the location of the currently displayed topic and the window it is displayed in and associates a text marker with that information. You can then jump to the topic in that window by using the GoToMark macro.

Parameter	Description

marker-text	Specifies the text marker that identifies the topic location. If the same text is used for more than one marker, the most recently entered marker is used.

Comments

If the marker-text was already used, the SaveMark macro replaces the information associated with the previously saved marker-text.

When the user quits WinHelp, all text markers are deleted.

The SaveMark macro is carried out as soon as it is encountered. If you use it in a macro string, it may be carried out before any macros that precede it.

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IfThen;IfThenElse;DeleteMark;GotoMark;IsMark;IsNotMark;Not")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IfThen;IfThenElse;DeleteMark;GotoMark;IsMark;IsNotMark;Not")
```

### Raw RTF Body

\b\fs24 SaveMark macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 SaveMark(\plain\f1\fs16 marker-text\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Saves the location of the currently displayed topic and the window it is displayed in and associates a text marker with that information. You can then jump to the topic in that window by using the GoToMark macro.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 marker-text\tab Specifies the text marker that identifies the topic location. If the same text is used for more than one marker, the most recently entered marker is used.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 If the marker-text was already used, the SaveMark macro replaces the information associated with the previously saved marker-text.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 When the user quits WinHelp, all text markers are deleted.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 The SaveMark macro is carried out as soon as it is encountered. If you use it in a macro string, it may be carried out before any macros that precede it.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IfThen;IfThenElse;DeleteMark;GotoMark;IsMark;IsNotMark;Not")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("MACRO_REF_TEXT_MARKER;macro_all;IfThen;IfThenElse;DeleteMark;GotoMark;IsMark;IsNotMark;Not")}

### Decoded Body

```text
SetContents macro

SetContents(filename, context-number)

Designates a specific topic as the contents (default) topic in the specified Help file.

Parameter	Description

filename	Specifies the name of the Help file that contains the contents (default) topic. If WinHelp cannot find this file, it displays an error message and does not carry out the jump.

context number	Specifies the context number2O07FH3 of the topic in the specified file. The context number must be defined in the [MAP] section of the project file. If the context number is not valid, WinHelp displays an error message.

Example

The following example sets the topic mapped to the context number 801 in the Progman.hlp file as the default topic.32JI7C9 After running this macro, any unsuccessful jump causes the topic specified by the context-number parameter to appear:

	SetContents(PROGMAN.HLP, 801)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;Contents;JumpContents;CONTENTS_hpj;MAP")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_BUTTON;macro_all;Contents;JumpContents;CONTENTS_hpj;MAP")
```

### Raw RTF Body

\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 SetContents macro
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \fs16 SetContents(\plain\f1\fs16 filename\b ,\plain\f1\fs16 \'a0context-number\b )\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Designates a specific topic as the contents (default) topic in the specified Help file.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1445\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16

\par context number\tab Specifies the \ul context number\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2O07FH3} of the topic in the specified file. The context number must be defined in the [MAP] section of the project file. If the context number is not valid, WinHelp displays an error message.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example sets the topic mapped to the context number 801 in the Progman.hlp file as the \ul default topic.\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 32JI7C9} After running this macro, any unsuccessful jump causes the topic specified by the context-number parameter to appear:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \tab SetContents(PROGMAN.HLP, 801)
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295

### Decoded Body

```text
SetPopupColor macro

SetPopupColor(r, g, b)

Sets the background color for all subsequent pop-up windows.

Parameter	Description

r	Specifies the red component of the color. This value is an integer in the range 0 to 255.

g	Specifies the green component of the color. This value is an integer in the range 0 to 255.

b	Specifies the blue component of the color. This value is an integer in the range 0 to 255.

Comments

After this macro is run, the set color applies to all pop-up topics.

Help Workshop automatically converts the SetPopupColor macro to SPC, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

The SetPopupColor macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

Example

The following macro sets the background color for pop-up windows to blue:

	SetPopupColor(0, 0, 255)

{button ,AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all")} Related Topics%!AL("MACRO_REF_WINDOW;macro_all")
```

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Sets the background color for all subsequent pop-up windows.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\brdrb\brdrs\tx1575 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1575\ri125\fi-1455\tx1575 r\tab Specifies the red component of the color. This value is an integer in the range 0 to 255.
\par g\tab Specifies the green component of the color. This value is an integer in the range 0 to 255.
\par b\tab Specifies the blue component of the color. This value is an integer in the range 0 to 255.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 After this macro is run, the set color applies to all pop-up topics.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 Help Workshop automatically converts the SetPopupColor macro to SPC, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 The SetPopupColor macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1575 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macro sets the background color for pop-up windows to blue:

### Decoded Body

```text
ShellExecute macro

ShellExecute(filename, [options[, show-flag[, operation[, path[, topic-id]]]]])

Opens or prints the specified file.

Parameter	Description

filename	Specifies the name of the file to open.

options	Specifies parameters passed to the program when the filename parameter specifies an executable (.exe) file. If the filename parameter specifies a document file, this parameter is empty.

show-flag	Specifies how the program is shown when it is opened. If the filename parameter specifies a document file, this parameter should be zero.

Value	Meaning

HIDE (0)	Hides the window.

MAXIMIZE (3)	Activates the window and maximizes it.

MINIMIZE_ACTIVATE (6)	Activates the window and minimizes it.

MINIMIZE_NOACTIVE (7)	Minimizes the window, but WinHelp keeps the focus.

NOACTIVATE (4)	Displays a window in its current state, but WinHelp keeps the focus. (If the window was minimized before this call, it stays minimized.)

NORMAL (5)	Activates the window and displays it in its current size and position.

RESTORE (9)	Restores the window to its original size and position if the window is minimized or maximized.

operation	Specifies the operation to perform. This parameter can be "open" or "opencpl" or "print". If this parameter is not specified, "open" is the default value.

path	Specifies the default folder. Use “” if you do not need to specify a default folder.

topic-id	Specifies the ID of the topic to display if this macro fails.

Comments

The filename can be a document file or an executable file. If it is a document file, this function opens or prints it, depending on the value of the operation parameter. If it is an executable file, this function opens it, even if the operation parameter specifies print.

If no path is specified with the filename, WinHelp searches the following locations in the order listed:

·	The folder in which the Winhelp.exe program is located

·	The folder that contains the current Help file

·	The System subfolder in the Windows folder

·	The Windows folder

·	The PATH environment

·	The registry

The ShellExecute macro calls the Windows ShellExecute function.

Help Workshop automatically converts the ShellExecute macro to SE, which is the only form of this macro that WinHelp can use.

The ShellExecute macro is new for WinHelp version 4.0.

{button ,KL("ExecFile macro;ControlPanel macro;macros, syntax;ShellExecute")} Related Topics%!KL("ExecFile macro;ControlPanel macro;macros, syntax;ShellExecute")
```

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`

    ```
- Marker `>`

    ```
- Marker `A`

    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `macros,;macros, ShortCut;ShortCut macro`
  - Raw:
