\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 A table consists of a grid of cells in columns and rows. Each cell has an explicitly defined right edge; the position of a cell's left edge is the same as the position of the right edge of the adjacent cell. For the left-most cell in a row, the left edge position is equal to the Help window's left margin position. Each cell has a left and right margin between which WinHelp aligns and wraps text. By default, the margin positions are equal to the left and right edges. The \b \'5ctrgaph\plain\f1\fs16  and \b \'5ctrleft\plain\f1\fs16  statements can be used to set different margins for all cells in a row. 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a three-column table having two rows. The positions of the right edges of the three cells are 2, 4, and 6 inches, respectively:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5ccellx2880\'5ccellx5760\'5ccellx8640\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 1 Cell 1\'5ccell\line
Row 1 Cell 2\'5ccell\line
Row 1 Cell 3\'5ccell\line
\'5crow\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 2 Cell 1\'5ccell\line
Row 2 Cell 2\'5ccell\line
Row 2 Cell 3\'5ccell\line
\'5crow \'5cpard
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ccell\plain\f1\fs16 {\v cell_rtf}, \uldb \'5cintbl\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 11SVOAI}, \uldb \'5crow\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 25GX_PL}, \uldb \'5ctrgaph\plain\f1\fs16 {\v LLG5.9}, \uldb \'5ctrleft\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2N4IEVF}, \uldb \'5ctrowd\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 3H85SN}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ccb
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5ccb\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ccb\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the background color. The new color applies to all subsequent text up to the next \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5ccb\plain\f1\fs16  statement. 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the color number to set as background. The number must be an integer number in the range 1 to the maximum number of colors specified in the color table for the Help file. If an invalid color number is specified, WinHelp uses the default background color.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 No \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5ccb\plain\f1\fs16  statement is required if the \b \'5ccb\plain\f1\fs16  statement and subsequent text are enclosed in braces. Braces limit the scope of a character property statement to the enclosed text only.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 If the \b \'5ccb\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the default background color is the text color set by Control Panel.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a light gray background color:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \{\-\'5ccolortbl;\'5cred128\'5cgreen128\'5cblue128;\'7d\line
\{\-\'5ccb1 This background is light gray.\'7d
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ccf\plain\f1\fs16 {\v CF_RTF}, \uldb \'5ccolortbl\plain\f1\fs16 {\v IG1A_U}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ccf
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5ccf\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ccf\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the foreground color. The new color applies to all subsequent text up to the next \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5ccf\plain\f1\fs16  statement. 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the color number to set as foreground. The number must be an integer number in the range 1 to the maximum number of colors specified in the color table for the Help file. If an invalid color number is specified, WinHelp uses the default foreground color.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 No \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5ccf\plain\f1\fs16  statement is required if the \b \'5ccf\plain\f1\fs16  statement and subsequent text are enclosed in braces. Braces limit the scope of a character property statement to the enclosed text only.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 If the \b \'5ccf\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the default foreground color is the text color set by Control Panel.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example displays green text:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \{\-\'5ccolortbl;\'5cred0\'5cgreen255\'5cblue0;\'7d\line
\{\-\'5ccf1 This text is green.\'7d
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ccb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v CB_RTF}, \uldb \'5ccolortbl\plain\f1\fs16 {\v IG1A_U}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cchftn
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5cchftn\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cchftn\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the footnote reference character for subsequent \b \'5cfootnote\plain\f1\fs16  statements.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 Help Workshop ignores this statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the footnote reference character.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cfootnote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 6M1HH_}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cclmgf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cclmgf\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the first cell in a range of cells to be merged.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 Help Workshop ignores this statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 All cells between the \b \'5cclmgf\plain\f1\fs16  statement and a subsequent \b \'5cclmrg\plain\f1\fs16  statement are combined into a single cell. The left edge of the new cell is the same as that of the left-most cell to be merged; the right edge is the same as that of the rightmost cell.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cclmrg\plain\f1\fs16 {\v LBBY8O}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cclmrg
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cclmrg\plain\f1\fs16  statement merges the current cell with the preceding cell.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 Help Workshop ignores this statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 All cells between the \b \'5cclmgf\plain\f1\fs16  statement and a subsequent \b \'5cclmrg\plain\f1\fs16  statement are combined into a single cell. The left edge of the new cell is the same as that of the left-most cell to be merged; the right edge is the same as that of the rightmost cell.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cclmgf\plain\f1\fs16 {\v AABY8O}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ccolortbl
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \{\-\'5ccolortbl\'5cred\plain\f1\fs16 r\b \'5cgreen\plain\f1\fs16 g\b \'5cblue\plain\f1\fs16 b\b ;\'7d
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ccolortbl\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates a color table for the Help file. The color table consists of one or more color definitions. Each color definition consists of one \b \'5cred\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cgreen\plain\f1\fs16 , and \b \'5cblue\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifying the amount of primary color to use to generate the final color. Each color definition must end with a semicolon (;).
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 r\tab Specifies the intensity of red in the color. It must be an integer in the range 0 through 255.
\par g\tab Specifies the intensity of green in the color. It must be an integer in the range 0 through 255.
\par b\tab Specifies the intensity of blue in the color. It must be an integer in the range 0 through 255.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 Color definitions are implicitly numbered starting at zero. A color definition's implicit number can be used in the \b \'5ccf\plain\f1\fs16  statement to set the foreground color.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 The default colors are the window text and window background colors set by Control Panel. To override the default colors, a \b \'5ccolortbl\plain\f1\fs16  statement and \b \'5ccf\plain\f1\fs16  and \b \'5ccb\plain\f1\fs16  statements must be given.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a color table containing two color definitions. The first color definition is empty (only the semicolon is given), so color number 0 always represents the default color. The second definition specifies green; color number 1 can be used to display green text:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \{\-\'5ccolortbl;\'5cred0\'5cgreen255\'5cblue0;\'7d
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ccb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v CB_RTF}, \uldb \'5ccf\plain\f1\fs16 {\v CF_RTF}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cdeff
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5cdeff\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cdeff\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the default font number. Help Workshop uses the number to set the default font whenever a \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  statement is given or an invalid font number is given in a \b \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the number of the font to be used as the default font. This parameter must be a valid font number as specified by the \b \'5cfonttbl\plain\f1\fs16  statement for the Help file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5cdeff\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the default font number is zero.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16 {\v f_rtf}, \uldb \'5cfonttbl\plain\f1\fs16 {\v W50JJN}, \uldb \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4KLX0ZP}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cemdash
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cemdash
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cemdash\plain\f1\fs16  statement inserts an em dash character if either Times New Roman or Arial has been specified in the \'5cfonttable of the current rtf file. If neither font has been specified, Help Workshop substitutes a hyphen character.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cendash\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 99UDT3}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cemspace
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cemspace
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cemspace\plain\f1\fs16  statement inserts two spaces in the current font.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5censpace\plain\f1\fs16 {\v GLD2DA}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cendash
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cendash
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cendash\plain\f1\fs16  statement inserts an en dash character if either Times New Roman or Arial has been specified in the \'5cfonttable of the current rtf file. If neither font has been specified, Help Workshop substitutes a hyphen character.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cemdash\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1_S4HV.}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5censpace
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5censpace
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5censpace\plain\f1\fs16  statement inserts an a space character in Courier font using the current point size if Courier has been specified in the \'5cfonttable of the current rtf file. If Coureir has been specified, Help Workshop substitutes a space in the current font.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cemspace\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1X9ISO4}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cewc
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5c\{\-ewc \plain\f1\fs16 DLL-name, window-class, author-data\b \'5c\'7d
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b ewc\plain\f1\fs16  statement displays information (bitmap, multimedia element, and so on) under the control of a dynamic-link library in the current line of text. The statement positions the object as if it were the next character in the line, aligning it on the base line and applying the current paragraph properties.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\brdrb\brdrs\tx1435 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 DLL-name\tab Specifies the name of the DLL that controls the embedded window. The filename must not include an extension or be fully qualified, but it can include a relative path. WinHelp assumes .DLL or .EXE to be the default extension.
\par window-class\tab Specifies the name of the embedded window class as defined in the source code for the DLL.
\par author-data\tab Specifies an arbitrary string, which WinHelp passes to the embedded window when it creates the window. This string can be one or more substrings separated by any punctuation mark except a comma. The DLL is responsible for parsing this string.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 Since the \b ewc\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not a standard RTF token, Help Workshop relies on the opening and closing braces, including the backslashes (\'5c), to distinguish the statement from regular text. 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 Because the embedded window is treated as text, paragraph formatting properties assigned to the paragraph also apply to the window. Text coming before or after the window does not wrap around the window. Help adjusts the height of the line containing the embedded window to allow enough space for the embedded window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 If you use the \b \'5csl\plain\f1\fs16  statement in conjunction with an \b ewc\plain\f1\fs16  statement, don't specify a negative value. If you do, the embedded window might appear on top of the succeeding paragraph when WinHelp displays the topic.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example displays a 256-color bitmap in an embedded window within a paragraph:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5cpar\line
preceding text \'5c\{\-ewc MVBMP, ViewerBMP, ..\'5cart\'5cocean.bmp\f7 \'5c\f1 \'7d following text...\line
\'5cpar
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cewl\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 7PA6CJ}, \uldb \'5cewr\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 7VA6CJ}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cewl
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5c\{\-ewl \plain\f1\fs16 DLL-name, window-class, author-data\b \'5c\'7d
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b ewl\plain\f1\fs16  statement displays information (bitmap, multimedia element, and so on) under the control of a dynamic-link library at the left margin of the Help window. The first line of subsequent text aligns with the upper-right corner of the embedded window and subsequent lines wrap along the right edge of the window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\brdrb\brdrs\tx1435 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 DLL-name\tab Specifies the name of the DLL that controls the embedded window. The filename must not include an extension or be fully qualified, but it can include a relative path. WinHelp assumes .DLL or .EXE to be the default extension.
\par window-class\tab Specifies the name of the embedded window class as defined in the source code for the DLL.
\par author-data\tab Specifies an arbitrary string, which WinHelp passes to the embedded window when it creates the window. This string can be one or more substrings separated by any punctuation mark except a comma. The DLL is responsible for parsing this string.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 Since the \b ewl\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not a standard RTF token, Help Workshop relies on the opening and closing braces, including the backslashes (\'5c), to distinguish the statement from regular text. 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 Do not put any space characters between the \b ewl\plain\f1\fs16  statement and the paragraph text unless you want the first line of text indented from the rest of the text that wraps along the right edge of the window. To be sure that text wraps correctly around an embedded window, insert a soft carriage return at the end of each line of text.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example places an embedded window at the left margin. The subsequent paragraph wraps around the embedded window: 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5cpar\line
\'5c\{\-ewl \f7 FADE, AmfWnd, clipbrd.amf\'5c\f1 \'7d\line
The map at the left shows the easiest route to the school. \line
Although many people use Highway 125, there are fewer stops \line
and less traffic if you use Ames Road. 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cewc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ewc_rtf}, \uldb \'5cewr\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 7VA6CJ}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cewr
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5c\{\-ewr \plain\f1\fs16 DLL-name, window-class, author-data\b \'5c\'7d
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b ewr\plain\f1\fs16  statement displays information (bitmap, multimedia element, and so on.) under the control of a dynamic-link library at the right margin of the Help window. The first line of subsequent text aligns with the upper-left corner of the embedded window and subsequent lines wrap along the left edge of the window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\brdrb\brdrs\tx1435 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 DLL-name\tab Specifies the name of the DLL that controls the embedded window. The filename must not include an extension or be fully qualified, but it can include a relative path. WinHelp assumes .DLL or .EXE to be the default extension.
\par window-class\tab Specifies the name of the embedded window class as defined in the source code for the DLL.
\par author-data\tab Specifies an arbitrary string, which WinHelp passes to the embedded window when it creates the window. This string can be one or more substrings separated by any punctuation mark except a comma. The DLL is responsible for parsing this string.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 Since the \b ewr\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not a standard RTF token, Help Workshop relies on the opening and closing braces, including the backslashes (\'5c), to distinguish the statement from regular text. 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 Do not put any space characters between the \b ewr\plain\f1\fs16  statement and the paragraph text unless you want the first line of text indented from the rest of the text that wraps from the left topic margin. To be sure that text wraps correctly around an embedded window, insert a soft carriage return at the end of each line of text.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example places an embedded window at the right margin. The subsequent paragraph wraps around the embedded window: 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5cpar\line
\'5c\{\-ewl \f7 FADE, AmfWnd, clipbrd.amf\'5c\f1 \'7d\line
The map at the right shows the easiest route to the school.\line
Although many people use Highway 125, there are fewer stops\line
and less traffic if you use Ames Road.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cewc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ewc_rtf}, \uldb \'5cewl\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 7PA6CJ}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 ee
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cf
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the font. The new font applies to all subsequent text up to the next \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the font number. This parameter must be one of the integer font numbers defined in the font table for the Help file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16  statement does not set the point size of the font; use the \b \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16  statement instead.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 No \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16  statement is required if the \b \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16  statement and subsequent text are enclosed in braces. Braces limit the scope of a character property statement to just the enclosed text. 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 If the \b \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the default font is defined by the \b \'5cdeff\plain\f1\fs16  token (or is zero if no \b \'5cdeff\plain\f1\fs16  token is supplied). 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example uses the Arial font to display text:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \{\-\'5cfonttbl \{\-\'5cf0\'5cfswiss Arial;\'7d\'7d\line
\'5cpar\line
\{\-\'5cf0\line
This text illustrates the Arial font.\'7d\line
\'5cpar
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cdeff\plain\f1\fs16 {\v deff_rtf}, \uldb \'5cfonttbl\plain\f1\fs16 {\v W50JJN}, \uldb \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16 {\v FS_RTF}, \uldb \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4KLX0ZP}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cfi
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5cfi\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cfi\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the first-line indent for the paragraph. The new indent applies to the first line of each subsequent paragraph up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  statement or \b \'5cfi\plain\f1\fs16  statement. The first-line indent is always relative to the current left indent.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the indent in twips. This parameter can be either a positive or negative number.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5cfi\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the first-line indent is zero by default.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example uses the first-line indent and a tab stop to make a numbered list:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5ctx360\'5cli360\'5cfi-360\line
1\line
\'5ctab\line
Insert the disk in drive A.\line
\'5cpar\line
2\line
\'5ctab\line
Type a:setup and press the ENTER key.\line
\'5cpar\line
3\line
\'5ctab\line
Follow the instructions on the screen.\line
\'5cpar \'5cpard
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cli\plain\f1\fs16 {\v li_rtf}, \uldb \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cfield
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cfield\plain\f1\fs16  statement defines a field.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores this statement and all related field statements except the \b \'5cfldrslt\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cfldrslt\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1.ZQAI}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cfldrslt
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cfldrslt\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the most recently calculated result of a field. Help Workshop interprets the result as text and formats it using the current character and paragraph properties. 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores all field statements except the \b \'5cfldrslt\plain\f1\fs16  statement. Any text associated with other field statements is ignored.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cfield\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1FDOD4E}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cfonttbl
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5cfonttbl \{\-\'5cf\plain\f1\fs16 n\b \'5c\plain\f1\fs16 family font-name\b ;   .\'7d
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cfonttbl\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates a font table for the Help file. The font table consists of one or more font definitions. Each definition consists of a font number, a font family, and a font name.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the font number. This parameter must be an integer. This number can be used in subsequent \b \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16  statements to set the current font to the specified font. In the font table, font numbers should start at zero and increase by one for each new font definition.
\par family\tab Specifies the font family. This parameter must be one of the following.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\brdrb\brdrs\tx1265\tx2155 \b Value\tab Meaning\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-885\tx2155 \b fnil\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Unknown or default fonts (default)
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-885\tx2155 \b froman\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Roman, proportionally spaced serif fonts (for example, MS Serif and Palatino)
\par \b fswiss\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Swiss, proportionally spaced sans serif fonts (for example, Swiss)
\par \b fmodern\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Fixed-pitch serif and sans serif fonts (for example, Courier, Elite, and Pica)
\par \b fscript\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Script fonts (for example, Cursive)
\par \b fdecor\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Decorative fonts (for example, Old English and ITC Zapf Chancery)
\par \b ftech\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Technical, symbol, and mathematical fonts (for example, Symbol)
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 font-name\tab Specifies the name of the font. This parameter should specify an available Windows font.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If a font with the specified name is not available, WinHelp chooses a font from the specified family. If no font from the given family exists, WinHelp chooses a font having the same character set as specified for the Help file.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 The \b \'5cdeff\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the default font number for the Help file. The default font is set whenever the \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  statement is given.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cdeff\plain\f1\fs16 {\v deff_rtf}, \uldb \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16 {\v f_rtf}, \uldb \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16 {\v FS_RTF}, \uldb \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cfootnote
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\b \{\-\'5cfootnote\plain\f1\fs16  text\b \'7d
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cfootnote\plain\f1\fs16  statement defines topic-specific information, such as the topic's build tags, topic ID, title, browse code, keywords, and entry macros. Every topic must have at least a topic ID footnote in order to give the user access to the topic through links.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the footnote character. It can be one of the following.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\brdrb\brdrs\tx1265\tx2155 \b Value\tab Meaning\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-885\tx2155 \b *\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Specifies a build tag. Help Workshop uses build tags to determine whether to include the topic in the Help file. The text parameter can be any combination of characters but must not contain spaces. Uppercase and lowercase characters are treated as equivalent characters (case insensitive). If a topic has build-tag statements, they must be the first statements in the topic. Help Workshop checks a topic for build tags if the Help project (.HPJ) file specifies a build expression using the \b BUILD\plain\f1\fs16  option.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-885\tx2155 \b #\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Specifies a topic ID. The text parameter can be any combination of letters and digits but must not contain spaces. Uppercase and lowercase characters are treated as equivalent characters (case insensitive). The topic ID can be used with the \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16  statement in other topics to create links to this topic.
\par \b $\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Specifies a topic title. WinHelp uses the topic title to identify the topic in the Topics Found and History dialog boxes. The text parameter can be any combination of characters including spaces.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-885\tx2155 >\tab Specifies a default window type for individual topics. WinHelp uses this window definition to display the topic when it is accessed from the index.
\par \b +\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Specifies the browse-sequence identifier. WinHelp adds topics having an identifier to the browse sequence and allows users to view the topics by using the browse buttons. The text parameter can be a combination of letters and digits. WinHelp determines the order of topics in the browse sequence by sorting the identifier alphabetically. If two topics have the same identifier, WinHelp assumes that the topic that was compiled first is to be displayed first. WinHelp uses the browse sequence identifier only if the browse buttons have been enabled by using the \b BrowseButtons\plain\f1\fs16  macro.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-885\tx2155 \b K\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Specifies a keyword. WinHelp displays all keywords in the Help file in the Index property sheet and allows a user to choose a topic to view by choosing a keyword. The text parameter can be any combination of characters including spaces. If the first character is the letter K; it must be preceded with an extra space or a semicolon. More than one keyword can be given by separating the keywords with semicolons (;). A topic cannot contain keywords unless it also has a topic title.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-885\tx2155 \b A\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Specifies a keyword used for an \b ALink\plain\f1\fs16  macro. 'A' keywords do not appear in the index.
\par \b !\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Specifies a Help macro. WinHelp runs the macro when the topic is displayed. The text parameter can be any Help macro.
\par \b @\plain\f1\fs16 \tab Specifies an author-defined comment about the Help topic. This footnote is provided for authoring purposes only. Help Workshop ignores this footnote when building the Help file. The text parameter can be any combination of letters and digits, including accented characters and spaces.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\tx2155 If n is any letter (other than K or A), the footnote specifies an alternative keyword. Windows programs can search for topics having alternative keywords by using the \b HELP_MULTIKEY\plain\f1\fs16  command with the \b WinHelp\plain\f1\fs16  function.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 text\tab Specifies the build tag, topic ID, topic title, browse-sequence number, keyword, or macro associated with the footnote. This parameter depends on the footnote type as specified by the n parameter.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 A topic can have more than one build tag, topic ID, keyword, and Help-macro statement, but must not have more than one topic-title or browse-sequence-number statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 In print-based documents, the \b \'5cfootnote\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates a footnote and the footnote is anchored to the character immediately preceding the \b \'5cfootnote\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example defines a topic titled "Short Topic." The topic ID "topic1" can be used to create links to the topic. The keywords "example topic" and "short topic" appear in the Search dialog box and can be used to choose the topic for viewing. Or the user can find the topic by browsing since the topic is included in the short browse sequence. The topic also includes an entry macro that starts the Microsoft Clock program when Help displays the topic:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab $\{\-\'5cfootnote Short Topic\'7d\line
#\{\-\'5cfootnote topic1\'7d\line
K\{\-\'5cfootnote example topic;short topic\'7d\line
+\{\-\'5cfootnote short:015\'7d\line
!\{\-\'5cfootnote ExecProgram(`clock.exe', 0)\'7d\line
This topic has a title, topic ID, and two keywords.\line
\'5cpar\line
\'5cpage
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cchftn\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 13H26MY}, \uldb \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16 {\v v_rtf}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cfs
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the size of the font. The new font size applies to all subsequent text up to the next \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the size of the font, in half points.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16  statement does not set the font face; use the \b \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16  statement instead.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 No \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16  statement is required if the \b \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16  statement and subsequent text are enclosed in braces. Braces limit the scope of a character property statement to just the enclosed text.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 If the \b \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the default font size is 24.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example sets the size of the font to 10 points:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \{\-\'5cfs20 This line is in 10 point type.\'7d\line
\'5cpar
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16 {\v f_rtf}, \uldb \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4KLX0ZP}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5c'
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5c'\plain\f1\fs16 hh\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5c'\plain\f1\fs16  statement converts the specified hexadecimal number into a character value and inserts the value into the Help file. The appearance of the character when displayed depends on the character set specified for the Help file.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 hh\tab Specifies a two-digit hexadecimal value.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 Since Help Workshop does not accept character values greater than 127, the \b \'5c'\plain\f1\fs16  statement is the only method to insert such character values into the Help file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example inserts a trademark in a Help file that uses the \b \'5cwindows\plain\f1\fs16  statement to set the character set:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab ABC\'5c'99 is a trademark of the ABC Product Corporation.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cansi\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ansi_rtf}, \uldb \'5cpc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 301S_6}, \uldb \'5cpca\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 18DE.TA}, \uldb \'5cwindows\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1YTFWK}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ci
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ci\plain\f1\fs16  statement starts italic text. The statement applies to all subsequent text up to the next \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5ci0\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 No \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5ci0\plain\f1\fs16  statement is required if the \b \'5ci\plain\f1\fs16  statement and subsequent text are enclosed in braces. Braces limit the scope of a character property statement to just the enclosed text.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example sets "not" to italic:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab You must \{\-\'5ci not\'7d save the file without first setting the\line
Auto option.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v b_rtf}, \uldb \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4KLX0ZP}, \uldb \'5cscaps\plain\f1\fs16 {\v AIIXHD}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cintbl
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cintbl\plain\f1\fs16  statement marks subsequent paragraphs as part of a table. The statement applies to all subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5crow\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a three-column table having two rows:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5ccellx1440\'5ccellx2880\'5ccellx4320\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 1 Column 1\'5ccell\line
Row 1 Column 2\'5ccell\line
Row 1 Column 3\'5ccell \'5crow\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 2 Column 1\'5ccell\line
Row 2 Column 2\'5ccell\line
Row 2 Column 3\'5ccell \'5crow \'5cpard
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ccell\plain\f1\fs16 {\v cell_rtf}, \uldb \'5ccellx\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 8K00FX}, \uldb \'5crow\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 25GX_PL}, \uldb \'5ctrgaph\plain\f1\fs16 {\v LLG5.9}, \uldb \'5ctrleft\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2N4IEVF}, \uldb \'5ctrowd\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 3H85SN}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 

\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ckeep
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ckeep\plain\f1\fs16  statement prevents WinHelp from wrapping text to fit the Help window. The statement applies to all subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 If the text in a paragraph exceeds the width of the Help window, WinHelp displays a horizontal scroll bar.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 In print-based documents, the \b \'5ckeep\plain\f1\fs16  statement keeps paragraphs intact.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example makes the paragraph beginning "Cardfile has two views \plain\f0\fs16 \'97\f1  Card and List" nonwrapping text:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5cpar\'5cpard\'5ckeep\line
Cardfile has two views--Card and List.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cline\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1YHDZM6}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 

\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ckeepn
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ckeepn\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates a nonscrolling region at the top of the Help window for the given topic. The \b \'5ckeepn\plain\f1\fs16  statement applies to all subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  statement. All paragraphs at the beginning of a topic that meet this criteria are placed in the nonscrolling region.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx7465 \plain\f1\fs16 If a \b \'5ckeepn\plain\f1\fs16  statement is used in a topic, it must be applied to the first paragraph in the topic (and subsequent paragraphs as needed). Help Workshop displays an error message and does not create a nonscrolling region if paragraphs are given before the \b \'5ckeepn\plain\f1\fs16  statement. Only one nonscrolling region per topic is allowed.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx7465 WinHelp formats, aligns, and wraps text in the nonscrolling region just as it does in the rest of the topic. It separates the nonscrolling region from the rest of the Help window with a horizontal bar. WinHelp sets the height of the nonscrolling region so that all paragraphs in the region can be viewed if the Help window is large enough. If the window is smaller than the nonscrolling region, the user will be unable to view the rest of the topic. For this reason, the nonscrolling region is typically reserved for a single line of text specifying the name or title of the topic.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx7465 In print-based documents, the \b \'5ckeepn\plain\f1\fs16  statement keeps the subsequent paragraph with the paragraph that follows it.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx7465 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx7465 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example places the first paragraph of the topic beginning "Cardfile has two views \plain\f0\fs16 \'97\f1  Card and List" into a nonscrolling region:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5cpar\'5cpard\'5ckeepn\line
Cardfile has two views--Card and List.\line
\'5cpar\'5cpard
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpage\plain\f1\fs16 {\v page_rtf}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cldblquote
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5cldblquote
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cldblquote\plain\f1\fs16  statement inserts a left double quotation mark if either Times New Roman or Arial has been specified in the \'5cfonttable of the current rtf file. If neither font has been specified, Help Workshop substitutes a straight double-quote character. The actual quote character used depends on the language of the help file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5clquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v D2X9GS}, \uldb \'5crdblquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 12UI60T}, \uldb \'5crquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v _.DCI4}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cli
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5cli\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cli\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the left indent for the paragraph. The indent applies to all subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cli\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the indent in twips. The value can be either positive or negative.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5cli\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the left indent is zero by default. WinHelp automatically provides a small left margin; if no indent is specified the text does not start immediately at the left edge of the Help window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 Specifying a negative left indent moves the starting point for a line of text to the left of the default left margin. If the negative indent is large enough, the start of the text may be clipped by the left edge of the Help window.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example uses the left indent and a tab stop to make a bulleted list. In this example, font number 0 is assumed to be the Symbol font:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab Use the Auto command to:\line
\'5cpar\line
\'5ctx360\'5cli360\'5cfi-360\line
\{\-\'5cf0\'5c'B7\'7d\line
\'5ctab\line
Save files automatically\line
\'5cpar\line
\{\-\'5cf0\'5c'B7\'7d\line
\'5ctab\line
Prevent overwriting existing files\line
\'5cpar\line
\{\-\'5cf0\'5c'B7\'7d\line
\'5ctab\line
Create automatic backup files\line
\'5cpar \'5cpard
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16 {\v f_rtf}i, \uldb \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}, \uldb \'5cri\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ri_rtf}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cline
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cline\plain\f1\fs16  statement breaks the current line without ending the paragraph. Subsequent text starts on the next line and is aligned and indented according to the current paragraph properties.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpar\plain\f1\fs16 {\v DL9__1}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5clquote
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5clquote
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5clquote\plain\f1\fs16  statement inserts a left single quotation mark if either Times New Roman or Arial has been specified in the \'5cfonttable of the current rtf file. If neither font has been specified, Help Workshop substitutes a left single quotation mark in the current font.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cldblquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v XC5Y79}, \uldb \'5crdblquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 12UI60T}, \uldb \'5crquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v _.DCI4}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cmac
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cmac\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the Apple Macintosh character set.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cwindows\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1YTFWK}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpage
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpage\plain\f1\fs16  statement marks the end of a topic.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 In a print-based document, the \b \'5cpage\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates a page break.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example shows a complete topic:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab $\{\-\'5cfootnote Short Topic\'7d\line
#\{\-\'5cfootnote short_topic\'7d\line
Most topics in a topic file consist of topic-title and\line
topic-ID statements followed by the topic text. Every\line
topic ends with a \{\-\'5cb \'5c\'5cpage\'7d statement.\line
\'5cpar\line
\'5cpage
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpar\plain\f1\fs16 {\v DL9__1}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpar
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpar\plain\f1\fs16  statement marks the end of a paragraph. The statement ends the current line of text and moves the current position to the left margin and down by the current line-spacing and space-after-paragraph values. 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The first line of text after a \b \'5cpar\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cpage\plain\f1\fs16 , or \b \'5csect\plain\f1\fs16  statement marks the start of a paragraph. When a paragraph starts, the current position is moved down by the current space-before-paragraph value. Subsequent text is formatted using the current text alignment, line spacing, and the left, right, and first-line indents.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example has three paragraphs:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5cql\line
This paragraph is left-aligned.\line
\'5cpar \'5cpard\line
\'5cqc\line
This paragraph is centered.\line
\'5cpar \'5cpard\line
\'5cqr\line
This paragraph is right-aligned.\line
\'5cpar
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cline\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1YHDZM6}, \uldb \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}, \uldb \'5csect\plain\f1\fs16 {\v C83L2R}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpard
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  statement restores all paragraph properties to default values.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  statement appears anywhere before the end of a paragraph (that is, before the \b \'5cpar\plain\f1\fs16  statement), the default properties apply to the entire paragraph.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 The default paragraph properties are as follows.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\brdrb\brdrs\tx1615 \b Property\tab Default
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 \plain\f1\fs16 Alignment\tab Left-aligned
\par First-line indent\tab 0
\par Left indent\tab 0
\par Right indent\tab 0
\par Space before\tab 0
\par Space after\tab 0
\par Line spacing\tab Tallest character
\par Tab stops\tab None
\par Borders\tab None
\par Border style\tab Single-width
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpar\plain\f1\fs16 {\v DL9__1}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1615 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpc
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpc\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the OEM character set (also known as code page 437).
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cwindows\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1YTFWK}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1615 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpca
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpca\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the International English character set (also known as code page 850).
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cwindows\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1YTFWK}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1615 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpich
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 \fs16 \'5cpich\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1615 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpich\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the height of the picture. This statement must be used in conjunction with a \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the height of the picture, in twips or pixels, depending on the picture type. If the picture is a metafile, the width is in twips; otherwise, the width is in pixels.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 164LMJK}, \uldb \'5cpicw\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 167LMJK}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpichgoal
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cpichgoal\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpichgoal\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the desired height of a picture. If necessary, WinHelp stretches or compresses the picture to match the requested height. This statement must be used in conjunction with a \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the desired height, in twips.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 164LMJK}, \uldb \'5cpicwgoal\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2SRZYXK}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpicscalex
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cpicscalex\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpicscalex\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the horizontal scaling value. This statement must be used in conjunction with a \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the scaling value. The parameter must be a value in the range 0 through 100, representing a percentage.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5cpicscalex\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the default scaling value is 100.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpicscaley\plain\f1\fs16 {\v J_O862}, \uldb \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 164LMJK}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpicscaley
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cpicscaley\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpicscaley\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the vertical scaling value. This statement must be used in conjunction with a \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the scaling value. The parameter must be a value in the range 0 through 100, representing a percentage.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5cpicscaley\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the default scaling value is 100.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpicscalex\plain\f1\fs16 {\v J.O862}, \uldb \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 164LMJK}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpict
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cpict \plain\f1\fs16 picture-statements picture-data\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates a picture. A picture consists of hexadecimal or binary data representing a bitmap or metafile.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1795\ri125\fi-1665\brdrb\brdrs\tx1795 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1795\ri125\fi-1665\tx1795 \plain\f1\fs16 picture-statements\tab Specifies one or more statements defining the type of picture, the dimensions of the picture, and the format of the picture data. It can be a combination of the following statements:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li3595\ri125\fi-1795\brdrb\brdrs\tx3595 \b Statement\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li3595\ri125\fi-1795\tx3595 \uldb \'5cwbitmap\plain\f1\fs16 {\v wbitmap_rtf}\tab Specifies a Windows bitmap.
\par \uldb \'5cwmetafile\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2M8H0IA}\tab Specifies a Windows metafile.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li3595\ri125\fi-1795\tx3595 \uldb \'5cpicw\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 167LMJK}\tab Specifies the picture width.
\par \uldb \'5cpich\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 15SLMJK}\tab Specifies the picture height.
\par \uldb \'5cpicwgoal\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2SRZYXK}\tab Specifies the desired picture width.
\par \uldb \'5cpichgoal\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 13QZM1O}\tab Specifies the desired picture height.
\par \uldb \'5cpicscalex\plain\f1\fs16 {\v J.O862}\tab Specifies the horizontal scaling value.
\par \uldb \'5cpicscaley\plain\f1\fs16 {\v J_O862}\tab Specifies the vertical scaling value.
\par \uldb \'5cwbmbitspixel\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4GEGV6}\tab Specifies the number of bits per pixel.
\par \uldb \'5cwbmplanes\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 43VW.NN}\tab Specifies the number of planes.
\par \uldb \'5cwbmwidthbytes\plain\f1\fs16 {\v FKSPXX}\tab Specifies the bitmap width, in bytes.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li3595\ri125\fi-1795\tx3595 \uldb \'5cbin\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 0UR47D}\tab Specifies binary picture data.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1795\ri125\fi-1665\tx1795 picture-data\tab Specifies hexadecimal or binary data representing the picture. The picture data follows the last picture statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1795 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1795 \plain\f1\fs16 If a data format is not specified, the default format is hexadecimal.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1795 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1795 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cbin\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 0UR47D}, \uldb \'5cpich\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 15SLMJK}, \uldb \'5cpichgoal\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 13QZM1O}, \uldb \'5cpicscalex\plain\f1\fs16 {\v J.O862}, \uldb \'5cpicscaley\plain\f1\fs16 {\v J_O862}, \uldb \'5cpicw\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 167LMJK}, \uldb \'5cpicwgoal\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2SRZYXK}, \uldb \'5cwbitmap\plain\f1\fs16 {\v wbitmap_rtf}, \uldb \'5cwbmbitspixel\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4GEGV6}, \uldb \'5cwbmplanes\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 43VW.NN}, \uldb \'5cwbmwidthbytes\plain\f1\fs16 {\v FKSPXX}, \uldb \'5cwmetafile\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2M8H0IA}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1795 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1795 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpicw
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1795 \fs16 \'5cpicw\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1795 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpicw\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the width of the picture. This statement must be used in conjunction with a \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the width of the picture, in twips or pixels, depending on the picture type. If the picture is a metafile, the width is in twips; otherwise, the width is in pixels.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpich\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 15SLMJK}, \uldb \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 164LMJK}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cpicwgoal
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cpicwgoal\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cpicwgoal\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the desired width of the picture, in twips. If necessary, WinHelp stretches or compresses the picture to match the requested height. This statement must be used in conjunction with a \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the desired width, in twips.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpichgoal\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 13QZM1O}, \uldb \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 164LMJK}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cplain
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  statement restores the character properties to default values.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The default character properties are as follows.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Property\tab Default
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 Bold\tab Off
\par Italic\tab Off
\par Small caps\tab Off
\par Font\tab 0
\par Font size\tab 24
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v b_rtf}, \uldb \'5cf\plain\f1\fs16 {\v f_rtf}, \uldb \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16 {\v FS_RTF}, \uldb \'5ci\plain\f1\fs16 {\v i_rtf}, \uldb \'5cscaps\plain\f1\fs16 {\v AIIXHD}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cqc
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cqc\plain\f1\fs16  statement centers text between the current left and right indents. The statement applies to subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  statement or text-alignment statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If a \b \'5cql\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cqr\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cqc\plain\f1\fs16 , or \b \'5cqj\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the text is left-aligned by default.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cqj\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4A1S_6}, \uldb \'5cql\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4C1S_6}, \uldb \'5cqr\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4I1S_6}, \uldb \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cqj
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cqj\plain\f1\fs16  statement justifies text between the current left and right indents. The statement applies to subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  statement or text-alignment statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If a \b \'5cql\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cqr\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cqc\plain\f1\fs16 , or \b \'5cqj\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the text is left-aligned by default.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cqc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v qc_rtf}, \uldb \'5cql\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4C1S_6}, \uldb \'5cqr\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4I1S_6}, \uldb \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cql
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cql\plain\f1\fs16  statement aligns text along the left indent. The statement applies to subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  statement or text-alignment statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If a \b \'5cql\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cqr\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cqc\plain\f1\fs16 , or \b \'5cqj\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the text is left-aligned by default.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cqc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v qc_rtf}, \uldb \'5cqj\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4A1S_6}, \uldb \'5cqr\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4I1S_6}, \uldb \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cqr
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cqr\plain\f1\fs16  statement aligns text along the right indent. The statement applies to subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  statement or text-alignment statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If a \b \'5cql\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cqr\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cqc\plain\f1\fs16 , or \b \'5cqj\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the text is left-aligned by default.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cqc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v qc_rtf}, \uldb \'5cqj\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4A1S_6}, \uldb \'5cql\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4C1S_6}, \uldb \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5crdblquote
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5crdblquote
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5crdblquote\plain\f1\fs16  statement inserts a right double quotation mark if either Times New Roman or Arial has been specified in the \'5cfonttable of the current rtf file. If neither font has been specified, Help Workshop substitutes a straight double-quote character. The actual quote character used depends on the language of the help file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cldblquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v XC5Y79}, \uldb \'5clquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v D2X9GS}, \uldb \'5crquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v _.DCI4}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cri
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cri\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cri\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the right indent for the paragraph. The indent applies to all subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cri\plain\f1\fs16  statement. 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the right indent, in twips. It can be a positive or negative value.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5cri\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the right indent is zero by default. WinHelp automatically provides a small right margin so that when no right indent is specified, the text does not end abruptly at the right edge of the Help window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 WinHelp never displays less than one word for each line in a paragraph even if the right indent is greater than the width of the window.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 In the following example, the right and left indents are set to one inch and the subsequent text is centered between the indents:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5cli1440\'5cri1440\'5cqc\line
Microsoft WinHelp\'5cline\line
Sample File\'5cline
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cli\plain\f1\fs16 {\v li_rtf}, \uldb \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5crow
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5crow\plain\f1\fs16  statement marks the end of a table row. The statement ends the current row and begins a new row by moving down pass the end of the longest cell in the row. The next \b \'5ccell\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the text of the left-most cell in the next row.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a table having four rows and two columns:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5ccellx2880\'5ccellx5760\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 1, Column 1\'5ccell\line
Row 1, Column 2\'5ccell \'5crow\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 2, Column 1\'5ccell\line
Row 2, Column 2\'5ccell \'5crow\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 3, Column 1\'5ccell\line
Row 3, Column 2\'5ccell \'5crow\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 4, Column 1\'5ccell\line
Row 4, Column 2\'5ccell \'5crow\line
\'5cpar \'5cpard
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ccell\plain\f1\fs16 {\v cell_rtf}, \uldb \'5ccellx\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 8K00FX}, \uldb \'5cintbl\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 11SVOAI}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5crquote
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5crquote
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5crquote\plain\f1\fs16  statement inserts a right single quotation mark if either Times New Roman or Arial has been specified in the \'5cfonttable of the current rtf file. If neither font has been specified, Help Workshop substitutes a straight single-quote character in the current font.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cldblquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v XC5Y79}, \uldb \'5clquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v D2X9GS}, \uldb \'5crdblquote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 12UI60T}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5crtf
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5crtf\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cRTF token\plain\f1\fs16  identifies the file as a rich-text format (RTF) file and specifies the version of the RTF standard used.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the version of the RTF standard used.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cRTF token\plain\f1\fs16  must follow the first open brace in the Help file. A statement specifying the character set for the file must also follow the \b \'5cRTF token\plain\f1\fs16 .
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cwindows\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1YTFWK}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5csa
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5csa\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5csa\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the amount of vertical spacing after a paragraph. The vertical space applies to all subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5csa\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the amount of vertical spacing, in twips.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5csa\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the vertical spacing after a paragraph is zero by default.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}, \uldb \'5csb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 691S_6}
