```rtf
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5csb
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5csb\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5csb\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the amount of vertical spacing before the paragraph. The vertical space applies to all subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5csb\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the amount of vertical spacing, in twips.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5csb\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the vertical spacing before the paragraph is zero by default.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}, \uldb \'5csa\plain\f1\fs16 {\v sa_rtf}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cscaps
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cscaps\plain\f1\fs16  statement starts small-capital text. The statement converts all subsequent lowercase letters to uppercase before displaying the text. This statement applies to all subsequent text up to the next \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cscaps0\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 No \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cscaps0\plain\f1\fs16  statement is required if the \b \'5cscaps\plain\f1\fs16  statement and subsequent text are enclosed in braces. Braces limit the scope of a character property statement to just the enclosed text.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 The \b \'5cscaps\plain\f1\fs16  statement does not reduce the point size of the text. To reduce point size, the \b \'5cfs\plain\f1\fs16  statement must be used.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example displays the key name \fs19 ENTER\fs16  in small capitals:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab Press the \{\-\'5cscaps ENTER\'7d key to complete the action.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4KLX0ZP}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5csect
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5csect\plain\f1\fs16  statement marks the end of a section and paragraph.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpar\plain\f1\fs16 {\v DL9__1}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5csl
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5csl\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5csl\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the amount of vertical space between lines in a paragraph. The vertical space applies to all subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5csl\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the amount of vertical spacing, in twips. If this parameter is a positive value, WinHelp uses this value if it is greater than the tallest character. Otherwise, WinHelp uses the height of the tallest character as the line spacing. If this parameter is a negative value, WinHelp uses the absolute value of the number even if the tallest character is taller.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5csl\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given or the specified amount of spacing is 1000, WinHelp automatically sets the line spacing by using the tallest character in the line.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Y4OHIO}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cstrike
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cstrike\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates a hotspot. The statement is used in conjunction with a \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16  statement to create a link to another topic. When the user chooses a hotspot, WinHelp displays the associated topic in the Help window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 The \b \'5cstrike\plain\f1\fs16  statement applies to all subsequent text up to the next \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cstrike0\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 No \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cstrike0\plain\f1\fs16  statement is required if the \b \'5cstrike\plain\f1\fs16  statement and subsequent text are enclosed in braces. Braces limit the scope of a character property statement to just the enclosed text.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 In print-based documents, or whenever it is not followed by \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16 , the \b \'5cstrike\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates strikeout text.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a hotspot for a topic. When displayed, the hotspot text, "Hotspot," is green and has a solid line under it:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \{\-\'5cstrike Hotspot\'7d\{\-\'5cv Topic\'7d
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cul\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ul_rtf}, \uldb \'5culdb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 12B7468}, \uldb \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16 {\v v_rtf}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ctab
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ctab\plain\f1\fs16  statement inserts a tab character (ASCII character code 9).
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The tab character (ASCII character 9) has the same effect as the \b \'5ctab\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ctb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 791S_6}, \uldb \'5ctqc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 473X_PL}, \uldb \'5ctqc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 473X_PL}, \uldb \'5ctx\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 7O1S_6}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ctb
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ctb\plain\f1\fs16  statement advances to the next tab stop.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ctab\plain\f1\fs16 {\v tab_rtf}, \uldb \'5ctqc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 473X_PL}, \uldb \'5ctqr\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 47BX_PL}, \uldb \'5ctx\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 7O1S_6}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ctqc
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ctqc\plain\f1\fs16  statement advances to the next tab stop and centers text.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ctab\plain\f1\fs16 {\v tab_rtf}, \uldb \'5ctb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 791S_6}, \uldb \'5ctqr\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 47BX_PL}, \uldb \'5ctx\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 7O1S_6}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ctqr
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ctqr\plain\f1\fs16  statement advances to the next tab stop and aligns text to the right.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ctab\plain\f1\fs16 {\v tab_rtf}, \uldb \'5ctb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 791S_6}, \uldb \'5ctqc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 473X_PL}, \uldb \'5ctx\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 7O1S_6}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ctrgaph
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5ctrgaph\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ctrgaph\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the amount of space between text in adjacent cells in a table. For each cell in the table, WinHelp uses the space to calculate the cell's left and right margins. It then uses the margins to align and wrap the text in the cell. WinHelp applies the same margin widths to each cell ensuring that paragraphs in adjacent cells have the specified space between them.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 The \b \'5ctrgaph\plain\f1\fs16  statement applies to cells in all subsequent rows of a table up to the next \b \'5ctrowd\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the space, in twips, between text in adjacent cells. If this parameter exceeds the actual width of the cell, the left and right margins are assumed to be at the same position in the cell.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The width of the left margin in the first cell is always equal to the space specified by this statement. The \b \'5ctrleft\plain\f1\fs16  statement is typically used to move the left margin to a position similar to the left margins in all other cells.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a three-column table with one-quarter inch space between the text in the columns:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5ctrgaph360 \'5ccellx1440\'5ccellx2880\'5ccellx4320\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 1 Column 1\'5ccell\line
Row 1 Column 2\'5ccell\line
Row 1 Column 3\'5ccell \'5crow\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 2 Column 1\'5ccell\line
Row 2 Column 2\'5ccell\line
Row 2 Column 3\'5ccell \'5crow \'5cpard
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ccell\plain\f1\fs16 {\v cell_rtf}, \uldb \'5ccellx\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 8K00FX}, \uldb \'5cintbl\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 11SVOAI}, \uldb \'5crow\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 25GX_PL}, \uldb \'5ctrleft\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2N4IEVF}, \uldb \'5ctrowd\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 3H85SN}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ctrleft
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5ctrleft\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ctrleft\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the position of the left margin for the first (left-most) cell in a row of a table. This statement applies to the first cell in all subsequent rows of the table up to the next \b \'5ctrowd\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the relative position, in twips, of the left margin. This parameter can be a positive or negative number. The final position of the left margin is the sum of the current position and this value.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a three-column table with one-quarter inch space between the text in the columns. The left margin in the first cell is flush with the left margin of the Help window:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \'5ctrgaph360\'5ctrleft-360 \'5ccellx1440\'5ccellx2880\'5ccellx4320\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 1 Column 1\'5ccell\line
Row 1 Column 2\'5ccell\line
Row 1 Column 3\'5ccell \'5crow\line
\'5cintbl\line
Row 2 Column 1\'5ccell\line
Row 2 Column 2\'5ccell\line
Row 2 Column 3\'5ccell \'5crow \'5cpard
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ccell\plain\f1\fs16 {\v cell_rtf}, \uldb \'5ccellx\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 8K00FX}, \uldb \'5cintbl\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 11SVOAI}, \uldb \'5crow\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 25GX_PL}, \uldb \'5ctrgaph\plain\f1\fs16 {\v LLG5.9}, \uldb \'5ctrowd\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 3H85SN}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ctrowd
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ctrowd\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets default margins and cell positions for subsequent rows in a table.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ccell\plain\f1\fs16 {\v cell_rtf}, \uldb \'5ccellx\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 8K00FX}, \uldb \'5cintbl\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 11SVOAI}, \uldb \'5crow\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 25GX_PL}, \uldb \'5ctrgaph\plain\f1\fs16 {\v LLG5.9}, \uldb \'5ctrleft\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2N4IEVF}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ctrqc
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ctrqc\plain\f1\fs16  statement directs WinHelp to dynamically adjust the width of table columns to fit in the current window.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 In a print-based document, the \b \'5ctrqc\plain\f1\fs16  statement centers a table row with respect to its containing column.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ctrowd\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 3H85SN}, \uldb \'5ctrql\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 12_9FE.}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ctrql
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ctrql\plain\f1\fs16  statement aligns the text in each cell of a table row to the left.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ctrowd\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 3H85SN}, \uldb \'5ctrqc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1249FE.}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5ctx
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5ctx\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5ctx\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the position of a tab stop. The position is relative to the left margin of the Help window. A tab stop applies to all subsequent paragraphs up to the next \b \'5cpard\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the tab stop position, in twips.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5ctx\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, tab stops are set at every one-half inch by default.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5ctab\plain\f1\fs16 {\v tab_rtf}, \uldb \'5ctb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 791S_6}, \uldb \'5ctqc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 473X_PL}, \uldb \'5ctqr\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 47BX_PL}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cul
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cul\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates a link to a pop-up topic. The statement is used in conjunction with a \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16  statement to create a link to another topic. When the user chooses the link, WinHelp displays the associated topic in a pop-up window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 The \b \'5cul\plain\f1\fs16  statement applies to all subsequent text up to the next \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cul0\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 No \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5cul0\plain\f1\fs16  statement is required if the \b \'5cul\plain\f1\fs16  statement and subsequent text are enclosed in braces. Braces limit the scope of a character property statement to just the enclosed text.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 In print-based documents, or whenever it is not followed by \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16 , the \b \'5cul\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates continuous underline.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a pop-up link for a topic. When displayed, the link text, "Pop-up Link," is green and has a dotted line under it:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \{\-\'5cul Pop-up Link\'7d\{\-\'5cv PopupTopic\'7d
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cstrike\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1NTK807}, \uldb \'5culdb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 12B7468}, \uldb \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16 {\v v_rtf}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5culdb
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5culdb\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates a hotspot. This statement is used in conjunction with a \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16  statement to create a link to another topic. When the user chooses a hotspot, WinHelp displays the associated topic in the Help window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 The \b \'5culdb\plain\f1\fs16  statement applies to all subsequent text up to the next \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5culdb0\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 No \b \'5cplain\plain\f1\fs16  or \b \'5culdb0\plain\f1\fs16  statement is required if the \b \'5culdb\plain\f1\fs16  statement and subsequent text are enclosed in braces. Braces limit the scope of a character property statement to just the enclosed text.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 In print-based documents, or whenever it is not followed by \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16 , the \b \'5culdb\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates double underline.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a hotspot for a topic. When displayed, the hotspot text, "Hotspot," is green and has a solid line under it:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \{\-\'5culdb Hotspot\'7d\{\-\'5cv Topic\'7d
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cstrike\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1NTK807}, \uldb \'5cul\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ul_rtf}, \uldb \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16 {\v v_rtf}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cv
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \{\-\'5cv \plain\f1\fs16 topic-ID\b \'7d
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates a link to the topic having the specified topic ID. The \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16  statement is used in conjunction with the \b \'5cstrike\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cul\plain\f1\fs16 , and \b \'5culdb\plain\f1\fs16  statements to create hotspots and links to topics.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\brdrb\brdrs\tx1435 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 topic-ID\tab Specifies the topic ID of a topic in the Help file. The string can be any combination of characters, except spaces, and must also be specified in a topic ID \b \'5cfootnote\plain\f1\fs16  statement in some topic in the Help file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 If the topic ID is preceded by a percent sign (%), WinHelp displays the associated hotspot or link without applying the standard underline and color.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 If the topic ID is preceded by an asterisk (*), WinHelp displays the associated hotspot or link with an underline but without applying the standard color.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 In print-based documents, the \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16  statement creates hidden text.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 For links or hotspots, the syntax of the \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16  statement is as follows:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li715\ri125\tx1435 [\b %\plain\f1\fs16 |\b *\plain\f1\fs16 ]context[\b >\plain\f1\fs16 secondary-window][\b @\plain\f1\fs16 filename]
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 In this syntax, secondary-window is the name of the secondary window to jump to. When the secondary window is not specified, the jump is to the same window as the current Help topic is using. To jump to the main Help window, specify "main" for this parameter. This parameter may not be used with pop-up window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 The filename parameter specifies a jump to a topic in a different Help file.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 For a macro hotspot, the syntax of the \b \'5cv\plain\f1\fs16  statement is as follows:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li715\ri125\tx1435 [\b %\plain\f1\fs16 |\b *\plain\f1\fs16 ]\b !\plain\f1\fs16 macro[\b ;\plain\f1\fs16 macro][\b ;...\plain\f1\fs16 ]
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a hotspot for the topic having the topic ID "Topic." WinHelp applies an underline and the color green to the text "Hotspot" when it displays the topic:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \{\-\'5culdb Hotspot\'7d\{\-\'5cv Topic\'7d
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cfootnote\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 6M1HH_}, \uldb \'5cstrike\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 1NTK807}, \uldb \'5cul\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ul_rtf}, \uldb \'5culdb\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 12B7468}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cwbitmap
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5cwbitmap\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cwbitmap\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the picture type to Windows bitmap. This statement must be used in conjunction with a \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the bitmap type. This parameter is zero for a logical bitmap.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cwbitmap\plain\f1\fs16  statement is optional; if a \b \'5cwmetafile\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not specified, the picture is assumed to be a Windows bitmap.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a 32-by-8-pixel monochrome bitmap:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \{\-\line
\'5cpict \'5cwbitmap0\'5cwbmbitspixel1\'5cwbmplanes1\'5cwbmwidthbytes4\'5cpicw32\'5cpich8\line
3FFFFFFC\line
F3FFFFCF\line
FF3FFCFF\line
FFF3CFFF\line
FFFC3FFF\line
FFCFF3FF\line
FCFFFF3F\line
CFFFFFF3\line
\'7d
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cbmc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4AWD1BN}, \uldb \'5cbml\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 11233NT}, \uldb \'5cbmr\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 11833NT}, \uldb \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 164LMJK}, \uldb \'5cwmetafile\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2M8H0IA}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cwbmbitspixel
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \fs16 \'5cwbmbitspixel\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cwbmbitspixel\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the number of consecutive bits in the bitmap data that represent a single pixel. This statement must be used in conjunction with the \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the number of bits per pixel.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5cwbmbitspixel\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the default bits per pixel value is 1.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 164LMJK}, \uldb \'5cwbitmap\plain\f1\fs16 {\v wbitmap_rtf}, \'5cwbmplanes
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cwbmplanes
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cwbmplanes\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cwbmplanes\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the number of color planes in the bitmap data. This statement must be used in conjunction with a \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the number of bitmap planes.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If the \b \'5cwbmplanes\plain\f1\fs16  statement is not given, the default number of planes is 1.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 164LMJK}, \uldb \'5cwbitmap\plain\f1\fs16 {\v wbitmap_rtf}, \'5cwbmbitspixel
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cwbmwidthbytes
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cwbmwidthbytes\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cwbmwidthbytes\plain\f1\fs16  statement specifies the number of bytes in each scan line of the bitmap data. This statement must be used in conjunction with the \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the width of the bitmap, in bytes.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 164LMJK}, \uldb \'5cwbitmap\plain\f1\fs16 {\v wbitmap_rtf}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cwindows
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cwindows\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the Windows character set.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 If no \b \'5cwindows\plain\f1\fs16 , \b \'5cpc\plain\f1\fs16 , or \b \'5cpca\plain\f1\fs16  statement is given in the Help file, the Windows character set is used by default.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cansi\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ansi_rtf}, \uldb \'5cpc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 301S_6}, \uldb \'5cpca\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 18DE.TA}
\par \pard\sb115\sa115\sl-115\li-1765\ri25\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \fs8\cf1 
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 
\plain\f1\b\fs20 \'5cwmetafile
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \fs16 \'5cwmetafile\plain\f1\fs16 n\b 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b \'5cwmetafile\plain\f1\fs16  statement sets the picture type to a Windows metafile. This statement must be used in conjunction with the \b \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16  statement.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\brdrb\brdrs\tx1255 \b Parameter\tab Description
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1265\ri125\fi-1135\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 n\tab Specifies the metafile type. This parameter must be 8.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp expects the hexadecimal data associated with the picture to represent a valid Windows metafile. By default, WinHelp sets the MM_ANISOTROPIC mapping mode prior to displaying the metafile. To ensure that the picture is displayed correctly, the metafile data must either set the window origin and extents by using the \b SetWindowOrg\plain\f1\fs16  and \b SetWindowExt\plain\f1\fs16  records or set another mapping mode by using the \b SetMapMode\plain\f1\fs16  record.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \b Example
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1255 \plain\f1\fs16 The following example creates a picture using a metafile:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{bmc bm4.bmp\}\tab \{\-\{\-\'5cpict\'5cwmetafile8\'5cpicw2880\'5cpich2880\line
0100090000034f0000000200090000000000\line
050000000b0200000000050000000c026400\line
6400090000001d066200ff00640064000000\line
000008000000fa0200000200000000000000\line
040000002d01000005000000140200000000\line
050000001302640064000500000014020000\line
64000500000013026400000008000000fa02\line
00000000000000000000040000002d010100\line
04000000f00100000300000000004e0dff00\line
870020000050000020000000000000000000\'7d\line
\'5cpar \'7d
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b See Also
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 \'5cbmc\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 4AWD1BN}, \uldb \'5cbml\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 11233NT}, \uldb \'5cbmr\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 11833NT}, \uldb \'5cpict\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 164LMJK}, \uldb \'5cwbitmap\plain\f1\fs16 {\v wbitmap_rtf}
\par
```

  - Raw:

    ```rtf
    converting;converting, Viewer 2.0 files to WinHelp 4.0;files,;files, converting Viewer 2.0 to WinHelp 4.0;titles;titles, Viewer 2.0, converting to WinHelp file;Viewer 2.0;Viewer 2.0, file conversions to WinHelp 4.0;WinHelp 4.0;WinHelp 4.0, conversions from Viewer 2.0
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Converting from Viewer 2.0 to WinHelp 4.0

Perform the following procedures to convert a Microsoft Viewer 2.0 title to a Microsoft Windows Help version 4.0 file:

·	Update macro references.

·	Update multimedia references.

·	Convert the Viewer project file (.MVP) to a Help Workshop project file.

·	Create a contents file.2GB40S

·	Build the new Help (.HLP) file.

Notes

·	WinHelp does not support panes, groups, or fields

·	WinHelp has its own (optional) full-text search indexer and user interface.

·	WinHelp supports the LDLLHandler callback function, but does not support other Viewer APIs.

{button ,AL("from_V2_overview")} Related Topics%!AL("from_V2_overview")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Converting from Viewer 2.0 to WinHelp 4.0
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 Perform the following procedures to convert a Microsoft Viewer 2.0 title to a Microsoft Windows Help version 4.0 file:
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab Update macro references.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab Update multimedia references.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab Convert the Viewer project file (.MVP) to a Help Workshop project file.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab Create a \ul contents file.\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2GB40S}
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab Build the new Help (.HLP) file.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Notes
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \plain\f2\fs16 \'b7\f1 \tab WinHelp does not support panes, groups, or fields
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab WinHelp has its own (optional) full-text search indexer and user interface.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab WinHelp supports the \b LDLLHandler\plain\f1\fs16  callback function, but does not support other Viewer APIs.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL("from_V2_overview")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("from_V2_overview")}
\par
```

### Decoded Body

```text
Update Macro References

Help Workshop ignores unsupported macros in the Help project file, but alerts you to their presence with notes and warning messages. One or more of the following procedures may be necessary:

·	Remove Viewer-specific macros.

·	Update macros that have a slightly different form in WinHelp.

{button ,AL("from_v2_macros")} Related Topics%!AL("from_v2_macros")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Update Macro References
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores unsupported macros in the Help project file, but alerts you to their presence with notes and warning messages. One or more of the following procedures may be necessary:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab Remove Viewer-specific macros.
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab Update macros that have a slightly different form in WinHelp.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL("from_v2_macros")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("from_v2_macros")}
\par
```

## 413. Unsupported Viewer macros

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE0027:0192`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE0027:0192
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Unsupported Viewer macros`


  - Decoded: `V2_UNSUPPORTED_VIEWER_MACROS`
  - Raw:

- Marker `>`
  - Decoded: `main`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    main
    ```
- Marker `A`
  - Decoded: `from_v2_macros;V2_UNSUPPORTED_VIEWER_MACROS`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    from_v2_macros;V2_UNSUPPORTED_VIEWER_MACROS
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `macros,;macros, Viewer 2.0 unsupported;Viewer 2.0;Viewer 2.0, macros unsupported in WinHelp 4.0`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    macros,;macros, Viewer 2.0 unsupported;Viewer 2.0;Viewer 2.0, macros unsupported in WinHelp 4.0
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Unsupported Viewer Macros

The following Viewer macros are not supported in WinHelp version 4.0:

Viewer Macro	Reason

ChangeCitation	WinHelp supports only a single citation (specified in the project (.HPJ)file.)

ClosePane	Panes are not supported in WinHelp 4.0.

CopyBmp	WinHelp cannot copy images to the Clipboard.

DeleteMenu	Menus can be added or reset in WinHelp 4.0, but not deleted.

DeleteSeparator	Use the DeleteItemDeleteItem>main macro.

FTSearch	Use the FindFind>main macro to provide access to the full-text search user interface.

HideButtonBar	The WinHelp main windowO8.Q2 button bar can be turned off in the window definition in the project file.

HideMenuBar	WinHelp secondary windows2ZK4PT do not display menus.

Highlight	WinHelp version 4.0 does not highlight the words found in full-text searches.

InsertButton	WinHelp does not support specific button positioning.

InsertSeparator	Use the ExtInsertItemExtInsertItem>main macro.

JumpAddr	WinHelp provides its own full-text search.

JumpCookie	WinHelp uses hash codesOYZM9 rather than addresses.

KeyIndex	WinHelp uses the SearchSearch>main macro to display the keyword Index tab in the Help Topics dialog box.

MasterAspect	WinHelp does not support panes.

MasterNSRColor	Colors for nonscrolling regions are part of window definitions, which are specified in the project file.

MasterSRColor	Color for scrolling regions are part of window definitions, which are specified in the project file.

MCICommand	In WinHelp, MCI support is provided by the {-mci} topic file reference.

PaneId	WinHelp does not support panes.

PositionMaster	WinHelp does not support panes.

PositionTopic	WinHelp provides macro support only for device-independent window positioning (using the PositionWindowPositionWindow>main macro).

SearchDialog	WinHelp provides a SearchSearch>main macro to display the Index tab in the Help Topics dialog box.

ShowButtonBar	The WinHelp main window button bar is configurable in the window definition in the project file.

ShowMenuBar	Menus are not configurable in WinHelp

Std20Buttons	WinHelp provides its own standard buttons for the main window.

Std20Menus	WinHelp provides its own standard menus for the main window.

SwitchToTopicsFound	WinHelp provides its own full-text search user interface, which does not include a separate Topics Found dialog box.

WindowAspect	WinHelp does not support panes.

Help Workshop ignores these unsupported macros in the Help project file, but alerts you to their presence with notes and warning messages.

{button ,AL("CITATION;Search;Finder;PositionWindow;mci")} Related Topics%!AL("CITATION;Search;Finder;PositionWindow;mci")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Unsupported Viewer Macros
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 The following Viewer macros are not supported in WinHelp version 4.0:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\brdrb\brdrs\tx2155\tx8885 \b Viewer Macro\tab Reason
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx8885 ChangeCitation\plain\f1\fs16 \tab WinHelp supports only a single citation (specified in the project (.HPJ)file.)
\par \b ClosePane\tab \plain\f1\fs16 Panes are not supported in WinHelp 4.0.
\par \b CopyBmp\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp cannot copy images to the Clipboard.
\par \b DeleteMenu\tab \plain\f1\fs16 Menus can be added or reset in WinHelp 4.0, but not deleted.
\par \b DeleteSeparator\tab \plain\f1\fs16 Use the \uldb DeleteItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v DeleteItem>main} macro.
\par \b FTSearch\tab \plain\f1\fs16 Use the \uldb Find\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Find>main} macro to provide access to the full-text search user interface.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx8885 \b HideButtonBar\tab \plain\f1\fs16 The WinHelp \ul main window\plain\f1\fs16 {\v O8.Q2} button bar can be turned off in the window definition in the project file.
\par \b HideMenuBar\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp \ul secondary windows\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2ZK4PT} do not display menus.
\par \b Highlight\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp version 4.0 does not highlight the words found in full-text searches.
\par \b InsertButton\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp does not support specific button positioning.
\par \b InsertSeparator\tab \plain\f1\fs16 Use the \uldb ExtInsertItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ExtInsertItem>main} macro.
\par \b JumpAddr\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp provides its own full-text search.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx8885 \b JumpCookie\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp uses \ul hash codes\plain\f1\fs16 {\v OYZM9} rather than addresses.
\par \b KeyIndex\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp uses the \uldb Search\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Search>main} macro to display the keyword Index tab in the Help Topics dialog box.
\par \b MasterAspect\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp does not support panes.
\par \b MasterNSRColor\tab \plain\f1\fs16 Colors for nonscrolling regions are part of window definitions, which are specified in the project file.
\par \b MasterSRColor\tab \plain\f1\fs16 Color for scrolling regions are part of window definitions, which are specified in the project file.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx8885 \b MCICommand\tab \plain\f1\fs16 In WinHelp, MCI support is provided by the \b \{\-mci\'7d\plain\f1\fs16  topic file reference.
\par \b PaneId\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp does not support panes.
\par \b PositionMaster\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp does not support panes.
\par \b PositionTopic\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp provides macro support only for device-independent window positioning (using the \uldb PositionWindow\plain\f1\fs16 {\v PositionWindow>main} macro).
\par \b SearchDialog\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp provides a \uldb Search\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Search>main} macro to display the Index tab in the Help Topics dialog box.
\par \b ShowButtonBar\tab \plain\f1\fs16 The WinHelp main window button bar is configurable in the window definition in the project file.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx8885 \b ShowMenuBar\tab \plain\f1\fs16 Menus are not configurable in WinHelp
\par \b Std20Buttons\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp provides its own standard buttons for the main window.
\par \b Std20Menus\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp provides its own standard menus for the main window.
\par \b SwitchToTopicsFound\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp provides its own full-text search user interface, which does not include a separate Topics Found dialog box.
\par \b WindowAspect\tab \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp does not support panes.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx2155\tx8885 Help Workshop ignores these unsupported macros in the Help project file, but alerts you to their presence with notes and warning messages.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL("CITATION;Search;Finder;PositionWindow;mci")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("CITATION;Search;Finder;PositionWindow;mci")}
\par
```

### WinHelp Footnotes

### Decoded Body

```text
WinHelp-Specific Macros

The following macros are not provided in Viewer 2.0, but are supported in WinHelp version 4.0.

WinHelp Macro	Purpose

ALinkALink	Searches for matching 'A' keywords in the current (and associated) Help (.HLP) files. New for WinHelp 4.0.

ChangeEnableChangeEnable	Assigns a macro to a navigation bar button and enables that button. New for WinHelp 4.0.

CloseSecondarysCloseSecondarys	Closes all secondary windows. New for WinHelp 4.0.

CompareCompare	Displays a Help file in a second instance of WinHelp. New for WinHelp 4.0.

ControlPanelControlPanel	Displays the specified control panel program. New for WinHelp 4.0.

EndMPrintEndMPrint	Dismisses the printing message box and terminates the printing of multiple topics. New for WinHelp 4.0.

ExecFileExecFile	Runs a Windows-based program with a specified file. New for WinHelp 4.0.

ExtAbleItemExtAbleItem	Enables or disables a menu item.

ExtInsertItemExtInsertItem	Allows the initial state of a menu item to be set as enabled or disabled.

ExtInsertMenuExtInsertMenu	Allows submenus (cascading menus) to be created.

FileExistFileExist	Checks to see whether the specified file exists on the user's computer. New for WinHelp 4.0.

FinderFinder	Displays the Help Topics dialog box. New for WinHelp 4.0.

FloatingMenuFloatingMenu	Displays the floating menu at the current mouse position.

FlushFlush	Causes WinHelp to process any pending messages.

GenerateGenerate	Sends a message to the currently active Help window.

HelpOnHelpOn	Displays the Help file for the WinHelp program.

HelpOnTopHelpOnTop	Toggles the on-top state of WinHelp.

InitMPrintInitMPrint	Begins printing multiple topics. New for WinHelp 4.0.

IsBookIsBook	Determines whether WinHelp is being run standalone or called from a program. New for WinHelp 4.0.

IsNotMarkIsNotMark	Tests whether or not a marker set by SaveMark exists. Same as Not(IsMark(text)). New for WinHelp 4.0.

JumpContextJumpContext	Jumps to a specific context (specified in the [MAP] section of the project (.HPJ)file) in a Help file.

JumpHashJumpHash	Jumps to the topic specified by the hash code.

JumpHelpOnJumpHelpOn	Jumps to the contents topic of the How to Use Help file.

KLinkKLink	Searches for matching keywords in the current (and associated) Help file(s). New for WinHelp 4.0.

MenuMenu	Displays the context (right-click) menu for the current Help window. New for WinHelp 4.0.

NoShowNoShow	Causes the current topic (and the Help window) to NOT display. New for WinHelp 4.0.

PopupContextPopupContext	Displays a specified topic in a pop-up window.

PopupHashPopupHash	Displays the topic, specified by the hash code, in a pop-up window.

RemoveAcceleratorRemoveAccelerator	Removes the assignment made with AddAccelerator.

ResetMenuResetMenu	Restores the WinHelp menus to their default states.

SearchSearch	Displays the Help Topics dialog box with the Index tab selected. New for WinHelp 4.0.

SetContentsSetContents	Designates a specific topic as the contents topic in the Help file.

SetHelpOnFileSetHelpOnFile	Designates the name of the "How To Use Help" file, if different from the standard file.

SetPopupColorSetPopupColor	Sets the background color for pop-up windows. New for WinHelp 4.0.

ShellExecuteShellExecute	Opens or prints the specified file. New for WinHelp 4.0

ShortCutShortCut	Runs a program and sends it a WM_COMMAND message. New for WinHelp 4.0.

TCardTCard	Sends a message to the program that is invoking WinHelp as a training card. New for WinHelp 4.0.

TestTest	Tests the current Help file. New for WinHelp 4.0.

UpdateWindowUpdateWindow	Jumps to the topic with the specified context string in the specified window and returns the focus to the window that called the macro. New for WinHelp 4.0.

{button ,AL("SaveMark;AddAccelerator")} Related Topics%!AL("SaveMark;AddAccelerator")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 WinHelp-Specific Macros
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 The following macros are not provided in Viewer 2.0, but are supported in WinHelp version 4.0.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\brdrb\brdrs\tx2155\tx8875 \b WinHelp Macro\tab Purpose
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx2875\tx8875 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 ALink\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ALink}\tab Searches for matching 'A' keywords in the current (and associated) Help (.HLP) files. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb ChangeEnable\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ChangeEnable}\tab Assigns a macro to a navigation bar button and enables that button. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb CloseSecondarys\plain\f1\fs16 {\v CloseSecondarys}\tab Closes all secondary windows. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb Compare\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Compare}\tab Displays a Help file in a second instance of WinHelp. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb ControlPanel\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ControlPanel}\tab Displays the specified control panel program. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx2875\tx8875 \uldb EndMPrint\plain\f1\fs16 {\v EndMPrint}\tab Dismisses the printing message box and terminates the printing of multiple topics. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb ExecFile\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ExecFile}\tab Runs a Windows-based program with a specified file. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb ExtAbleItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ExtAbleItem}\tab Enables or disables a menu item.
\par \uldb ExtInsertItem\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ExtInsertItem}\tab Allows the initial state of a menu item to be set as enabled or disabled.
\par \uldb ExtInsertMenu\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ExtInsertMenu}\tab Allows submenus (cascading menus) to be created.
\par \uldb FileExist\plain\f1\fs16 {\v FileExist}\tab Checks to see whether the specified file exists on the user's computer. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx2875\tx8875 \uldb Finder\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Finder}\tab Displays the Help Topics dialog box. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb FloatingMenu\plain\f1\fs16 {\v FloatingMenu}\tab Displays the floating menu at the current mouse position.
\par \uldb Flush\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Flush}\tab Causes WinHelp to process any pending messages.
\par \uldb Generate\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Generate}\tab Sends a message to the currently active Help window.
\par \uldb HelpOn\plain\f1\fs16 {\v HelpOn}\tab Displays the Help file for the WinHelp program.
\par \uldb HelpOnTop\plain\f1\fs16 {\v HelpOnTop}\tab Toggles the on-top state of WinHelp.
\par \uldb InitMPrint\plain\f1\fs16 {\v InitMPrint}\tab Begins printing multiple topics. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx2875\tx8875 \uldb IsBook\plain\f1\fs16 {\v IsBook}\tab Determines whether WinHelp is being run standalone or called from a program. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb IsNotMark\plain\f1\fs16 {\v IsNotMark}\tab Tests whether or not a marker set by SaveMark exists. Same as Not(IsMark(text)). New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb JumpContext\plain\f1\fs16 {\v JumpContext}\tab Jumps to a specific context (specified in the [MAP] section of the project (.HPJ)file) in a Help file.
\par \uldb JumpHash\plain\f1\fs16 {\v JumpHash}\tab Jumps to the topic specified by the hash code.
\par \uldb JumpHelpOn\plain\f1\fs16 {\v JumpHelpOn}\tab Jumps to the contents topic of the How to Use Help file.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx2875\tx8875 \uldb KLink\plain\f1\fs16 {\v KLink}\tab Searches for matching keywords in the current (and associated) Help file(s). New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb Menu\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Menu}\tab Displays the context (right-click) menu for the current Help window. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb NoShow\plain\f1\fs16 {\v NoShow}\tab Causes the current topic (and the Help window) to NOT display. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb PopupContext\plain\f1\fs16 {\v PopupContext}\tab Displays a specified topic in a pop-up window.
\par \uldb PopupHash\plain\f1\fs16 {\v PopupHash}\tab Displays the topic, specified by the hash code, in a pop-up window.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx2875\tx8875 \uldb RemoveAccelerator\plain\f1\fs16 {\v RemoveAccelerator}\tab Removes the assignment made with AddAccelerator.
\par \uldb ResetMenu\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ResetMenu}\tab Restores the WinHelp menus to their default states.
\par \uldb Search\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Search}\tab Displays the Help Topics dialog box with the Index tab selected. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb SetContents\plain\f1\fs16 {\v SetContents}\tab Designates a specific topic as the contents topic in the Help file.
\par \uldb SetHelpOnFile\plain\f1\fs16 {\v SetHelpOnFile}\tab Designates the name of the "How To Use Help" file, if different from the standard file.
\par \uldb SetPopupColor\plain\f1\fs16 {\v SetPopupColor}\tab Sets the background color for pop-up windows. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li2155\ri125\fi-2025\tx2155\tx2875\tx8875 \uldb ShellExecute\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ShellExecute}\tab Opens or prints the specified file. New for WinHelp 4.0\uldb 
\par ShortCut\plain\f1\fs16 {\v ShortCut}\tab Runs a program and sends it a WM_COMMAND message. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb TCard\plain\f1\fs16 {\v TCard}\tab Sends a message to the program that is invoking WinHelp as a training card. New for WinHelp 4.0.
\par \uldb Test\plain\f1\fs16 {\v Test}\tab Tests the current Help file. New for WinHelp 4.0.
```

```text
Differences in Macros

The following macros are provided in both Viewer 2.0 and WinHelp version 4.0, but in slightly different formats.

Macro	Change

AddAcceleratorAddAccelerator	Uses slightly different virtual key (VK_*) codes.

PopupIdPopupId	Does not support the pop-up window specification.

{button ,AL("REF_STD_BTN;REF_STD_MENU")} Related Topics%!AL("REF_STD_BTN;REF_STD_MENU")
```

### Decoded Body

```text
Update Multimedia References

Help authors include multimedia files (AVI, WAV, MIDI, and so on) in Help topics by using the {-mci} statement in the topic (.RTF) files.

WinHelp version 4.0 supports all the multimedia APIs used by Microsoft Video for Windows version 1.1.

{button ,AL("BAS_ADDING_SOUND;BAS_ADDING_VIDEO;mci")} Related Topics%!AL("BAS_ADDING_SOUND;BAS_ADDING_VIDEO;mci")
```

## 417. Convert the project file

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`

  - Raw:

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `MVP files, converting to WinHelp 4.0;project files;project files, Viewer 2.0 converting;Viewer 2.0;Viewer 2.0, converting project files`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    MVP files, converting to WinHelp 4.0;project files;project files, Viewer 2.0 converting;Viewer 2.0;Viewer 2.0, converting project files
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Convert the Project File

To convert your Viewer project file into a WinHelp version 4.0 project (.HPJ) file:

1	Rename your Viewer project file, replacing the .MVP filename extension with .HPJ filename extension.

2	Run Help Workshop and open the renamed project file.

3	Edit the project file as necessary.

4	Compile the project file. Help Workshop will display messages if there are problems.

Notes

·	Some Viewer options and sections are not supported by the version 4.0 Help Workshop.

·	You can add WinHelp-specific options and sections by using Help Workshop.

·	Use Help Workshop to make changes and additions to the project file. Editing the project file as a text file can introduce serious malfunctions.

{button ,AL("from_v2_update_hpj")} Related Topics%!AL("from_v2_update_hpj")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Convert the Project File
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 To convert your Viewer project file into a WinHelp version 4.0 project (.HPJ) file:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 1\tab Rename your Viewer project file, replacing the .MVP filename extension with .HPJ filename extension.
\par 2\tab Run Help Workshop and open the renamed project file.
\par 3\tab Edit the project file as necessary.
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 4\tab Compile the project file. Help Workshop will display messages if there are problems.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx295 \b Notes
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \plain\f2\fs16 \'b7\f1 \tab Some Viewer options and sections are not supported by the version 4.0 Help Workshop.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab You can add WinHelp-specific options and sections by using Help Workshop.
\par \f2 \'b7\f1 \tab Use Help Workshop to make changes and additions to the project file. Editing the project file as a text file can introduce serious malfunctions.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295

\par
```


### Decoded Body

```text
Unsupported Viewer Project File Options

The following Viewer project file sections are not supported by Help Workshop version 4.0. If present in your project (.HPJ) file, Help Workshop ignores unsupported sections in the Help project file, but alerts you to their presence with notes and warning messages.

·	[FTINDEX]

·	[GROUPS]

·	[KEYINDEX]

·	[PANEASSOC]

·	[PANES]

·	[POPUPS]

·	[SEARCHDLG]

·	[WWHEEL]

The following options in the [OPTIONS] section of the project file are not supported by Help Workshop. The compiler ignores these options in the Help project file, but alerts you to their presence with notes and warning messages.

·	ALIAS

·	BATCH

·	ICON

·	IGNORESECTION

·	MAKE

·	OPERATOR

·	SYSTEM

·	WARNING

{button ,AL("HPJ_Sections;OVERVIEW;V2_WINHELP_PROJECT_FILE;V2_CHANGED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_UPDATE_PROJECT_FILE;V2_NEW_PROJECT_FILE")} Related Topics%!AL("HPJ_Sections;V2_WINHELP_PROJECT_FILE;V2_CHANGED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_UPDATE_PROJECT_FILE;V2_NEW_PROJECT_FILE")
```

- Marker `K`

  - Raw:
    ```rtf

    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
WinHelp-specific Project File Options

These options are supported by Help Workshop but not the Viewer compiler. Use Help Workshop to add or edit these options.

Option	Description

CHARSET0H87TF	Specifies the character set to use for all fonts.

CNTRB3WX	Specifies the name of the contents (.CNT) file.2GB40S to associate with the Help (.HLP) file.

DBCSRB4KGS	Specifies that the topic (.RTF) files use a double-byte character set

DEFFONTECK4F6	Specifies the default font to use in WinHelp.

ERRORLOGYCLLJE	Specifies the name of the log file to create when compiling the Help file.

FORCEFONT24ML4CE	Forces WinHelp to use the specified font when displaying the Help file, in place of the fonts used in the topic files.

FTSRB72W	Specifies the level of information to include in the full-text search index file.

HCW2OLG6TE	Reserved for use by Help Workshop.

HLPRB8UT	Specifies the name of the Help file to create.

INDEX_SEPARATORS2A9VNJZ	Identifies the characters used to denote separate keyword entries in topics.

LCIDRB.LMD	Specifies the locale ID.

MAPFONTSIZE12DOBQV	Maps font sizes specified in the topic files to different sizes when displayed in the WinHelp window.

MULTIKEYK.Z2KQ	Customizes the keyword search so that it uses more than one keyword table.

NOTES15Q.CUI	Specifies whether or not the compiler will display any notes during compilation.

OLDKEYPHRASE4_QY9B	Specifies whether or not to create a new keyword phrase (.PH) file.

REPLACE06VZSZ	Specifies a path prefix to replace and its replacement.

REPORTJOHTZB	Specifies whether or not to display messages when compiling the Help file.

TMPDIR2NHAHAE	Specifies the folder to place the temporary files created while compiling the Help file.

{button ,AL("V2_UPDATE_PROJECT_FILE;V2_CHANGED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_UNSUPPORTED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_NEW_PROJECT_FILE")} Related Topics%!AL("V2_UPDATE_PROJECT_FILE;V2_CHANGED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_UNSUPPORTED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_NEW_PROJECT_FILE")
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 These options are supported by Help Workshop but not the Viewer compiler. Use Help Workshop to add or edit these options.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li1985\ri125\fi-1855\brdrb\brdrs\tx425\tx575\tx1975 \b Option\tab Description
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1985\ri125\fi-1855\tx1975 \uldb \plain\f1\uldb\fs16 CHARSET\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 0H87TF}\tab Specifies the character set to use for all fonts.
\par \uldb CNT\plain\f1\fs16 {\v RB3WX}\tab Specifies the name of the \ul contents (.CNT) file.\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2GB40S} to associate with the Help (.HLP) file.
\par \uldb DBCS\plain\f1\fs16 {\v RB4KGS}\tab Specifies that the topic (.RTF) files use a double-byte character set
```

### Decoded Body

```text
Differences in Project File Options

The following parameters have the same name in both Viewer and WinHelp, but are slightly different:

The COMPRESS option in the [OPTIONS] section has slightly different values for Help Workshop version 4.0. Help Workshop will automatically change this value to 0, so you must specifically set the compression yourself.

The window definitions in the [WINDOWS] section of the Help project file use a different format.

{button ,AL("WINDOWS;COMPRESS;HPJ_Sections;V2_UPDATE_PROJECT_FILE;V2_WINHELP_PROJECT_FILE;V2_UNSUPPORTED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_NEW_PROJECT_FILE")} Related Topics%!AL("WINDOWS;COMPRESS;HPJ_Sections;V2_UPDATE_PROJECT_FILE;V2_WINHELP_PROJECT_FILE;V2_UNSUPPORTED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_NEW_PROJECT_FILE")
```


### Decoded Body

```text
WinHelp-specific Project File Sections

The following sections are supported by Help Workshop version 4.0, but not by the Viewer compiler. Use Help Workshop to add or edit these options.

[MACROS] Section

The optional [MACROS] section associates macros with keywords. The specified macros are run when a user selects an associated keyword from the index.

[CONFIG:name] Section

The optional [CONFIG:name] section is similar to the [CONFIG] section, except any specified macros are run when a particular window name is opened. The x in the section name is a number that associates the section with a particular window definition. This number is assigned by the Project File Editor.

{button ,AL("MACROS;CONFIG_x;V2_UPDATE_PROJECT_FILE;V2_CHANGED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_UNSUPPORTED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_WINHELP_PROJECT_FILE")} Related Topics%!AL("MACROS;CONFIG_x;V2_UPDATE_PROJECT_FILE;V2_CHANGED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_UNSUPPORTED_PROJECT_FILE;V2_WINHELP_PROJECT_FILE")
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 The following sections are supported by Help Workshop version 4.0, but not by the Viewer compiler. Use Help Workshop to add or edit these options.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b [MACROS] Section
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b optional [MACROS] section associates\plain\f1\fs16  macros with keywords. The specified macros are run when a user selects an associated keyword from the index.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b [CONFIG:name] Section
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 The optional [CONFIG:name] section is similar to the [CONFIG] section, except any specified macros are run when a particular window name is opened. The x in the section name is a number that associates the section with a particular window definition. This number is assigned by the Project File Editor.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `contents files;contents files, creating when converting from Viewer 2.0;creating;creating, contents files;Viewer 2.0;Viewer 2.0, creating`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    contents files;contents files, creating when converting from Viewer 2.0;creating;creating, contents files;Viewer 2.0;Viewer 2.0, creating
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Create a Contents File

WinHelp version 4.0 supports the use of a contents (.CNT) file,2GB40S a text file that specifies what topics display in the Contents tab in the Help Topics dialog box.KMK38I The Contents tab replaces the "contents topic" used in both former versions of WinHelp and Viewer version 2.0. The contents file also allows authors to create a combined index using the keywords in other Help (.HLP) files, and to specify which Help files to search when the user clicks a hotspot that runs the ALink or KLink macros.

Help Workshop can be used to create or edit contents files.

{button ,AL("BAS_CNT_OVERVIEW;BAS_CONTENTS_FILE_EDITOR;ALink;KLink")} Related Topics%!AL("BAS_CNT_OVERVIEW;BAS_CONTENTS_FILE_EDITOR;ALink;KLink")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 Create a Contents File
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp version 4.0 supports the use of a \ul contents (.CNT) file,\plain\f1\fs16 {\v 2GB40S} a text file that specifies what topics display in the Contents tab in the \ul Help Topics dialog box.\plain\f1\fs16 {\v KMK38I} The Contents tab replaces the "contents topic" used in both former versions of WinHelp and Viewer version 2.0. The contents file also allows authors to create a combined index using the keywords in other Help (.HLP) files, and to specify which Help files to search when the user clicks a hotspot that runs the ALink or KLink macros.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Help Workshop can be used to create or edit contents files.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL("BAS_CNT_OVERVIEW;BAS_CONTENTS_FILE_EDITOR;ALink;KLink")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("BAS_CNT_OVERVIEW;BAS_CONTENTS_FILE_EDITOR;ALink;KLink")}
\par
```

## 424. Empty keyword string

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:0001`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:0001
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Empty keyword string`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Empty keyword string
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC1000`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC1000
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC1000`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC1000
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC1000: Note: A keyword footnote has been specified without a keyword.

Problem: There are no characters in the keyword footnote in the topic (.rtf) files.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the keyword footnote. If there are no additional keyword footnotes, the topic will be inaccessible from the Index tab in the Help Topics dialog box.

Solution: Add one or more keywords to the keyword footnote, and then recompile.

{button ,AL(`BAS_ADDING_KEYWORDS')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_ADDING_KEYWORDS')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125 
\b\cf2 HC1000:\plain\f1\b\fs16  Note: A keyword footnote has been specified without a keyword.
```

## 426. Using existing phrase table

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:0003`
  - Raw:

    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:0003
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Using existing phrase table`
  - Raw:

    ```rtf
    Using existing phrase table
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC1002`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC1002
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC1002`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC1002
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC1002: Note: Using existing phrase table: [ ].

Problem: Help Workshop found an existing phrase (.ph) table for the Help file you are building, and the project (.hpj) file does not specify not to use the table.

Result: Help Workshop uses the phrase table that was generated from an earlier build instead of creating a new table.

Solution: To achieve the best phrase compression when compiling your Help file, you must delete the old key-phrase table before each recompilation, or clear the "Use old phrase file" option in the Compression tab in the Options dialog box of Help Workshop.

{button ,AL(`OLDKEYPHRASE;BAS_COMPRESSION')} Related Topics%!AL(`OLDKEYPHRASE;BAS_COMPRESSION')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125 
\b\cf2 HC1002:\plain\f1\b\fs16  Note: Using existing phrase table: [\'a0].
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop found an existing phrase (.ph) table for the Help file you are building, and the project (.hpj) file does not specify not to use the table.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop uses the phrase table that was generated from an earlier build instead of creating a new table.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 To achieve the best phrase compression when compiling your Help file, you must delete the old key-phrase table before each recompilation, or clear the "Use old phrase file" option in the Compression tab in the Options dialog box of Help Workshop.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`OLDKEYPHRASE;BAS_COMPRESSION')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`OLDKEYPHRASE;BAS_COMPRESSION')}
\par
```

- Marker `$`

  - Decoded: `HC1003`
  - Raw:

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC1003`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC1003
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC1003: Note: A paragraph mark is formatted as a hidden character.

Problem: A paragraph mark has been formatted as hidden text. This often occurs when a hotspot is the last word in a paragraph.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the paragraph mark, so the two paragraphs will run together.

Solution: Reformat the paragraph marker as plain text in the topic file, and then recompile.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf

\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores the paragraph mark, so the two paragraphs will run together.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Reformat the paragraph marker as plain text in the topic file, and then recompile.
\par
```


### Decoded Body

```text
HC1004: Note: A previous instance of [ ] does not contain [ ].

Problem: A {-bmx} command specifying only one resolution version of a bitmap is followed later in the topic files by a {-bmx} command specifying that version plus one or more additional versions.

Result: WinHelp will treat the earlier single-resolution command as if it were the same as the command specifying multiple resolutions.

Solution: Replace the single resolution command with a copy of the multiple-resolution command.

{button ,AL(`BAS_MULT_COLOR_GRAPHICS;bmx')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_MULT_COLOR_GRAPHICS;bmx')
```

```text

Problem: The parameter for this macro should be unsigned, but the number has been prefixed with a minus character.

Result: Help Workshop will remove the minus sign when it creates the Help file.

Solution: Remove the minus sign from the macro definition, and then recompile.

{button ,AL(`syntax_more')} Related Topics%!AL(`syntax_more')
```

## 430. ExecProgram used

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:0006`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:0006
    ```
- Marker `$`

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC1006`

    ```rtf

    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC1006`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC1006
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC1006: Note: The ExecProgram macro has been used instead of ExecFile.

Problem: The ExecProgram macro has been used.

Result: The ExecProgram macro ignores the restricted programs that can be set in Windows 95. It will not search the registry from programs, so some programs cannot be launched with this command.

Solution: Replace instances of ExecProgram macro commands with ExecFile macro commands, and then recompile.

{button ,AL("ExecProgram;ExecFile")} Related Topics%!AL("ExecProgram;ExecFile")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 The \b ExecProgram\plain\f1\fs16  macro has been used.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 The ExecProgram macro ignores the restricted programs that can be set in Windows\'a095. It will not search the registry from programs, so some programs cannot be launched with this command.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Replace instances of ExecProgram macro commands with ExecFile macro commands, and then recompile.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL("ExecProgram;ExecFile")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL("ExecProgram;ExecFile")}

```


### Decoded Body

```text
HC1013: Note: The "[ ]" section is missing a right bracket.

Problem: A section in your project file begins with a left bracket, but does not end with a right bracket.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the section heading on this line. The following lines will be processed as if they are part of the previous section which may result in errors.

Solution: Insert the right section heading bracket or remove the line entirely, and then recompile.

{button ,AL(`HPJ_Sections')} Related Topics%!AL(`HPJ_Sections')
```

```text
HC1015: Note: A page break is formatted as a hidden character.

Problem: A page break has been formatted as hidden text.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the page break. If the page break was used to separate two topics, the topics will appear as one.

Solution: Reformat the page break in the topic file as plain text, and then recompile.
```

```rtf
\par
```

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC1017`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf

    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC1017: Note: A nonscrolling region crosses a page break.

Problem: The Keep With Next paragraph formatting used for specifying a non-scrolling region crosses a page break boundary.

Result: The topic beginning with the page break will have a nonscrolling region, whether or not one is intended.

Solution: Remove the Keep With Next paragraph formatting from the page break, and then recompile.
```

```rtf
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Remove the Keep With Next paragraph formatting from the page break, and then recompile.
\par
```

## 444. Keywords differ in case only

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:0014`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:0014
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Keywords differ in case only`
  - Raw:

    ```rtf
    Keywords differ in case only
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC2002`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC2002
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC2002: Note: The keywords [ ] and [ ] differ only by case. Help Workshop has modified one keyword to match the other.

Problem: Two keywords are identical except for case.

Result: Help Workshop automatically changes the case of one of the keywords to agree with that of another otherwise identical keyword. For example: The uppercase letters in the keyword "MyKeyword" may be changed to lowercase letters in order to agree with the keyword, "mykeyword."

Solution: Change the keywords so that they all use the same case, and then recompile.

{button ,AL(`BAS_ADDING_KEYWORDS')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_ADDING_KEYWORDS')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf

\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop automatically changes the case of one of the keywords to agree with that of another otherwise identical keyword. For example: The uppercase letters in the keyword "MyKeyword" may be changed to lowercase letters in order to agree with the keyword, "mykeyword."
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Change the keywords so that they all use the same case, and then recompile.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295

    ```

```text
Problem: Help Workshop does not recognize the macro you have entered.

Result: WinHelp may not be able to run the macro.

Solution: If you meant to use a WinHelp macro, confirm that you have spelled it correctly. Note that macro names are always entered in English no matter what language the text of the topic file is in. If the macro is supplied by a dynamic-link library (DLL), make certain you have registered the macro name using the RegisterRoutine macro in the [CONFIG] section of your project file.

{button ,AL(`syntax_more')} Related Topics%!AL(`syntax_more')
```

```text
Problem: You have specified a {-button } command without specifying a comma followed by the macro to run when the button is clicked.

Solution: Look up the {-button } command in Related Topics and correct the syntax.

{button ,AL(`syntax_more;button')} Related Topics%!AL(`syntax_more;button')
```

```text
HC3010: Warning: The macro name [ ] is undefined.

Problem: The macro name you have used is not a valid WinHelp macro and has not been defined by a RegisterRoutine macro.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the rest of the macro. WinHelp cannot run the macro.

Solution: If this is a macro that calls a dynamic-link library (DLL) function, add the RegisterRoutine macro to the project file that defines this function name. Otherwise, you have probably misspelled the macro name.

{button ,AL(`syntax_more;RegisterRoutine')} Related Topics%!AL(`syntax_more;RegisterRoutine')
```


### Decoded Body

```text
HC3015: Warning: This window position is invalid: [ ].

Problem: An invalid window position has been used. The window position in a window definition consists of four numbers that define the window’s location on the screen and its width and height. The four numbers must be given in WinHelp’s 1024-by-1024 coordinate system, and they must be enclosed in parentheses and separated by commas.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the window definition.

Solution: In Help Workshop, open the project file. Click the Windows button and then click the Position tab. Adjust the position as necessary.

{button ,AL(`WINDOWS;PRO_HPJ_CREATE_WINDOW;PRO_HPJ_CUSTOMIZE_WINDOW')} Related Topics%!AL(`WINDOWS;PRO_HPJ_CREATE_WINDOW;PRO_HPJ_CUSTOMIZE_WINDOW')
```

```rtf
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores the window definition.

\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`HPJ_Overview')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`HPJ_Overview')}
\par
```

  - Decoded: `HC3019`

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC3019`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3019
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC3019: Warning: Missing quotation mark or parenthesis in the macro [ ].

Problem: There is an unmatched quotation mark or parenthesis.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the rest of the macro. WinHelp cannot run the macro.

Solution: Add the missing quotation or right parenthesis to the macro definition in the topic file. Help Workshop will usually supply the quotes for you — deleting all the quotes in the macro can be an effective method for fixing this problem.

{button ,AL(`syntax_more')} Related Topics%!AL(`syntax_more')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 There is an unmatched quotation mark or parenthesis.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores the rest of the macro. WinHelp cannot run the macro.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Add the missing quotation or right parenthesis to the macro definition in the topic file. Help Workshop will usually supply the quotes for you \plain\f0\fs16 \'97\f1  deleting all the quotes in the macro can be an effective method for fixing this problem.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`syntax_more')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`syntax_more')}
```

## 466. Duplicate bitmap

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Duplicate bitmap`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Duplicate bitmap
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC3022`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3022
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC3022`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3022
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC3022: Warning: The bitmap [ ] has already been used as a non-transparent bitmap.

Problem: A bitmap cannot be transparent in one instance and non-transparent in another.

Result: The bitmap will always be displayed as non-transparent.

Solution: Change either bitmap command so that they are both transparent or both non-transparent. Alternatively, you can create a new file for a bitmap.

{button ,AL(`bmx;BAS_ADDING_GRAPHICS')} Related Topics%!AL(`bmx;BAS_ADDING_GRAPHICS')
```

### Raw RTF Body
