```rtf
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 The bitmap will always be displayed as non-transparent.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Change either bitmap command so that they are both transparent or both non-transparent. Alternatively, you can create a new file for a bitmap.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`bmx;BAS_ADDING_GRAPHICS')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`bmx;BAS_ADDING_GRAPHICS')}
```

```text
HC3024: Warning: The window name [ ] contains more than eight characters and has been truncated to [ ].

Problem: The window name is too long.

Result: Help Workshop truncates the name to 8 characters. If this results in a duplicate window name, you will get another warning message.

Solution: Run Help Workshop and open the project file. You will be prompted for a correction to the window name. Make the correction, and then recompile the project file.

{button ,AL(`WINDOWS;PRO_HPJ_CREATE_WINDOW;PRO_HPJ_CUSTOMIZE_WINDOW')} Related Topics%!AL(`WINDOWS;PRO_HPJ_CREATE_WINDOW;PRO_HPJ_CUSTOMIZE_WINDOW')
```

```rtf
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Run Help Workshop and open the project file. You will be prompted for a correction to the window name. Make the correction, and then recompile the project file.
```

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC3028`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3028
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC3028: Warning: The bitmap file [ ] is corrupted.

Problem: Either the file is corrupt, or it has not been saved as an uncompressed bitmap (.bmp) file.

Result: Help Workshop ignores this bitmap, and WinHelp displays the “Unable to display picture” message in the topic instead of the bitmap.

Solution: Replace the bitmap file with one that is not corrupt, or remove the bitmap reference in the topic file.

{button ,AL(`BAS_ADDING_GRAPHICS')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_ADDING_GRAPHICS')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf

\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores this bitmap, and WinHelp displays the \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 Unable to display picture\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  message in the topic instead of the bitmap.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Replace the bitmap file with one that is not corrupt, or remove the bitmap reference in the topic file.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```

```text
{button ,AL(`BAS_ADDING_TOPIC_ID')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_ADDING_TOPIC_ID')
```

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC3038`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3038
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC3038: Warning: The following topic IDs were not defined in the [MAP] section of the project file.

Problem: Help Workshop cannot find the specified topic IDs listed in the [MAP] section of the project (.hpj) file.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the lines in the project file. If the program uses that number in the WinHelp function, WinHelp displays the "Help topic not found" error message.

Solution: Verify that all topic IDs listed in the [MAP] section of the project file are assigned to topics included in the build and that the topic IDs are spelled correctly. Then recompile the project file.

{button ,AL(`MAP;PRO_HPJ_ADD_MAP')} Related Topics%!AL(`MAP;PRO_HPJ_ADD_MAP')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop cannot find the specified topic IDs listed in the [MAP] section of the project (.hpj) file.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores the lines in the project file. If the program uses that number in the WinHelp function, WinHelp displays the "Help topic not found" error message.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Verify that all topic IDs listed in the [MAP] section of the project file are assigned to topics included in the build and that the topic IDs are spelled correctly. Then recompile the project file.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```

## 488. Nonscrolling region after scrolling region

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:003f`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:003f
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Nonscrolling region after scrolling region`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Nonscrolling region after scrolling region
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC3048`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3048
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC3048`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3048
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC3048: Warning: Nonscrolling region is defined after scrolling region.

Problem: The Keep With Next attribute was applied to a paragraph after a paragraph that did not have this attribute.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the Keep With Next attribute; the paragraph is treated as plain text and is displayed as part of the topic text displayed in the scrolling region of the topic.

Solution: Set the Keep With Next attribute for the first paragraph in the topic if you want a nonscrolling region, and remove the Keep With Next attribute from following paragraphs. Then recompile the Help file.

{button ,AL(`keepn_rtf;PRO_NONSCROLL')} Related Topics%!AL(`keepn_rtf;PRO_NONSCROLL')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125 
\b\cf2 HC3048:\plain\f1\b\fs16  Warning: Nonscrolling region is defined after scrolling region.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 The Keep With Next attribute was applied to a paragraph after a paragraph that did not have this attribute.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores the Keep With Next attribute; the paragraph is treated as plain text and is displayed as part of the topic text displayed in the scrolling region of the topic.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Set the Keep With Next attribute for the first paragraph in the topic if you want a nonscrolling region, and remove the Keep With Next attribute from following paragraphs. Then recompile the Help file.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`keepn_rtf;PRO_NONSCROLL')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`keepn_rtf;PRO_NONSCROLL')}
\par
```

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC3053`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3053
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC3053: Warning: The project file contains more than 255 window definitions.

Problem: You have defined more than 255 window types in your project file.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the additional window definitions. If you attempt to jump to a topic in one of the window definitions beyond the first 255, you will get an error message about the window type not being defined.

Solution: Remove the extra window definitions from the project file, and then recompile.

{button ,AL(`WINDOWS;PRO_HPJ_CUSTOMIZE_WINDOW')} Related Topics%!AL(`WINDOWS;PRO_HPJ_CUSTOMIZE_WINDOW')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Remove the extra window definitions from the project file, and then recompile.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```

    ```rtf
    HC3054
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC3054`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3054
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC3054: Warning: Build tag contains invalid characters. The following is invalid: [ ].

Problem: A build tag in the [BUILDTAGS] section contains something other than a alphanumeric character or the underscore (_) character.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the line containing invalid characters.

Solution: Edit the build tag so that it contains only valid characters. Make any necessary changes in topics containing the specified build tag, and then recompile. Alternatively, specify the build tags to include/exclude in the Build Tags tab of the Options dialog box. This form of including/excluding topics places no restrictions on the characters, length, or number of build tags, and does not use the [BUILDTAGS] section.

{button ,AL(`BUILDTAGS;BAS_INSERTING_BUILD_TAG')} Related Topics%!AL(`BUILDTAGS;BAS_INSERTING_BUILD_TAG')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 A build tag in the [BUILDTAGS] section contains something other than a alphanumeric character or the underscore (_) character.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores the line containing invalid characters.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Edit the build tag so that it contains only valid characters. Make any necessary changes in topics containing the specified build tag, and then recompile. Alternatively, specify the build tags to include/exclude in the Build Tags tab of the Options dialog box. This form of including/excluding topics places no restrictions on the characters, length, or number of build tags, and does not use the [BUILDTAGS] section.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`BUILDTAGS;BAS_INSERTING_BUILD_TAG')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`BUILDTAGS;BAS_INSERTING_BUILD_TAG')}
\par
```


### Decoded Body

```text
HC3055: Warning: The build tag [ ] has already been used.

Problem: The build tag in the [BUILDTAGS] section of your project file has already been declared previously in the same section.

Result: Help Workshop uses the first build tag and ignores the duplicate build tag.

Solution: Remove (or rename) the duplicate build tags in the project file, and then recompile.

{button ,AL(`BUILDTAGS')} Related Topics%!AL(`BUILDTAGS')
```

```rtf
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop uses the first build tag and ignores the duplicate build tag.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Remove (or rename) the duplicate build tags in the project file, and then recompile.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```

```text
HC3057: Warning: A hotspot is defined with a macro (using !), but the macro is not specified.

Problem: You started a hotspot with a ‘!’ character, but did not follow it with a macro.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the macro call from the hotspot.

Solution: Specify a valid macro in the hotspot, or remove the '!' character from the hotspot definition. Make certain that all text from the ‘!’ to the end of the macro is marked as hidden.

{button ,AL(`BAS_MACRO_JUMP')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_MACRO_JUMP')
```

## 545. Topic has keyword entries but no title

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Topic has keyword entries but no title`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Topic has keyword entries but no title
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC3111`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3111
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC3111`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3111
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC3111: Warning: This topic contains keywords but no title.

Problem: One or more keywords have been specified for the topic, but a title footnote was not specified.

Result: If no title is specified for the topic, and the keyword appears in more than one topic (including other Help files), WinHelp will display “Untitled topic #n” in the Topics Found dialog box.

Solution: If this is not a pop-up topic, add a title using the title footnote ($), and then recompile. If the topic will only be displayed in a pop-up window, remove the keyword footnotes.

{button ,AL(`BAS_ADDING_KEYWORDS')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_ADDING_KEYWORDS')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125 
\b\cf2 HC3111:\plain\f1\b\fs16  Warning: This topic contains keywords but no title.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 One or more keywords have been specified for the topic, but a title footnote was not specified.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 If no title is specified for the topic, and the keyword appears in more than one topic (including other Help files), WinHelp will display \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 Untitled topic #n\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  in the Topics Found dialog box.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 If this is not a pop-up topic, add a title using the title footnote ($), and then recompile. If the topic will only be displayed in a pop-up window, remove the keyword footnotes.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`BAS_ADDING_KEYWORDS')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`BAS_ADDING_KEYWORDS')}
\par
```

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC3113`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3113
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC3113: Warning: Build tag is longer than 32 characters: [ ].

Problem: The build tag is too long when used in conjunction with the BUILD option in the project file.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the build tag assigned to the topic.

Solution: Shorten the build tag to 32 or fewer characters, and then recompile. Alternatively, specify the build tags to include/exclude in the Build Tags tab of the Options dialog box. This form of including/excluding topics places no restrictions on the characters, length, or number of build tags.

{button ,AL(`BAS_INSERTING_BUILD_TAG')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_INSERTING_BUILD_TAG')
```

```rtf
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores the build tag assigned to the topic.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Shorten the build tag to 32 or fewer characters, and then recompile. Alternatively, specify the build tags to include/exclude in the Build Tags tab of the Options dialog box. This form of including/excluding topics places no restrictions on the characters, length, or number of build tags.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```

## 548. Bitmap commands not coordinated

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:007a`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:007a
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Bitmap commands not coordinated`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Bitmap commands not coordinated
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC3114`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3114
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC3114`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC3114
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC3114: Warning: The bitmap [ ] has been used as part of another {-bmx} command.

Problem: Two {-bmx} commands that specify bitmaps for multiple color depths have a bitmap in common but are otherwise different or in a different order.

Result: WinHelp will treat the shared bitmap files as separate files, and this effectively doubles the space required for each of the specified bitmaps.

Solution: Make sure that bitmap commands that are intended to be identical include the same bitmaps in the same order.

{button ,AL(`BAS_MULT_COLOR_GRAPHICS;bmx')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_MULT_COLOR_GRAPHICS;bmx')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125 
\b HC3114: Warning: The bitmap [\'a0] has been used as part of another \{\-bmx\'7d command.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 Two \{\-bmx\'7d commands that specify bitmaps for multiple color depths have a bitmap in common but are otherwise different or in a different order. 
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp will treat the shared bitmap files as separate files, and this effectively doubles the space required for each of the specified bitmaps.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Make sure that bitmap commands that are intended to be identical include the same bitmaps in the same order.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`BAS_MULT_COLOR_GRAPHICS;bmx')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`BAS_MULT_COLOR_GRAPHICS;bmx')}
\par
```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC3119: Warning: Hash conflict between [ ] and [ ]. One of these topic IDs must be changed.

Problem: The hash algorithm used by Help Workshop to convert a topic ID into an internal number that WinHelp uses, has generated the same hash value for both of the listed topic IDs.

Result: WinHelp displays the topic with the first topic ID whenever the user chooses a hotspot containing either topic ID.

Solution: Change one of the specified topic IDs, and then recompile.

{button ,AL(`BAS_ADDING_TOPIC_ID')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_ADDING_TOPIC_ID')
```

```rtf
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp displays the topic with the first topic ID whenever the user chooses a hotspot containing either topic ID.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Change one of the specified topic IDs, and then recompile.
```

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC4002`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC4002
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC4002: Warning: The RTF file [ ] is corrupted at offset [ ].

Problem: Help Workshop encountered an error while processing the topic (.rtf) file. This problem can occur when the number of opening and closing braces don’t match, when the end of the file is reached before all columns of a table have been specified, or when the number specified for an RTF token is invalid.

Result: Help Workshop ignores the rest of the topic file.

Solution: Open the file and save it again as Rich Text Format (RTF), and then recompile. If the problem persists, look for errors in the topic file.

{button ,AL(`BAS_CREATE_TOPIC_FILE;RTF_OVERVIEW')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_CREATE_TOPIC_FILE;RTF_OVERVIEW')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop encountered an error while processing the topic (.rtf) file. This problem can occur when the number of opening and closing braces don\plain\f0\fs16 \'92\f1 t match, when the end of the file is reached before all columns of a table have been specified, or when the number specified for an RTF token is invalid.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop ignores the rest of the topic file.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Open the file and save it again as Rich Text Format (RTF), and then recompile. If the problem persists, look for errors in the topic file.
```

```text
Problem: Help Workshop reached the end of all topic files without encountering any text.

Result: Help Workshop does not create a Help file.

Solution: Confirm that you have specified at least one topic file that contains text. If all topic contain build tags, you must specify at least one build tag to include (use the Build Tags tab in the Options dialog box of Help Workshop).

{button ,AL(`BAS_SPECIFY_BUILD')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_SPECIFY_BUILD')
```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC4009: Warning: The topic ID specified in the project file as the default topic does not exist.

Problem: You have specified a default topic ID in the project file, but none of your topics contains that topic ID.

Result: Help Workshop uses the first topic in the build as the default topic.

Solution: Verify that the topic ID exists in the desired topic and that it is spelled correctly, and that the topic file is included in the [FILES] section of the project file, and then recompile.

{button ,AL(`CONTENTS_hpj;BAS_ADDING_TOPIC_ID')} Related Topics%!AL(`CONTENTS_hpj;BAS_ADDING_TOPIC_ID')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 You have specified a default topic ID in the project file, but none of your topics contains that topic ID.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop uses the first topic in the build as the default topic.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Verify that the topic ID exists in the desired topic and that it is spelled correctly, and that the topic file is included in the [FILES] section of the project file, and then recompile.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295

```


- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC4010`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC4010
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC4010: Warning: There are more opening braces than closing braces.

Problem: When Help Workshop reached the end of the file, the number of opening and closing braces did not match.

Result: The Help file may be corrupt.

Solution: Load and resave the topic (.rtf) file, and then recompile. If the problem persists, you will need to edit the topic file directly.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf

\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 The Help file may be corrupt.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Load and resave the topic (.rtf) file, and then recompile. If the problem persists, you will need to edit the topic file directly.
\par
```

## 568. Too many opening braces

- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC4011`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC4011
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC4011: Warning: There are 20 opening braces without intervening closing braces.

Problem: More opening braces without matching closing braces have been encountered than should ever occur in a normal topic (.rtf) file.

Result: The rest of the file is ignored.

Solution: Load and resave the topic file, and then recompile. If the problem persists, you will need to edit the topic file directly.
```

```rtf
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Load and resave the topic file, and then recompile. If the problem persists, you will need to edit the topic file directly.
\par
```

## 569. Full-text search index not created

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:008f`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:008f
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Full-text search index not created`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Full-text search index not created
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC4012`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC4012
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC4012`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC4012
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC4012: Warning: The full-text search index cannot be created because neither Phrase nor Hall compression has been selected.

Problem: In order to create a full-text search index, you must compile with either Maximum or Hall compression.

Result: Help Workshop is unable to generate an full-text search (.fts) index file.

Solution: Open the project file in Help Workshop, click the Options button, click the Compression tab, and check the Maximum radio button. Save the project file and recompile.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125 
\b\cf2 HC4012:\plain\f1\b\fs16  Warning: The full-text search index cannot be created because neither Phrase nor Hall compression has been selected.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 In order to create a full-text search index, you must compile with either Maximum or Hall compression.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop is unable to generate an full-text search (.fts) index file.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Open the project file in Help Workshop, click the Options button, click the Compression tab, and check the Maximum radio button. Save the project file and recompile.
\par
```

## 570. Specified default font is not declared

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:0090`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:0090
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Specified default font is not declared`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Specified default font is not declared
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC4013`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC4013
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC4013`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC4013
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC4013: Warning: Invalid default font number in [ ]. Using [ ] as the default font.

Problem: The RTF token \deff specifies as a default font a font number that is not defined in the \fonttbl section.

Result: Help Workshop will ignore the specification and use the first font declared in the \fonttbl section as the default font.

Solution: In the topic (.rtf) file change the number after the \deff token to specify a font declared in the \fonttbl section. Then recompile.

{button ,AL(`RTF1')} Related Topics%!AL(`RTF1')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 The RTF token \'5cdeff specifies as a default font a font number that is not defined in the \'5cfonttbl section.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop will ignore the specification and use the first font declared in the \'5cfonttbl section as the default font.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 In the topic (.rtf) file change the number after the \'5cdeff token to specify a font declared in the\b  \plain\f1\fs16 \'5cfonttbl section. Then recompile.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`RTF1')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`RTF1')}
\par
```

## 571. Text after closing brace in RTF file

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Text after closing brace in RTF file`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Text after closing brace in RTF file
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC4014`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC4014
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC4014`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC4014
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC4014: Warning: There is text after the closing brace in the RTF file.

Problem: Help Workshop encountered the closing brace that should signify the end of the topic (.rtf) file, and then encountered additional text.

Result: Help Workshop adds the text to the Help file, but the text cannot be accessed.

Solution: In the raw topic file, move or delete the text after the closing brace, and then recompile.

{button ,AL(`BAS_CREATE_TOPIC_FILE')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_CREATE_TOPIC_FILE')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop encountered the closing brace that should signify the end of the topic (.rtf) file, and then encountered additional text.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 Help Workshop adds the text to the Help file, but the text cannot be accessed.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 In the raw topic file, move or delete the text after the closing brace, and then recompile.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`BAS_CREATE_TOPIC_FILE')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`BAS_CREATE_TOPIC_FILE')}
\par
```

## 575. Cannot open file

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:0093`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:0093
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Cannot open file`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Cannot open file
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC5003`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC5003
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC5003`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC5003
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC5003: Error: Permission to open the file [ ] is denied. Another program has probably locked the file.

Problem: You do not have the required file privileges to open the requested file. This can happen if the file has been opened by another program. For example, if WinHelp has the Help file open, Help Workshop is not be able to write to that Help file.

Result: The Help file is not created.

Solution: If the file has been loaded in another program, switch to that program and either close the file or close the program. If you are trying to write a file to a networked drive, make certain you have write permission on that server.

{button ,AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 You do not have the required file privileges to open the requested file. This can happen if the file has been opened by another program. For example, if WinHelp has the Help file open, Help Workshop is not be able to write to that Help file.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 The Help file is not created.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 If the file has been loaded in another program, switch to that program and either close the file or close the program. If you are trying to write a file to a networked drive, make certain you have write permission on that server.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```

```text
Result: The Help file is not created.

```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 The Help file is not created.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Add the missing comma or parameter to the macro in the topic file, and then recompile.
```


## 581. Cannot open file

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:0099`
  - Raw:

- Marker `$`

    ```rtf
    Cannot open file
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC5011`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC5011
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC5011`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC5011
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC5011: Error: Cannot open the file [ ].

Problem: The file cannot be opened. This can occur when another program has the file locked, or the file is on a network and the network is down.

Result: The Help file is not created.

Solution: If another program has the file open, close the file. In some cases, it may be necessary to close the program.

{button ,AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES')
```

```rtf
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 The Help file is not created.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 If another program has the file open, close the file. In some cases, it may be necessary to close the program.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES')}
```

## 582. Too many nested #include files

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:009a`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:009a
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Too many nested #include files`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Too many nested #include files
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC5012`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC5012
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC5012`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC5012
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC5012: Error: Too many nested #include files. Cannot include the file [ ].

Problem: A file specified in a #include statement can contain an included file which can in turn contain an included file, up to five levels. This limit has been exceeded.

Result: The Help file is not created.

Solution: Merge the contents of the deepest #include file into its parent, and then recompile.

{button ,AL(`BAS_CNT_OVERVIEW;BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_CNT_OVERVIEW;BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125 
\b\cf2 HC5012:\plain\f1\b\fs16  Error: Too many nested #include files. Cannot include the file [\'a0].
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 A file specified in a #include statement can contain an included file which can in turn contain an included file, up to five levels. This limit has been exceeded.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 The Help file is not created.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Merge the contents of the deepest #include file into its parent, and then recompile.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`BAS_CNT_OVERVIEW;BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`BAS_CNT_OVERVIEW;BAS_ABOUT_HPJ_FILES')}
\par
```

## 584. Out of memory

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:009c`
  - Raw:

    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:009c
    ```
- Marker `$`

    ```rtf
    Out of memory
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC6000`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC6000
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC6000`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC6000
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC6000: Error: Help Workshop is out of memory. If you have any other programs running, close them and try compiling again.

Problem: There is insufficient memory and swap file space.

Result: The Help file is not created.

Solution: If you are running Windows 95, increase the amount of hard disk space available on the drive Windows is installed on. Otherwise, increase the size of your permanent swap file.

{button ,AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125

\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 There is insufficient memory and swap file space.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 The Help file is not created.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 If you are running Windows 95, increase the amount of hard disk space available on the drive Windows is installed on. Otherwise, increase the size of your permanent swap file.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')}

```

```text

Problem: There is insufficient space on the drive where Help Workshop is creating temporary files.

Solution: Either free space on the specified drive, or tell Help Workshop to create temporary files on a different drive (or network connection).

{button ,AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')
```


## 587. Out of file handles

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:009f`
  - Raw:

    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:009f
    ```
- Marker `$`

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC6003`
  - Raw:

    ```rtf
    HC6003
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HC6003`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC6003
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC6003: Error: Out of file handles. Increase the FILES= line in CONFIG.SYS.

Problem: There are not enough file handles available.

Result: The Help file is not created.

Solution: If possible, increase the FILES setting in the CONFIG.SYS file on your computer to FILES=50 or greater. Reboot your computer, then recompile.

{button ,AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')
```

```rtf
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 The Help file is not created.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 If possible, increase the FILES setting in the CONFIG.SYS file on your computer to FILES=50 or greater. Reboot your computer, then recompile.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 The [FILES] section of your project file did not specify any files.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 The Help file is not created.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Open the project file using Help Workshop, click the Files button, click the Add button and enter the name of a topic file.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`FILES;PRO_HPJ_RTF_FILES')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`FILES;PRO_HPJ_RTF_FILES')}
\par
```

## 593. Language identifier incorrect

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE003b:00a5`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    BROWSE003b:00a5
    ```
- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `Language identifier incorrect`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    Language identifier incorrect
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `HC6009`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HC6009
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HC6009: Error: The language identifier [ ] is not supported in this version of Windows.

Problem: The language specified for the Help file is not supported on your version of Windows. For example, you cannot compile a Japanese Help file on an English version of Windows.

Result: The Help file is not created.

Solution: Confirm that correct language has been specified. If the language is correct, you must compile the Help file on a version of Windows appropriate to that language.

{button ,AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')} Related Topics%!AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b\cf2 HC6009:\plain\f1\b\fs16  Error: The language identifier [\'a0] is not supported in this version of Windows.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Problem: \plain\f1\fs16 The language specified for the Help file is not supported on your version of Windows. For example, you cannot compile a Japanese Help file on an English version of Windows.
\par \b Result: \plain\f1\fs16 The Help file is not created.
\par \b Solution: \plain\f1\fs16 Confirm that correct language has been specified. If the language is correct, you must compile the Help file on a version of Windows appropriate to that language.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`BAS_THE_HELP_COMPILER')}
\par
```

## 604. 1E0XAGK

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `1E0XAGK`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    1E0XAGK
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Provides a place for you to type the ID of the topic that WinHelp displays whenever the user initiates a jump to a topic that is unavailable. For Help files that do not have an associated contents (.cnt) file, WinHelp displays the default topic as the first topic when the user first opens the Help file and when the Contents button is clicked. If you do not specify a default topic, WinHelp uses the first topic of the first file listed in the project (.hpj) file.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Provides a place for you to type the ID of the topic that WinHelp displays whenever the user initiates a jump to a topic that is unavailable. If you do not specify a default topic, WinHelp uses the first topic of the first file listed in the project (.hpj) file.
\par
```

## 611. FQP20.

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `FQP20.`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    FQP20.
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Uses both Hall and Zeck compression. If you use maximum compression, it takes approximately three times longer to compile your Help file than it would if you used no compression at all. You can temporarily turn off compression In the Compile a Help File dialog box.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Uses both Hall and Zeck compression. If you use maximum compression, it takes approximately three times longer to compile your Help file than it would if you used no compression at all. You can temporarily turn off compression In the Compile a Help File dialog box.
\par
```

## 612. 13AGWBA

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `13AGWBA`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    13AGWBA
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Enables you to choose between Hall and Zeck compression. If you choose only Zeck compression, you can also choose Phrase compression and specify whether you want to use an existing phrase (.ph) file. More Help is available for each of the items below.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Enables you to choose between Hall and Zeck compression. If you choose only Zeck compression, you can also choose Phrase compression and specify whether you want to use an existing phrase (.ph) file. More Help is available for each of the items below.
\par
```

## 613. 32Z776

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `32Z776`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    32Z776
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked, Help Workshop uses Hall compression, which is more effective than Phrase compression for files that are 100 K or larger in size. If you use Hall compression, your file will compile more quickly than if you use Phrase compression--unless you use Phrase compression with an existing phrase (.ph) file, in which case Phrase compression is significantly faster.

Hall compression is most effective when used with Zeck compression. However, if your Help file will ultimately be compressed by a utility before it is copied to distribution disks, the utility might achieve higher compression if you use only Hall compression.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked, Help Workshop uses Hall compression, which is more effective than Phrase compression for files that are 100 K or larger in size. If you use Hall compression, your file will compile more quickly than if you use Phrase compression--unless you use Phrase compression with an existing phrase (.ph) file, in which case Phrase compression is significantly faster. 
\par Hall compression is most effective when used with Zeck compression. However, if your Help file will ultimately be compressed by a utility before it is copied to distribution disks, the utility might achieve higher compression if you use only Hall compression.
\par
```

## 614. C88N40

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `C88N40`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    C88N40
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked, Help Workshop uses Zeck compression, which, combined with Hall compression, is the most effective compression you can use. You can use Zeck compression without Hall compression to reduce compile time, but doing so will increase the size of your Help file.

If your Help file will ultimately be compressed by a utility before it is copied to distribution disks, the utility might achieve higher compression if you do not use Zeck compression on your Help files. In this case, clear this check box, and use Hall compression instead. Although this will cause the Help files on distribution disks to be smaller, the Help file on users' hard disks will be larger as a result.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked, Help Workshop uses Zeck compression, which, combined with Hall compression, is the most effective compression you can use. You can use Zeck compression without Hall compression to reduce compile time, but doing so will increase the size of your Help file.
\par If your Help file will ultimately be compressed by a utility before it is copied to distribution disks, the utility might achieve higher compression if you do not use Zeck compression on your Help files. In this case, clear this check box, and use Hall compression instead. Although this will cause the Help files on distribution disks to be smaller, the Help file on users' hard disks will be larger as a result. 
\par
```

## 615. R.UD5K

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `R.UD5K`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    R.UD5K
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked, Help Workshop uses Phrase compression, which is sometimes more effective than Hall compression for files that are 100K or smaller in size. You might also want to use Phrase compression if you want to achieve only minimal compression without significantly increasing compile time. Note, however, that compile speed is faster only if you use an existing phrase file. If your file is larger than 100 K, or you do not have an existing phrase file, use Hall compression instead.

To reduce compile time if you have compiled this file using Phrase compression before, make sure the Use Existing Phrase (.ph) File box is checked. Do this only while you are fine-tuning your file, however. You will achieve the best results by using Phrase compression only if the Use Existing Phrase (.ph) file box is clear.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked, Help Workshop uses Phrase compression, which is sometimes more effective than Hall compression for files that are 100K or smaller in size. You might also want to use Phrase compression if you want to achieve only minimal compression without significantly increasing compile time. Note, however, that compile speed is faster only if you use an existing phrase file. If your file is larger than 100 K, or you do not have an existing phrase file, use Hall compression instead.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 To reduce compile time if you have compiled this file using Phrase compression before, make sure the Use Existing Phrase (.ph) File box is checked. Do this only while you are fine-tuning your file, however. You will achieve the best results by using Phrase compression only if the Use Existing Phrase (.ph) file box is clear.
\par
```

## 616. 1_P3O0K

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `1_P3O0K`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    1_P3O0K
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked, and you have compiled this file using Phrase compression before, Help Workshop uses the existing Phrase (.ph) file instead of generating a new one. This can significantly reduce compile time. Use this option only while you are fine-tuning your file, however. You can achieve maximum compression by using Phrase compression only if the Use Existing Phrase (.ph) file box is clear.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked, and you have compiled this file using Phrase compression before, Help Workshop uses the existing Phrase (.ph) file instead of generating a new one. This can significantly reduce compile time. Use this option only while you are fine-tuning your file, however. You can achieve maximum compression by using Phrase compression only if the Use Existing Phrase (.ph) file box is clear.
\par
```

## 621. 22KXD67

    ```rtf
    22KXD67
    ```

### Decoded Body


```rtf
\par
```

## 640. DN0TNZ

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `DN0TNZ`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    DN0TNZ
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Provides a place for you to type the name and location of an ASCII text file that contains a list of items. Depending on the circumstances, the file can include a list of topic (.rtf) files and their location, macros, window types, mapped topic IDs, or a set of topic IDs that are aliased to an alternate set of topic IDs. Including an ASCII text file provides an alternative to adding these items individually. If you do not know the name or location of the file you want to include, click Browse.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Provides a place for you to type the name and location of an ASCII text file that contains a list of items. Including an ASCII text file provides an alternative to adding these items individually. If you do not know the name or location of the file you want to include, click Browse.
\par
```

## 644. 0YGBEU

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `0YGBEU`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    0YGBEU
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Provides a place for you to type the path prefix that is currently specified in the project (.hpj) file. The path you type is probably no longer valid, or is a path that you do not want to use anymore. For example, if you have moved topic (.rtf) or bitmap (.bmp) files to a different folder or server, you can type the old path here and the new one in the Substitute This Path Prefix box. This prevents you from having to update each individual path in your project (.hpj) file.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Provides a place for you to type the path prefix that is currently specified in the project (.hpj) file. The path you type is probably no longer valid, or is a path that you do not want to use anymore. For example, if you have moved topic (.rtf) or bitmap (.bmp) files to a different folder or server, you can type the old path here and the new one in the Substitute This Path Prefix box. This prevents you from having to update each individual path in your project (.hpj) file.
\par
```

This prevents you from having to update each individual path in your project (.hpj) file.

## 647. 3BU7_OO

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `3BU7_OO`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    3BU7_OO
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked, Help Workshop generates a full-text search index for the Help file. If you create a full-text search (.fts) file for users, the Find tab in Help is populated the first time users click it. In other words, users will not have to use the Find Setup wizard to create a full-text search file, which can take quite a while for larger Help files.

The disadvantage to creating an full-text search file for users is that it significantly increases compile time and takes up space on the distribution disks for your product. If you are shipping your product on a CD-ROM, you can probably provide the convenience of an .FTS file at little cost to you. If you are shipping on floppy disks, the size of the full-text search file can affect the number of disks needed to ship your product.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked, Help Workshop generates a full-text search index for the Help file. If you create a full-text search (.fts) file for users, the Find tab in Help is populated the first time users click it. In other words, users will not have to use the Find Setup wizard to create a full-text search file, which can take quite a while for larger Help files.
\par The disadvantage to creating an full-text search file for users is that it significantly increases compile time and takes up space on the distribution disks for your product. If you are shipping your product on a CD-ROM, you can probably provide the convenience of an .FTS file at little cost to you. If you are shipping on floppy disks, the size of the full-text search file can affect the number of disks needed to ship your product.
\par
```

## 648. 110A4MR

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `110A4MR`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    110A4MR
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked, Help Workshop includes topics that have no titles in the full-text search index. In most cases, untitled topics are context-sensitive help or pop-ups within topics. Untitled topics might be of little use to users.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked, Help Workshop includes topics that have no titles in the full-text search index. In most cases, untitled topics are context-sensitive help or pop-ups within topics. Untitled topics might be of little use to users.
\par
```

## 649. 1KGS0Y7

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `1KGS0Y7`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    1KGS0Y7
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Enables users to search only for individual words. Although this keeps a full-text search (.fts) file's size to a minimum, it prevents users from searching for phrases, matching phrases, or similar topics.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Enables users to search only for individual words. Although this keeps a full-text search (.fts) file's size to a minimum, it prevents users from searching for phrases, matching phrases, or similar topics.
\par
```

## 650. 4_EVW7

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `4_EVW7`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    4_EVW7
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Enables users to search for complete phrases, such as a person's first and last name. Although this makes the full-text search index more usable, it increases the size of the full-text search (.fts) file.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Enables users to search for complete phrases, such as a person's first and last name. Although this makes the full-text search index more usable, it increases the size of the full-text search (.fts) file.
\par
```

## 651. 1.9I.7U

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `1.9I.7U`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    1.9I.7U
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Enables users to see matching phrases as they type them, which can make it easier for users to find a phrase. Although this makes the full-text search index more usable, it increases the size of the full-text search (.fts) file. If your Help file is large, or a user's computer is slow, specifying this option can significantly increase the amount of time it takes for Help to search the full-text search file.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Enables users to see matching phrases as they type them, which can make it easier for users to find a phrase. Although this makes the full-text search index more usable, it increases the size of the full-text search (.fts) file. If your Help file is large, or a user's computer is slow, specifying this option can significantly increase the amount of time it takes for Help to search the full-text search file.
\par
```

## 652. 5RG9_M

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `5RG9_M`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    5RG9_M
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Enables users to mark Help topics for later reference. Help can then search for information that is related to the information in the marked topics.

This feature is most useful for searching large or multiple Help files. Although it makes the full-text search index more usable, it increases the size of the full-text search (.fts) file.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Enables users to mark Help topics for later reference. Help can then search for information that is related to the information in the marked topics. 
\par This feature is most useful for searching large or multiple Help files. Although it makes the full-text search index more usable, it increases the size of the full-text search (.fts) file.
\par
```

## 662. 1NPLMCZ

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `1NPLMCZ`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    1NPLMCZ
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Lists the build tags of topics that you want to exclude when you compile your Help file. A build tag is an asterisk (*) footnote in your topic (.rtf) file that precedes all other footnotes, and it identifies a topic or topics that you want to include in or exclude from a build.

If a build tag footnote appears in this list and in the list of build tags to include, this list will take precedence and topics containing the footnote will be excluded from the Help file. Topics without build tag footnotes are automatically included in a Help file.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Lists the build tags of topics that you want to exclude when you compile your Help file. A build tag is an asterisk (*) footnote in your topic (.rtf) file that precedes all other footnotes, and it identifies a topic or topics that you want to include in or exclude from a build.
\par If a build tag footnote appears in this list and in the list of build tags to include, this list will take precedence and topics containing the footnote will be excluded from the Help file. Topics without build tag footnotes are automatically included in a Help file.
\par
```

### Decoded Body

```text
Displays the font for topic titles and keywords that appear on the Contents, Index, and Find tabs, and in the Topics Found dialog box. If no font is appears in this box, WinHelp determines which font is used. To change the font, click Change.

Changing the font is useful if your Help file will be shipped to international markets unlocalized. For example, WinHelp is localized to use larger fonts in Far East versions, and unlocalized Help files will use the larger fonts unless you specify your own.
```

```rtf

\par Changing the font is useful if your Help file will be shipped to international markets unlocalized. For example, WinHelp is localized to use larger fonts in Far East versions, and unlocalized Help files will use the larger fonts unless you specify your own.
```
```rtf
\par
```

## 694. 1N1_KKY

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `1N1_KKY`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    1N1_KKY
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked, the size of the specified window is based on a 1024 x 1024 screen resolution. WinHelp automatically adjusts the window's size and position so it retains its relative size, regardless of the resolution of the user's screen. Lines of text will wrap differently, depending on a user's screen resolution.

If this box is clear, the exact coordinates specified below are used, regardless of a user's screen resolution. In this case, a window that seems reasonably sized on a high-resolution monitor will appear much larger on a low-resolution monitor. Lines of text wrap in the same place, regardless of a user's screen resolution.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked, the size of the specified window is based on a 1024 x 1024 screen resolution. WinHelp automatically adjusts the window's size and position so it retains its relative size, regardless of the resolution of the user's screen. Lines of text will wrap differently, depending on a user's screen resolution.
\par If this box is clear, the exact coordinates specified below are used, regardless of a user's screen resolution. In this case, a window that seems reasonably sized on a high-resolution monitor will appear much larger on a low-resolution monitor. Lines of text wrap in the same place, regardless of a user's screen resolution. 
\par
```

## 695. YY38YN

  - Decoded: `YY38YN`

    ```rtf
    YY38YN
    ```


```rtf
\par
```

## 697. 41QHR9U

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `41QHR9U`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    41QHR9U
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If the Adjust For User's Screen Resolution box is checked, these values determine the size and position of the specified window based on a 1024 x 1024 screen resolution. WinHelp automatically adjusts the window's size and position so it retains its relative size, regardless of the resolution of the user's screen. Lines of text will wrap differently, depending on a user's screen resolution.

If the Adjust For User's Screen Resolution box is clear, the coordinates specified here are used regardless of a user's screen resolution. In this case, a window that occupies half the screen of a low-resolution monitor will occupy much less space on a high-resolution monitor. Lines of text will wrap in the same place, regardless of a user's screen resolution.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If the Adjust For User's Screen Resolution box is checked, these values determine the size and position of the specified window based on a 1024 x 1024 screen resolution. WinHelp automatically adjusts the window's size and position so it retains its relative size, regardless of the resolution of the user's screen. Lines of text will wrap differently, depending on a user's screen resolution.
\par If the Adjust For User's Screen Resolution box is clear, the coordinates specified here are used regardless of a user's screen resolution. In this case, a window that occupies half the screen of a low-resolution monitor will occupy much less space on a high-resolution monitor. Lines of text will wrap in the same place, regardless of a user's screen resolution.
\par
```

## 698. FXTWAH

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `FXTWAH`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    FXTWAH
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked, a Contents button appears on the button bar in the specified window. If a contents (.cnt) file is present, the Contents button displays the Contents tab in the Help Topics dialog box. If a contents file is not present, the Contents button displays either the default topic or the first topic in the first file that is listed in your project (.hpj) file. You can specify a default topic and the order of files in the Options dialog box.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked, a Contents button appears on the button bar in the specified window. If a contents (.cnt) file is present, the Contents button displays the Contents tab in the Help Topics dialog box. If a contents file is not present, the Contents button displays either the default topic or the first topic in the first file that is listed in your project (.hpj) file. You can specify a default topic and the order of files in the Options dialog box. 
\par
```

## 703. 34V4F4

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `34V4F4`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    34V4F4
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked, an Options button appears on the button bar in the specified window. The Options button displays a menu containing the following commands: Annotate, Copy, Print Topic, Font, Keep Help On Top, and Use System Colors. This menu also appears when users click a topic using their right mouse button.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked, an Options button appears on the button bar in the specified window. This menu also appears when users click a topic using their right mouse button.
\par
```

## 704. V1VVWV

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `V1VVWV`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    V1VVWV
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked, a Help Topics button appears on the button bar in the specified window. If a contents (.cnt) file is present, clicking the Help Topics button for the first time displays the Contents tab in the Help Topics dialog box. Thereafter, clicking the Help Topics button displays the tab that a user most recently worked with.

If there is no contents file, the Help Topics button displays either the default topic or the first topic in the first file that is listed in your project (.hpj) file. You can specify a default topic and the order of files in the Options dialog box.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked, a Help Topics button appears on the button bar in the specified window. If a contents (.cnt) file is present, clicking the Help Topics button for the first time displays the Contents tab in the Help Topics dialog box. Thereafter, clicking the Help Topics button displays the tab that a user most recently worked with. 
\par If there is no contents file, the Help Topics button displays either the default topic or the first topic in the first file that is listed in your project (.hpj) file. You can specify a default topic and the order of files in the Options dialog box. 
\par
```

## 705. D4V0.H

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `D4V0.H`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    D4V0.H
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked, a pair of browse buttons appears on the button bar in the specified window. The browse buttons enable users to move from one topic to another, as specified by your browse sequence. For these buttons to work property, you must specify a plus sign (+) footnote and browse sequence information in the topics that you want to include in the browse sequence.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked, a pair of browse buttons appears on the button bar in the specified window. The browse buttons enable users to move from one topic to another, as specified by your browse sequence. For these buttons to work property, you must specify a plus sign (+) footnote and browse sequence information in the topics that you want to include in the browse sequence.
\par
```

## 706. PJ9QYO

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `PJ9QYO`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    PJ9QYO
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Shows the background color for the nonscrolling region in the specified window. A nonscrolling region is an area that is always visible while users scroll through the information in a topic. This is especially useful for titles or other information that should always be available. To specify a nonscrolling region, apply the Keep With Next paragraph style (or its equivalent) to the paragraph(s) that you want to place in a nonscrolling region.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Shows the background color for the nonscrolling region in the specified window. A nonscrolling region is an area that is always visible while users scroll through the information in a topic. This is especially useful for titles or other information that should always be available. To specify a nonscrolling region, apply the Keep With Next paragraph style (or its equivalent) to the paragraph(s) that you want to place in a nonscrolling region.
\par
```

## 719. 4EMNHZ

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `4EMNHZ`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    4EMNHZ
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Lists the folders in which Help Workshop can find the bitmap (.bmp) files that you use in your topic (.rtf) files. Help Workshop searches for bitmap files in the folder that contains your project file, in the folder that contains the topic (.rtf) file that specified the bitmap, and in all the folders specified for your topic files. If a bitmap file is not in any of these folders, you must specify the folder here so Help Workshop can find it.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Lists the folders in which Help Workshop can find the bitmap (.bmp) files that you use in your topic (.rtf) files. Help Workshop searches for bitmap files in the folder that contains your project file, in the folder that contains the topic (.rtf) file that specified the bitmap, and in all the folders specified for your topic files. If a bitmap file is not in any of these folders, you must specify the folder here so Help Workshop can find it.
\par
```

## 734. 251SMH3

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `251SMH3`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    251SMH3
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Lists aliases, which are topic IDs that are replaced by alternate topic IDs. Topic IDs are specified by a number sign (#) footnote in your topic (.rtf) file.

Aliases enable you to reassign topic IDs to other topics, without having to edit your topic (.rtf) files. They are especially useful if you have made a change that causes a mapped numeric value to refer to a topic other than the one you originally intended. An alias enables you to display the intended topic without having to change the code in the program.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Lists aliases, which are topic IDs that are replaced by alternate topic IDs. Topic IDs are specified by a number sign (#) footnote in your topic (.rtf) file. 
\par Aliases enable you to reassign topic IDs to other topics, without having to edit your topic (.rtf) files. They are especially useful if you have made a change that causes a mapped numeric value to refer to a topic other than the one you originally intended. An alias enables you to display the intended topic without having to change the code in the program. 
\par
```

## 806. 3.8M0ZU

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `3.8M0ZU`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    3.8M0ZU
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text

```

```rtf

\par
```

## 808. CFRV_U

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `CFRV_U`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    CFRV_U
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Specifies the Help file for which you want to create a report. To specify a file, type its path and name, or click Browse. Reports are especially useful if you want to extract information from a Help file that has already been compiled. For example, you can determine what the A- and K-keywords in a compiled Help file are, and then jump to them from your Help file.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Specifies the Help file for which you want to create a report. To specify a file, type its path and name, or click Browse. For example, you can determine what the A- and K-keywords in a compiled Help file are, and then jump to them from your Help file. 
\par
```

## 813. ZV9UBO

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `ZV9UBO`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    ZV9UBO
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Lists the title and hash number for every topic in the specified Help file. A hash number is a unique number that Help Workshop assigns to topics based on their topic IDs. It is this number, and not the topic ID itself, that WinHelp uses. If a topic doesn't contain an A-footnote or its K-footnotes are not specific enough, you can jump to the topic by using the JumpHash macro and its hash number.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Lists the title and hash number for every topic in the specified Help file. A hash number is a unique number that Help Workshop assigns to topics based on their topic IDs. It is this number, and not the topic ID itself, that WinHelp uses. If a topic doesn't contain an A-footnote or its K-footnotes are not specific enough, you can jump to the topic by using the JumpHash macro and its hash number.
\par
```

## 841. 11EDRE4

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `11EDRE4`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    11EDRE4
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
Procedure: This window type is normally used for displaying procedures. It is auto-sizing, contains three buttons on the button bar, and is positioned in the upper right corner of the screen.

Reference: This window type is normally used for displaying reference material. It is auto-sizing, contains three buttons on the button bar, and is positioned on the left side of the screen and takes up approximately two thirds of the width of the screen.

Error message: This window type is normally used for displaying error messages. It is auto-sizing, contains no buttons, and lets WinHelp determine the position (upper right corner of the screen unless the user changes the position).
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 Procedure: This window type is normally used for displaying procedures. It is auto-sizing, contains three buttons on the button bar, and is positioned in the upper right corner of the screen.
\par Reference: This window type is normally used for displaying reference material. It is auto-sizing, contains three buttons on the button bar, and is positioned on the left side of the screen and takes up approximately two thirds of the width of the screen.
\par Error message: This window type is normally used for displaying error messages. It is auto-sizing, contains no buttons, and lets WinHelp determine the position (upper right corner of the screen unless the user changes the position).
\par
```

## 843. 328BXCT

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `328BXCT`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    328BXCT
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
If this box is checked and Help Author is enabled, two new fields appear in the Topic Information dialog box, which appears when you use your right mouse button to click a topic, and then click Topic Information. The additional fields show which topic (.rtf) file the topic is in and what the topic ID is. This feature is especially useful for testing, debugging, and editing topics —  but it does increase the size of your Help file. Therefore, make sure this box is clear when you compile your file for the last time.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li85\ri125 If this box is checked and Help Author is enabled, two new fields appear in the Topic Information dialog box, which appears when you use your right mouse button to click a topic, and then click Topic Information. The additional fields show which topic (.rtf) file the topic is in and what the topic ID is. This feature is especially useful for testing, debugging, and editing topics \plain\f0\fs16 \'97\f1   but it does increase the size of your Help file. Therefore, make sure this box is clear when you compile your file for the last time.
\par
```

## 849. T.A_S1

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `T.A_S1`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    T.A_S1
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
{bmlt bm15.bmp}	Tips of the Day

Tips of the Day introduce new Help Workshop features that make it easier to create, compile, and test your Help files. Each tip provides information that will help you get the most out of Help Workshop. To turn Tips of the Day off or on, click Help, and then click Tip Of The Day. To print a Tip of the Day, use your right mouse button to click it, and then click Print Topic.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb75\sl-235\ri125\tx535 \b \{bmlt bm15.bmp\}\tab Tips of the Day
\par \pard\sl-235\li535\ri355\tx535 \plain\f1\fs16 Tips of the Day introduce new Help Workshop features that make it easier to create, compile, and test your Help files. Each tip provides information that will help you get the most out of Help Workshop. To turn Tips of the Day off or on, click Help, and then click Tip Of The Day. To print a Tip of the Day, use your right mouse button to click it, and then click Print Topic.
\par \pard\sl-235\li535\ri125\tx535 
\par
```

## 850. ZI0CV.

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `ZI0CV.`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    ZI0CV.
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
{bmlt bm15.bmp}	Tip: Including and excluding topics

You can exclude specific topics when you compile a Help file. This is useful if you want to create different versions of a help file based on specific criteria. For example, you can exclude incomplete topics or topics that are only relevant under specific circumstances. For more information, look up “Build tags” in Help for Help Workshop.
```

```rtf

For more information, look up \plain\f0\fs16 \'93\f1 Build tags\plain\f0\fs16 \'94\f1  in Help for Help Workshop.
\par \pard\sl-235\li535\ri125\tx535 
\par
```

## 852. 3.WHVFP

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `3.WHVFP`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    3.WHVFP
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
{bmlt bm15.bmp}	Tip: Viewing WinHelp messages

Help Workshop provides information about almost every command a program sends to WinHelp, every macro that WinHelp runs, the topic number and title of every topic that appears, the command for every hotspot you click, and more. To view this information, click the View menu, and then click WinHelp Messages.
```

```rtf
\par \pard\sl-235\li535\ri125\tx535
```

## 853. 179QAQJ

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `179QAQJ`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    179QAQJ
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
{bmlt bm15.bmp}	Tip: Smarter compiling

You can compile a project file without opening it. To do this, click the File menu, and then click Compile. In the dialog box, you can choose settings that make compiling faster and testing easier. For example, you can temporarily turn off compression so files compile faster or include each topic's topic ID and the name of the .rtf file the topic is in. For help on an item in the Compile A Help File dialog box, click {bmc bm0.bmp} in its title bar, and then click the item.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sl-235\ri125\tx535 \b \{bmlt bm15.bmp\}\tab Tip: Smarter compiling
\par \pard\sl-235\li535\ri125\tx535 \plain\f1\fs16 You can compile a project file without opening it. To do this, click the File menu, and then click Compile. In the dialog box, you can choose settings that make compiling faster and testing easier. For example, you can temporarily turn off compression so files compile faster or include each topic's topic ID and the name of the .rtf file the topic is in. For help on an item in the Compile A Help File dialog box, click \{bmc bm0.bmp\} in its title bar, and then click the item.
\par \pard\sl-235\li535\ri125\tx535 
\par
```

## 854. CVTDVX

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `CVTDVX`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    CVTDVX
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
{bmlt bm15.bmp}	Tip: Specifying bitmaps for different display types

You can combine bitmaps with different color depths by specifying all the bitmaps in your topic (.rtf) file, separated by semicolons. WinHelp will choose the bitmap whose color depth most closely matches a user's display. For example:

{-bmc mono.bmp; 16.bmp; 256.bmp}

For more information, see Help in Help Workshop.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sl-235\ri125\tx535 \b \{bmlt bm15.bmp\}\tab Tip: Specifying bitmaps for different display types
\par \pard\sl-235\li535\ri125\tx535 \plain\f1\fs16 You can combine bitmaps with different color depths by specifying all the bitmaps in your topic (.rtf) file, separated by semicolons. WinHelp will choose the bitmap whose color depth most closely matches a user's display. For example:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li545\ri125\tx535 \b \{\-bmc\plain\f1\fs16  \b mono.bmp; 16.bmp; 256.bmp\'7d
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li545\ri125\tx535 \plain\f1\fs16 For more information, see Help in Help Workshop.
\par \pard\sl-235\li545\ri125\tx535 
\par
```

## 855. 1FX.JL3

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `1FX.JL3`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    1FX.JL3
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
{bmlt bm15.bmp}	Tip: Testing the jumps in your contents file

You can determine whether the jumps in your contents (.cnt) file are correct by clicking the Test menu, and then clicking Contents File. This command tests the syntax of the specified contents file, and then tests each jump. If an error occurs, a message that contains information about the incorrect jump appears. For more information, see Help in Help Workshop.
```

## 856. YGRC.F

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `YGRC.F`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    YGRC.F
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
{bmlt bm15.bmp}	Tip: Displaying general topic information

You can display information about each Help topic, in addition to more detailed error messages, by clicking the File menu and making sure Help Author is checked. This causes a topic number to appear in the title bar of each Help topic. It also provides two additional commands on the context menu that can help you test your files. For more information, see Help in Help Workshop.
```

```rtf

This causes a topic number to appear in the title bar of each Help topic. It also provides two additional commands on the context menu that can help you test your files. For more information, see Help in Help Workshop.
\par \pard\sl-235\li535\ri125\tx535 
\par
```
