    ```rtf
    HELPWININFO
    ```
- Marker `>`
  - Decoded: `main`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    main
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HELPWININFO structure;reference;reference, WinHelp APIs;windows,;windows, structure containing size and position`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HELPWININFO structure;reference;reference, WinHelp APIs;windows,;windows, structure containing size and position
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
HELPWININFO

typedef struct {-    	// hwi

    int   wStructSize;

    int   x;

    int   y;

    int   dx;

    int   dy;

    int   wMax;

    TCHAR rgchMember[2];

} HELPWININFO;

The HELPWININFO structure contains the size and position of either a primary or a secondary Help window.

Parameter	Description

wStructSize	Size of this structure, in bytes.

x	X-coordinate of the upper left corner of the window, in screen coordinates.

y	Y-coordinate of the upper left corner of the window, in screen coordinates.

dx	Width of the window, in pixels.

dy	Height of the window, in pixels.

wMax	Value specifying how to show the window. This member must be one of these values:

Value	Meaning

SW_HIDE	Hides the window and passes activation to another window.

SW_MINIMIZE	Minimizes the specified window and activates the top-level window in the Z order.

SW_RESTORE	Same as SW_SHOWNORMAL.

SW_SHOW	Activates a window and displays it in its current size and position.

SW_SHOWMAXIMIZED	Activates the window and displays it as a maximized window.

SW_SHOWMINIMIZED	Activates the window and displays it as an icon.

SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE	Displays the window as an icon. The window that is currently active remains active.

SW_SHOWNA	Displays the window in its current state. The window that is currently active remains active.

SW_SHOWNOACTIVATE	Displays a window in its most recent size and position. The window that is currently active remains active.

SW_SHOWNORMAL	Activates and displays the window. Whether the window is minimized or maximized, Windows restores it to its original size and position.

rgchMember	Name of the window.

Comments

WinHelp divides the display into 1024 units in both the x- and y-directions. To create a secondary window that fills the upper left quadrant of the display, for example, a program would specify zero for the x and y members and 512 for the dx and dy members.

A program can set the size and position information by calling the WinHelp function with the HELP_SETWINPOS value.

{button ,AL(`winhelp_api')} Related Topics%!AL(`winhelp_api')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 
\b\fs24 HELPWININFO
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-195\li115\ri125\tx355 \plain\f1\fs16 typedef struct \{\-    \tab // hwi \line
    int   wStructSize;\line
    int   x;\line
    int   y;\line
    int   dx;\line
    int   dy;\line
    int   wMax;\line
    TCHAR rgchMember[2];\line
\'7d HELPWININFO;
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx355 The \b HELPWININFO\plain\f1\fs16  structure contains the size and position of either a primary or a secondary Help window.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\brdrb\brdrs\tx1435 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\tx1435 wStructSize\tab Size of this structure, in bytes.
\par x\tab X-coordinate of the upper left corner of the window, in screen coordinates.
\par y\tab Y-coordinate of the upper left corner of the window, in screen coordinates.
\par dx\tab Width of the window, in pixels.
\par dy\tab Height of the window, in pixels.
\par wMax\tab Value specifying how to show the window. This member must be one of these values:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li3595\ri125\fi-2125\brdrb\brdrs\tx3595 \b Value\tab Meaning\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li3595\ri125\fi-2125\tx3595 SW_HIDE\tab Hides the window and passes activation to another window.
\par SW_MINIMIZE\tab Minimizes the specified window and activates the top-level window in the Z order.
\par SW_RESTORE\tab Same as SW_SHOWNORMAL.
\par SW_SHOW\tab Activates a window and displays it in its current size and position.
\par SW_SHOWMAXIMIZED\tab Activates the window and displays it as a maximized window.
\par SW_SHOWMINIMIZED\tab Activates the window and displays it as an icon.
\par SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE\tab Displays the window as an icon. The window that is currently active remains active.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li3595\ri125\fi-2125\tx3595 SW_SHOWNA\tab Displays the window in its current state. The window that is currently active remains active.
\par SW_SHOWNOACTIVATE\tab Displays a window in its most recent size and position. The window that is currently active remains active.
\par SW_SHOWNORMAL\tab Activates and displays the window. Whether the window is minimized or maximized, Windows restores it to its original size and position.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\tx1435 rgchMember\tab Name of the window.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 WinHelp divides the display into 1024 units in both the x- and y-directions. To create a secondary window that fills the upper left quadrant of the display, for example, a program would specify zero for the \b x\plain\f1\fs16  and \b y\plain\f1\fs16  members and 512 for the \b dx\plain\f1\fs16  and \b dy\plain\f1\fs16  members.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 A program can set the size and position information by calling the \b WinHelp\plain\f1\fs16  function with the HELP_SETWINPOS value.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`winhelp_api')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`winhelp_api')}
\par
```

### Decoded Body

```text
MULTIKEYHELP

typedef struct tagMULTIKEYHELP {- 	// mkh

    DWORD mkSize;

    BYTE  mkKeylist;

    TCHAR szKeyphrase[1];

} MULTIKEYHELP;

The MULTIKEYHELP structure specifies a keyword table and an associated keyword to be used by the WinHelp program.

Parameter	Description

mkSize	Size of this structure, in bytes.

mkKeylist	A single character that identifies the keyword table to search.

szKeyphrase	A null-terminated text string that specifies the keyword to locate in the keyword table.

{button ,AL(`winhelp_api')} Related Topics%!AL(`winhelp_api')
```

```rtf
    TCHAR szKeyphrase[1];\line
\'7d MULTIKEYHELP;
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx355 The \b MULTIKEYHELP\plain\f1\fs16  structure specifies a keyword table and an associated keyword to be used by the WinHelp program.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\brdrb\brdrs\tx1435 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16
```


### Decoded Body

```text
WinHelp

BOOL WinHelp(HWND hwnd, LPCTSTR lpszHelpFile, UINT fuCommand, DWORD dwData)

The WinHelp function starts Windows Help (WINHELP.EXE) and passes additional data indicating the nature of the help requested by the program.

Parameter	Description

hwnd	Handle of the window requesting Help. The WinHelp function uses this handle to keep track of which programs have requested Help. If fuCommand specifies HELP_CONTEXTMENU or HELP_WM_HELP, hwnd identifies the control requesting Help.

lpszHelpFile	Address of a null-terminated string containing the path, if necessary, and the name of the Help file that WinHelp is to display.

	The filename may be followed by an angle bracket (>) and the name of a secondary window if the topic is to be displayed in a secondary window rather than in the primary window. The name of the secondary window must have been defined previously in the [WINDOWS] section of the Help Project (.HPJ) file.

fuCommand	Type of help requested. For a list of possible values and how they affect the value to place in the dwData parameter, see the Comments section.

dwData	Additional data. The value used depends on the value of the fuCommand parameter. For a list of possible values, see the Comments section.

Return Value

If successful, the return value is TRUE; otherwise, it is FALSE.

Comments

The program specifies the name and, where required, the directory path of the Help file to display.

Before closing the window that requested Help, the program must call WinHelp with the fuCommand parameter set to HELP_QUIT. Until all programs have done this, WinHelp will not terminate. Note that calling WinHelp with the HELP_QUIT command is not necessary if you used the HELP_CONTEXTPOPUP command to start Help.

The following table shows the possible values for the fuCommand parameter and the corresponding formats of the dwData parameter:

fuCommandActiondwData
HELP_COMMANDRuns a Help macro or macro string. Address of a string that specifies the name of the Help macro(s) to run. If the string specifies multiple macro names, the names must be separated by colons or semicolons . You must use the short form of the macro name for some macros because WinHelp does not support the long name.
HELP_CONTENTSDisplays the topic specified by the Contents option in the [OPTIONS] section of the .HPJ file. This command is for backward compatibility. New programs should provide a .CNT file and use the HELP_FINDER command.Ignored; set to 0.
HELP_CONTEXTDisplays the topic identified by the specified context identifier defined in the [MAP] section of the .HPJ file. Unsigned long integer containing the context identifier for the topic.
HELP_CONTEXTMENUDisplays the Help menu for the selected window, and then displays (in a pop-up window) the topic for the selected control.Address of an array of double word pairs. The first double word in each pair is a control identifier, and the second is a context number for a topic.
HELP_CONTEXTPOPUPDisplays, in a pop-up window, the topic identified by the specified context identifier defined in the [MAP] section of the .HPJ file.Unsigned long integer containing the context identifier for a topic.
HELP_FINDERDisplays the Help Topics dialog box.Ignored; set to 0.
HELP_FORCEFILEEnsures that WinHelp is displaying the correct Help file. If the incorrect Help file is being displayed, WinHelp opens the correct one; otherwise, there is no action. Ignored; set to 0.
HELP_HELPONHELPDisplays Help on how to use WinHelp, if the WINHLP32.HLP file is available.Ignored; set to 0.
HELP_INDEXDisplays the topic specified by the CONTENTS option in the [OPTIONS] section of the .HPJ file. This command is for backward compatibility. New programs should provide a .CNT file and use the HELP_FINDER command.Ignored; set to 0.
HELP_KEYDisplays the topic in the keyword table that matches the specified keyword, if there is an exact match. If there is more than one match, this command displays the Topics Found list box.Address of a keyword string. Multiple keywords must be separated by semi-colons.
HELP_MULTIKEYDisplays the topic specified by a keyword in an alternative keyword table.Address of a MULTIKEYHELP structure that specifies a table footnote character and a keyword.
HELP_PARTIALKEYDisplays the topic in the keyword table that matches the specified keyword if there is an exact match. If there is more than one match, this command displays the Topics Found dialog box. To display the Index without passing a keyword, you should use a pointer to an empty string.Address of a keyword  string. Multiple keywords must be separated by semi-colons.
HELP_QUITInforms the WinHelp program that it is no longer needed. If no other programs have asked for Help, Windows closes the WinHelp program.Ignored; set to 0.
HELP_SETCONTENTSSpecifies the Contents topic. The WinHelp program displays this topic when the user clicks the Contents button if the Help file does not have an associated .CNT file.Unsigned long integer containing the context identifier for the Contents topic.
HELP_SETPOPUP_POSSets the position of the subsequent pop-up window.Address of a POINTS structure. The pop-up window is positioned as if the mouse cursor were at the specified point when the pop-up window is invoked.
HELP_SETWINPOSDisplays the Help window, if it is minimized or in memory, and sets its size and position as specified.Address of a HELPWININFO structure that specifies the size and position of either a primary or secondary Help window.
HELP_TCARDIndicates that a command is for a training card instance of the WinHelp program. A program combines the HELP_TCARD flag with other commands by using the bitwise OR operator.Depends on the command with which the flag is combined.
HELP_WM_HELPDisplays, in a pop-up window, the topic for the control identified by hwnd.Address of an array of double word pairs. The first double word in each pair is a control identifier, and the second is a context identifier for a topic.
Comments

The following values may also be used, however, since they are not in windows.h, you must add them to your own header file:

#define HELP_FORCE_GID	0x000e

#define HELP_TAB				0x000f

fuCommandActiondwData
HELP_FORCE_GIDChanges to the .GID file associated with the help file passed in as the lpszHelpFile parameter.Ignored; set to 0.
HELP_TABOpens the Help Topics dialog box, displaying the specified extensible tab.Zero-based index of the extensible tab to display (0 is the first tab, 1 the second, etc.).

{button ,KL("HELPWININFO structure;MULTIKEYHELP structure;POINTS;training card Help;WM_HELP message;WM_TCARD message;MAP section")} Related Topics%!KL("HELPWININFO structure;MULTIKEYHELP structure;POINTS;training card Help;WM_HELP message;WM_TCARD message;MAP section")
```

```rtf
\par \tab The filename may be followed by an angle bracket (>) and the name of a secondary window if the topic is to be displayed in a secondary window rather than in the primary window. The name of the secondary window must have been defined previously in the \b [WINDOWS]\plain\f1\fs16  section of the Help Project (.HPJ) file.

\par \trowd\trgaph-5\trleft-6 \cellx2065\cellx4765\cellx7735\pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \b fuCommand\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Action\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 dwData\cell\intbl\row

The pop-up window is positioned as if the mouse cursor were at the specified point when the pop-up window is invoked.\cell\intbl\row
\trowd\trgaph-5\trleft-6 \cellx2065\cellx4765\cellx7735\pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 HELP_SETWINPOS\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Displays the Help window, if it is minimized or in memory, and sets its size and position as specified.\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Address of a \b HELPWININFO\plain\f1\fs16  structure that specifies the size and position of either a primary or secondary Help window. \cell\intbl\row
\trowd\trgaph-5\trleft-6 \cellx2065\cellx4765\cellx7735\pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 HELP_TCARD\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Indicates that a command is for a training card instance of the WinHelp program. A program combines the HELP_TCARD flag with other commands by using the bitwise OR operator.\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Depends on the command with which the flag is combined.\cell\intbl\row
\trowd\trgaph-5\trleft-6 \cellx2065\cellx4765\cellx7735\pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 HELP_WM_HELP\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Displays, in a pop-up window, the topic for the control identified by hwnd.\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Address of an array of double word pairs. The first double word in each pair is a control identifier, and the second is a context identifier for a topic.\cell\intbl\row
\pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx355 \b Comments\tab 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx355 \plain\f1\fs16 The following values may also be used, however, since they are not in windows.h, you must add them to your own header file:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx355 \f4 #define HELP_FORCE_GID\tab 0x000e\f1 
\par \f4 #define HELP_TAB\tab \tab \tab \tab 0x000f
\par \trowd\trgaph-5\trleft-6 \cellx2065\cellx4765\cellx7735\pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \f1\b fuCommand\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Action\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 dwData\cell\intbl\row
\trowd\trgaph-5\trleft-6 \cellx2065\cellx4765\cellx7735\pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 HELP_FORCE_GID\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Changes to the .GID file associated with the help file passed in as the lpszHelpFile parameter.\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Ignored; set to 0.\cell\intbl\row
\trowd\trgaph-5\trleft-6 \cellx2065\cellx4765\cellx7735\pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 HELP_TAB\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Opens the Help Topics dialog box, displaying the specified extensible tab.\cell\pard \pard\intbl\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 Zero-based index of the extensible tab to display (0 is the first tab, 1 the second, etc.).\cell\intbl\row
\pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,KL("HELPWININFO structure;MULTIKEYHELP structure;POINTS;training card Help;WM_HELP message;WM_TCARD message;MAP section")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!KL("HELPWININFO structure;MULTIKEYHELP structure;POINTS;training card Help;WM_HELP message;WM_TCARD message;MAP section")}
\par
```

## 989. WM_HELP message

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `$`

    ```rtf
    WM_HELP message
    ```

- Marker `#`

- Marker `>`

  - Raw:

    ```rtf
    main
    ```
- Marker `A`
  - Decoded: `CS_HLP`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    CS_HLP
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `F1 key;F1 key, WM_HELP message;messages;messages, sent when user presses F1;reference;reference, WinHelp APIs;windows,;windows, F1 message sent to active window;WM_HELP message`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    F1 key;F1 key, WM_HELP message;messages;messages, sent when user presses F1;reference;reference, WinHelp APIs;windows,;windows, F1 message sent to active window;WM_HELP message
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
WM_HELP

WM_HELP

lphi = (LPHELPINFO) lParam;

The WM_HELP message is sent whenever the user presses the f1 key.

Parameter	Description

lphi	Address of a HELPINFO structure that contains information about the menu item, control, dialog box, or window for which Help is requested.

Default Action

The DefWindowProc function passes WM_HELP to the parent window of a child window, or to the owner of a top-level window.

Comments

If a menu is active when F1 is pressed, WM_HELP is sent to the window associated with the menu. Otherwise, WM_HELP is sent to the window that has the keyboard focus. If no window has the keyboard focus, WM_HELP is sent to the currently active window.

{button ,KL("HELPINFO structure;context-sensitive Help")} Related Topics%!KL("HELPINFO structure;context-sensitive Help")
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 WM_HELP
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 WM_HELP\line
lphi = (LPHELPINFO) lParam;
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 The WM_HELP message is sent whenever the user presses the \scaps\fs17 f1\plain\f1\fs16  key.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\brdrb\brdrs\tx1435 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\tx1435 lphi\tab Address of a \b HELPINFO\plain\f1\fs16  structure that contains information about the menu item, control, dialog box, or window for which Help is requested.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Default Action
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 The \b DefWindowProc\plain\f1\fs16  function passes WM_HELP to the parent window of a child window, or to the owner of a top-level window.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 If a menu is active when \fs17 F1\fs16  is pressed, WM_HELP is sent to the window associated with the menu. Otherwise, WM_HELP is sent to the window that has the keyboard focus. If no window has the keyboard focus, WM_HELP is sent to the currently active window.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,KL("HELPINFO structure;context-sensitive Help")\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!KL("HELPINFO structure;context-sensitive Help")}
\par
```

## 990. WM_TCARD message

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `+`
  - Decoded: `BROWSE0027:00e9`

- Marker `$`

- Marker `#`

  - Raw:

    ```rtf
    WM_TCARD
    ```
- Marker `>`
  - Decoded: `main`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    main
    ```
- Marker `A`
  - Decoded: `WM_TCARD`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    WM_TCARD
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `HELP_TCARD_DATA command;HELP_TCARD_NEXT command;HELP_TCARD_OTHER_CALLER command;messages;messages, initiating training card Help;reference;reference, WinHelp APIs;training card Help;training card Help, message sent to initiating program;WM_TCARD message`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    HELP_TCARD_DATA command;HELP_TCARD_NEXT command;HELP_TCARD_OTHER_CALLER command;messages;messages, initiating training card Help;reference;reference, WinHelp APIs;training card Help;training card Help, message sent to initiating program;WM_TCARD message
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
WM_TCARD

WM_TCARD

idAction = wParam;      	// user action

dwActionData = lParam;  	// action data

The WM_TCARD message is sent to a program that has initiated a training card with the WinHelp program.

Parameter	Description

idAction	Value of wParam. Indicates the action that the user has taken. This parameter can be one of the following values:

Value	Meaning

IDABORT	The user clicked an authorable Abort button.

IDCANCEL	The user clicked an authorable Cancel button.

IDCLOSE	The user closed the training card.

IDHELP	The user clicked an authorable Help button.

IDIGNORE	The user clicked an authorable Ignore button.

IDOK	The user clicked an authorable OK button.

IDNO	The user clicked an authorable No button.

IDRETRY	The user clicked an authorable Retry button.

IDYES	The user clicked an authorable Yes button.

HELP_TCARD_DATA	The user clicked an authorable button. The lParam parameter contains a long integer specified by the Help author.

HELP_TCARD_NEXT	The user clicked an authorable Next button.

HELP_TCARD_OTHER_CALLER	Another program has requested training cards.

dwActionData	Value of lParam. If idAction specifies HELP_TCARD_DATA, this parameter is a long integer specified by the Help author. Otherwise, this parameter is zero.

Return Value

The return value is ignored; use zero.

Comments

The message informs the program when the user clicks an authorable button or hotspot that runs the TCard macro. A program initiates a training card by specifying the HELP_TCARD command in a call to the WinHelp function.

WinHelp can run only one training card at a time. If another program requests a training card, the program currently using training cards receives a WM_TCARD message with wParam == HELP_TCARD_OTHER_CALLER. The program receiving this message should either terminate the training card (HELP_QUIT | HELP_TCARD), or the next time it gets the focus, it should call WinHelp with a HELP_FORCEFILE | HELP_TCARD command.

{button ,AL(`winhelp_api;TCard')} Related Topics%!AL(`winhelp_api;TCard')
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\keepn\sb115\sa115\sl-235\li115\ri125 

\b\fs24 WM_TCARD
\par \pard\keepn\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx355 \plain\f1\fs16 WM_TCARD\line
idAction = wParam;      \tab // user action\line
dwActionData = lParam;  \tab // action data
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx355 The WM_TCARD message is sent to a program that has initiated a training card with the WinHelp program.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\brdrb\brdrs\tx1435 \b Parameter\tab Description\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\tx1435 idAction\tab Value of wParam. Indicates the action that the user has taken. This parameter can be one of the following values:
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li3955\ri125\fi-2515\brdrb\brdrs\tx3955 \b Value\tab Meaning\plain\f1\fs16 
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li3955\ri125\fi-2515\tx3955 IDABORT\tab The user clicked an authorable Abort button.
\par IDCANCEL\tab The user clicked an authorable Cancel button.
\par IDCLOSE\b \tab \plain\f1\fs16 The user closed the training card.
\par IDHELP\tab The user clicked an authorable Help button.
\par IDIGNORE\tab The user clicked an authorable Ignore button.
\par IDOK\tab The user clicked an authorable OK button.
\par IDNO\tab The user clicked an authorable No button.
\par IDRETRY\tab The user clicked an authorable Retry button.
\par IDYES\tab The user clicked an authorable Yes button.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li3955\ri125\fi-2515\tx3955 HELP_TCARD_DATA\tab The user clicked an authorable button. The lParam parameter contains a long integer specified by the Help author.
\par HELP_TCARD_NEXT\tab The user clicked an authorable Next button.
\par HELP_TCARD_OTHER_CALLER\tab Another program has requested training cards.
\par \pard\sb55\sl-235\li1435\ri125\fi-1305\tx1435 dwActionData\tab Value of lParam. If idAction specifies HELP_TCARD_DATA, this parameter is a long integer specified by the Help author. Otherwise, this parameter is zero.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Return Value
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 The return value is ignored; use zero.
\par \pard\sb115\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \b Comments
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 \plain\f1\fs16 The message informs the program when the user clicks an authorable button or hotspot that runs the \b TCard\plain\f1\fs16  macro. A program initiates a training card by specifying the HELP_TCARD command in a call to the \b WinHelp\plain\f1\fs16  function.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx1435 WinHelp can run only one training card at a time. If another program requests a training card, the program currently using training cards receives a WM_TCARD message with wParam == HELP_TCARD_OTHER_CALLER. The program receiving this message should either terminate the training card (HELP_QUIT | HELP_TCARD), or the next time it gets the focus, it should call WinHelp with a HELP_FORCEFILE | HELP_TCARD command.
\par \pard\sl-115\li265\ri125\fi-185\brdrb\brdrs\tx295 
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 \{button ,AL(`winhelp_api;TCard')\} \uldb Related Topics\plain\f1\fs16 {\v %!AL(`winhelp_api;TCard')}
```

## 991. WinHelp error numbers

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `$`
  - Decoded: `WinHelp error numbers`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    WinHelp error numbers
    ```
- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `WinHelp_errors`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    WinHelp_errors
    ```
- Marker `>`
  - Decoded: `proc4`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    proc4
    ```
- Marker `K`
  - Decoded: `error numbers, WinHelp;WinHelp error numbers`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    error numbers, WinHelp;WinHelp error numbers
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
WinHelp error numbers

When Help Author mode is turned on, WinHelp will usually give the author enough information to solve the problem in the help file. However, if a user encounters a problem in your help file, only the error number will be available. The following table lists the error numbers and the probably cause of the error. Errors that only occur when Help Author mode is on are not included in this list.

1024	A macro was specified that doesn’t exist.

1026	A macro parameter is incorrect (for example, using a number when a quoted string was expected).

1027	Missing close quote.

1028	Unrecognized macro or variable name.

1029	Parameter prototype is invalid (RegisterRoutine problem).

1031	Missing closing parenthesis in a macro.

1032	Invalid separator between macros.

1033	Invalid return type specified (RegisterRoutine problem)

1034	Invalid macro syntax.

1035	Parameter doesn’t match the expected type (for example, using a negative number for a paramter that expects an unsigned number).

1036	A variable used in a macro is unrecognized.

1037	The current macro cannot be executed until a previously called macro completes. For example, until the FileOpen macro completes, other macros cannot execute.

1049	A macro called itself.

1050	Missing separator in an authorable button command.

1053	Invalid multi-key keyword.

1061	Too many secondary windows open at once.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb115\sa35\sl-235\li115\ri125 
\b WinHelp error numbers
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125 \plain\f1\fs16 When Help Author mode is turned on, WinHelp will usually give the author enough information to solve the problem in the help file. However, if a user encounters a problem in your help file, only the error number will be available. The following table lists the error numbers and the probably cause of the error. Errors that only occur when Help Author mode is on are not included in this list.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li705\ri125\fi-575\tx355 
\par 1024\tab A macro was specified that doesn\'92t exist.
\par 1026\tab A macro parameter is incorrect (for example, using a number when a quoted string was expected).
\par 1027\tab Missing close quote.
\par 1028\tab Unrecognized macro or variable name.
\par 1029\tab Parameter prototype is invalid (RegisterRoutine problem).
\par 1031\tab Missing closing parenthesis in a macro.
\par 1032\tab Invalid separator between macros.
\par 1033\tab Invalid return type specified (RegisterRoutine problem)
\par 1034\tab Invalid macro syntax.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li705\ri125\fi-575\tx355 1035\tab Parameter doesn\'92t match the expected type (for example, using a negative number for a paramter that expects an unsigned number).
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li705\ri125\fi-575\tx355 1036\tab A variable used in a macro is unrecognized.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li705\ri125\fi-575\tx355 1037\tab The current macro cannot be executed until a previously called macro completes. For example, until the FileOpen macro completes, other macros cannot execute.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li705\ri125\fi-575\tx355 1049\tab A macro called itself.
\par 1050\tab Missing separator in an authorable button command.
\par 1053\tab Invalid multi-key keyword.
\par 1061\tab Too many secondary windows open at once.
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li115\ri125\tx355 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 
\par
```


## 1019. 9FS831

### WinHelp Footnotes

- Marker `#`
  - Decoded: `9FS831`
  - Raw:
    ```rtf
    9FS831
    ```

### Decoded Body

```text
1	In the Title Bar Text box, type text that you want to appear in the title bar for this window.

2	If you want to use the auto-sizing feature, click Auto-Size Height.

3	If you want your Help window to stay visible in front of other window, click Keep Help Window On Top.

4	When you are done, click OK.
```

### Raw RTF Body

```rtf
\pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 
\par \pard\sb75\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 1\tab In the Title Bar Text box, type text that you want to appear in the title bar for this window.
\par \pard\sb45\sl-235\li295\ri125\fi-185\tx295 2\tab If you want to use the auto-sizing feature, click Auto-Size Height.
\par 3\tab If you want your Help window to stay visible in front of other window, click Keep Help Window On Top.
\par 4\tab When you are done, click OK.
\par
```
